title: Product Manager
jd_text:
Product Manager
REMOTE (EU Based)
Full-time
Who we are
At Jaipur Robotics, we’re building AI-powered solutions that transform the waste industry — turning complex data into automation, efficiency, and environmental impact. As a fast-growing, VC-backed clean-tech startup based in Switzerland, we work with leading waste operators across Europe and are now expanding our product and customer-facing teams.
What we offer
We’re looking for a
Product Manager
who can combine product ownership, data analysis, and close customer engagement.
This is not a traditional desk-based product role. You will spend significant time working directly with customers, visiting industrial plants, understanding how our products are used in real operating environments, and identifying where they can create greater value.
You will act as the bridge between customers, engineering, AI, operations, and commercial teams. You will critically evaluate customer requests, distinguish between individual preferences and scalable product needs, and translate the most valuable opportunities into clear product priorities and specifications. The role combines elements of
product management, product analytics, and customer success
. You will be responsible not only for defining what should be built, but also for ensuring that customers understand, adopt, and benefit from the product.
Key Responsibilities
Build strong relationships with existing customers and develop a deep understanding of how they use Jaipur Robotics’ products.
Visit customer sites regularly to observe workflows, gather feedback, and identify improvement opportunities.
Evaluate customer requests critically, distinguishing scalable product needs from one-off customizations.
Consolidate feedback across customers and translate recurring needs into clear product requirements, user stories, and acceptance criteria.
Define and prioritize the product roadmap with engineering, AI, operations, commercial teams, and leadership.
Use customer feedback, product analytics, SQL, and statistical analysis to guide decisions and measure feature impact.
Monitor adoption, engagement, retention, customer satisfaction, and operational value after release.
Support product launches, onboarding, training, troubleshooting, and feature validation.
Communicate product decisions and trade-offs clearly to customers and internal teams.
Build scalable product and customer-success processes as the company expands.
Requirements
Degree in engineering, computer science, automation, data science, electronics, industrial engineering, or a related technical field.
2+ years of experience in product management, solutions engineering, product engineering, technical customer success, or a similar customer-facing role.
Strong analytical skills, including hands-on experience with SQL, relational databases, statistics, and data analysis.
Experience with product analytics or BI tools such as PostHog, Metabase, Amplitude, Mixpanel, Power BI, or Tableau.
Ability to turn complex customer and operational problems into clear, structured product requirements.
Strong judgment, communication, and customer relationship skills.
Comfortable collaborating with software, AI, field, IT, and industrial teams.
High ownership, independence, and ability to work effectively in an early-stage environment.
Willingness to travel frequently across Europe and work in industrial environments.
Fluent English.
Nice to Have
Experience with AI, computer vision, industrial automation, IoT, edge computing, or data-intensive products.
Background in industrial B2B SaaS, technical customer success, or product roles involving direct customer engagement.
Experience with manufacturing, utilities, waste management, infrastructure, or other industrial sectors.
Familiarity with Python, APIs, data pipelines, cloud platforms, and analytics engineering.
Experience in an early-stage startup or scale-up.
Knowledge of German, French, Italian, or Spanish.
Based in Europe with convenient access

Requires close customer engagement, translating field observations into product requirements, and managing the product roadmap. Blends product management, analytics, and technical customer success.

title: Product Manager
jd_text:
What you’ll do
Own the product strategy and roadmap for core Peec AI features
Define clear product requirements and success metrics in collaboration with leadership
Collaborate with our Data, Engineering, Sales and Growth teams to design, develop and launch new high-quality features
Conduct market research and competitive analysis to identify opportunities and inform product direction
Use our product daily to deeply understand user experience and identify improvement opportunities
What we’re looking for
2-5 years of product management experience, ideally in a fast-paced tech environment
Experience working in PLG (Product-Led-Growth) environments, including driving onboarding, activation, adoption, and retention
Appreciation for beautifully designed, intuitive products, with an eye for delivering exceptional user experiences
Strong understanding of AI, LLMs, or search technologies
A Strategic thinker with a hands-on mindset, able to go from high-level vision to detailed execution
Excellent communication skills with the ability to align stakeholders across disciplines
Passion for understanding users and solving real problems through product innovation
Bonus Points
Academic background in Computer Science or a technical field
Experience working at a high-growth, venture-backed startup
Previous experience building AI-driven or search-related products
What we offer
Exciting and challenging work with real impact and ownership at one of Europe’s fastest-growing Series A startups
Regular team events and off-sites
Aggressive equity compensation package
Paid Dinner & Uber home when working late
The most beautiful office space and work environment in Berlin

title: Product Manager
jd_text:
At eBay, we're more than a global ecommerce leader — we’re changing the way the world shops and sells. Our platform empowers millions of buyers and sellers in more than 190 markets around the world. We’re committed to pushing boundaries and leaving our mark as we reinvent the future of ecommerce for enthusiasts.
Our customers are our compass, authenticity thrives, bold ideas are welcome, and everyone can bring their unique selves to work — every day. We're in this together, sustaining the future of our customers, our company, and our planet.
Join a team of passionate thinkers, innovators, and dreamers — and help us connect people and build communities to create economic opportunity for all.
The Search at eBay team is responsible for the search page that drives a large part of eBay’s core marketplace business. The Search team strives to build an experience that enables users to easily explore, find, and buy what they want at the best value through an engaging, frictionless, and trusted Search experience, helping them make confident purchase decisions.
In this role, you will be responsible to building high quality advertising solutions for Search. Your expertise will drive the development of AI-driven features that improve product discovery, engagement, and overall ad quality. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in AI/ML technologies, along with a passion for applying these skills to redefine the way customers interact with our platform.
What You Will Accomplish
Lead the product strategy and roadmap for improving advertising quality on search
Be a strategic problem solver and able to synthesize data from multiple sources (including analytics, user research, marketing research, and design) to identify key customer unmet needs, key solutions that can address the needs and build product vision with search item tiles
You will work closely with a cross-functional team of engineering, design, analytics, product marketing, and user research to deliver these products
You will lead the execution of your roadmap by working with data science, engineering, and analytics teams
Build MVPs, iterate fast and be the champion of execution excellence
Conduct AB tests for launch candidates and work with analytics teams to measure the impact on success metrics
What You Will Bring
3+ years of Product Management experience
Experience with search and discovery systems, including familiarity with ranking models, search algorithms, and semantic search
Familiarity with pay per click search advertising systems
Strong analytic skills and ability to synthesize data from multiple sources
Passionate advocate for user, and has confirmed results in achieving superb user experience within tight constraints
Demonstrated leadership ability, self-motivated and directed, entrepreneurial ability to innovate quickly
Additional Details
eBay is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran status, and disability, or other legally protected status. If you have a need that requires accommodation, please contact us at talent@ebay.com. We will make every effort to respond to your request for accommodation as soon as possible. View our accessibility statement to learn more about eBay's commitment to ensuring digital accessibility for people with disabilities.
We use cookies to enhance your experience and may use AI tools for administrative tasks in the hiring process. To learn how we handle your personal data and use AI responsibly, please visit our Talent Privacy Notice, Privacy Center, and AI Hiring Guidelines.
summary: Product Manager for eBay's Search team, focusing on AI-driven advertising solutions. The role involves leading product strategy for ad quality, conducting A/B testing, and working closely with engineering and data science teams. It is a data-heavy role in a mature marketplace environment.

title: Product Manager
jd_text:
This is a role that
TechTree is recruiting for on behalf of one of our clients.
TechTree is an AI-driven recruitment platform working with high-growth companies.
When you apply, our AI Agent matches you
not just to this role
, but to other relevant opportunities across our network, so
one application can unlock multiple roles.
Product Manager — Own the core product line end-to-end at Europe’s #1 farm-level data platform. Berlin, on-site.
A well-funded agritech company is the #1 primary data collection platform for dairy in DACH, AI-enabled at the core. They raised >€10M in their first two years and signed some of the largest F&B players in Europe. Today the platform is the foundation for agricultural decarbonization across the continent — and the next chapter means new commodities and new geographies.
You will own the core product line end-to-end: from discovery to delivery to ongoing maintenance. You decide what gets built, when, and why. You lead the engineering and design squad. And you go deep on methodology — calculators, data sources, agricultural emission factors — becoming one of the most knowledgeable people in Europe on how farm-level emissions get calculated.
What You’ll Own
→ Own the product line end-to-end — discovery, delivery, maintenance; you decide what gets built
→ Lead an engineering and design squad — routines, rituals, prioritization are on you
→ Prioritize across competing demands — customer escalations, expansion bets, methodology updates
→ Go deep on emission methodology — calculators, data sources, agricultural emission factors
→ Drive expansion into new markets and commodities
→ Ship — time-to-customer-value beats perfection
What We’re Looking For
→ 2–3 years of product management experience at an early-stage B2B SaaS company that shipped fast
→ Strong prioritization — you hold competing demands in your head and communicate decisions clearly
→ Comfortable leading engineering and design
→ Some technical understanding — you can reason about systems, data models, and feasibility tradeoffs
→ Builder mentality — you’d rather have something live and imperfect than perfect and unshipped
→ AI-fluent — modern AI tools are part of your daily craft
→ Based in Berlin, on-site
Bonus:
genuine interest in farmers, agriculture, or the food system
What is TechTree?
TechTree combines AI agents with a human support team to give candidates a better, more transparent hiring experience.
Our AI Agent
gets to know your background, experience, and what you’re optimising for. It continuously matches you to live roles across our network where there’s
genuine alignment, not just keyword overlap.
Our Human Team
is there to support you through the process — answering questions, adding context, and making sure you’re never left guessing or ghosted.
We only post real jobs.
Every role on TechTree is a live, active hire.
summary: Product Manager for a well-funded AI-enabled agritech platform focusing on farm-level data and decarbonization. The role entails owning the core product line end-to-end, leading engineering squads, and going deep into agricultural methodologies. It requires a builder mentality with strong prioritization skills.
match_explanation: Good match for product ownership and early-stage scaling, but lacks the specific deep-tech/industrial networking focus.

title: Product Manager - Business Automation
jd_text:
As the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience, fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in around 65 countries worldwide powered by tech, designed by people. As one of Europe’s largest tech platforms, headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index. We enable creative minds to deliver solutions that create impact within our ecosystem. We move fast, take action and adapt. No matter where you're from or what you believe in, we build, we deliver, we lead. We are Delivery Hero.
Job Description
We are on the lookout for a Product Manager to join our Enterprise Technology Services team on our journey to always deliver amazing experiences. This role would support senior leadership in driving high-visibility Agentic AI initiatives, bridging technical product management and strategic project execution. You will lead the delivery of AI solutions tailored for People & Culture, Legal, and other departments. The ideal candidate navigates ambiguity effectively and transitions seamlessly between strategic oversight and detailed technical or operational deep dives. Your objective is to identify manual bottlenecks, deploy automation at scale, and measure the resulting operational impact.
Define and own the strategic product roadmap for internal AI and automation tools, ensuring alignment with broader business objectives.
Assess and synthesise broad organisational challenges to identify root causes, dependencies, and automation opportunities.
Lead the end-to-end product lifecycle of AI projects, such as large scale AI driven agents, Slack-integrated agents, enterprise search, and n8n workflows.
Define, track, and iterate on core success metrics, including financial savings, hours saved, ticket deflection rates, and AI adoption rates.
Facilitate cross-functional collaboration among engineering, operations, and senior stakeholders to manage dependencies and mitigate risks.
Develop structured communications, status updates, and decision-support materials to maintain executive visibility.
Qualifications
Proven background in technical program management, product management, or strategic operations within a large enterprise.
Pragmatic AI Advocate including daily usage of AI tools at expert level (e.g., Claude, Gemini) : deep understanding and a practical ability to prototype, build and automate using agentic AI focuses on business impact
Experience managing integration of enterprise platforms such as Slack, Google Workspace, Workday, Atlassian, n8n and other enterprise grade stacks.
Strong stakeholder management skills, including the ability to influence without direct authority and ask tactful yet challenging questions.
Demonstrated ability to define and own product KPIs (e.g., SLA adherence, user satisfaction, financial impact) and translate ambiguous problems into clear, prioritised engineering backlogs.
Systems Thinker: Adept at synthesising large amounts of information to navigate complex, ambiguous technical dependencies and undocumented workflows.
Nice to have
Positive Team Player: You naturally "bring good vibes" to the group. You focus on what is possible and actively contribute to a supportive, healthy workplace.
High Adaptability: You absorb rapid organisational change and shifting requirements with an open mind, free from premature assumptions or strong judgements.
Inclusive Mindset: You champion diversity of thought and background, ensuring our internal AI solutions are built equitably for a global workforce and you work well within a culturally diverse team.
Humble Learner: You stay humble and objective when evaluating feedback, prioritising continuous improvement and team success over personal ego.
Additional Information
Ensuring you and all our Heroes are looked after, happy, and healthy is always on the menu. Because if you’re in good shape, then we’re in good shape.
Make the most

title: Product Manager (m/f/d)
jd_text:
Company Description
Everyone’s story matters. Come shape your story with us at Riverty.
But where does that take you?
To one of our 30 hybrid workspaces – designed for exchanging ideas, learning from others, and shaping the way we work. An international community of over 4,000 people, representing almost 80 nationalities across 12 countries. United by one mission: Combining empathy, advanced technology and data-driven insights to keep people and businesses in flow. With payments made for them. So that they don’t have to worry about it.
And there’s more.
We are part of the family-owned Bertelsmann group. Established. Corporate. In a fast-paced industry. We enable flexible payments in various industries, simplifying the financial management of known brands and helping people repay debt to build financial confidence. In short: shaping FinTech.
Job Description
We are looking for a
Product Manager Conversational AI (m/f/d)
(unlimited, fulltime) Join our team at our locations in Berlin, Verl or Baden-Baden - flexible working conditions available
Millions of people each year end up in difficult financial situations. How they are treated in those moments matters, and technology can make it better.
As a Product Manager for Conversational AI within our BU Collection, you'll define how AI-powered experiences across chat, voice, and digital portals help people resolve debt in ways that are faster, clearer, and less stressful.
This is a builder role: you'll work hands-on with a cross-functional team spanning Platform Engineering, AI Engineering, Operations, Data, Compliance, and Corporate AI leadership, bringing structure, experimentation, and momentum to a domain that's still taking shape.
Your Responsibilities Will Be
Define and drive the product vision, strategy, and roadmap for Conversational AI across all consumer interaction channels
Analyze and prioritize key journeys (payment, installment, dispute, status inquiries) to maximize digital self-service and conversion
Bring clarity and ownership into a fragmented domain by establishing a structured product setup
Design and run experiments to validate use cases quickly, ensuring only the highest-impact bets get built without waiting for perfect conditions on interlinked systems
Ensure smooth end-to-end execution from discovery to launch and performance optimization
Define and track key performance metrics to drive continuous improvement across live channels
Coordinate cross-functionally with Platform Engineering, AI Engineering, Operations, Data, Compliance, Customer Service, and Corporate AI
Qualifications
What you bring:
Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Product Management, Information Systems, AI, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience
3–5+ years of product management experience in AI-driven products, B2C digital platforms within fintech or other regulated industries
Strong stakeholder management skills, with experience navigating complex international organizations
Ability to bring structure and prioritization to ambiguous environments with fragmented ownership
Affinity for conversational AI technologies (LLMs, NLU, chatbots, voicebots) and backend/platform constraints
Business fluent English is required; German skills are a plus
Additional Information
Disclosure requirements pertaining to the collection of your personal data:
Responsible for processing the information provided in your application is the company specified in the job advertisement, with its registered office as indicated. The company processes your data for the purpose of establishing an employment relationship on the basis of Art. 6 (1) b GDPR / Section 26 (1) sentence 1 BDSG.
The retention period for your data is determined by the statutory time limits applicable in the respective country, beginning upon completion of the recruitment process. You can find these here.
You can contact the company’s Data Protection Officer at the above-mentioned postal address.
Further information on data prote

title: AI Adoption Product Manager (m/w/d)
jd_text:
Du bekommst Energie, wenn du dich zu einer Kolleg:in setzt, ihr bei der Arbeit zuschaust und genau die Aufgabe entdeckst, die im Kleinen jede Woche Zeit kostet. Bei Candis suchen wir eine Person als AI Adoption Product Manager (m/w/d), die aus diesem Instinkt Tools macht, die Menschen wirklich gerne nutzen, und die dabei hilft, dass wir KI als die Chance sehen, die sie sein kann.
Warum brauchen wir diese Rolle?
Die KI-Adoption bei Candis läuft langsamer, als sie könnte. Unsere Teams sind im operativen Alltag stark ausgelastet, und die meisten Menschen haben keine Zeit, einen Schritt zurückzutreten und zu fragen, was automatisiert werden könnte oder wo ein kleines KI-Tool helfen würde. Hinzu kommt: Wenn unser AI Solutions Team ein Tool entwickelt hat, ist eigentlich niemand dafür verantwortlich. Wir verfolgen nicht systematisch, wer es nutzt, welche Probleme auftreten oder ob es auf weitere Bereiche ausgeweitet werden sollte. Wir wissen nicht, was wir nicht wissen, und man kann nichts adoptieren, von dem man nichts weiß. Genau diese Lücke soll diese Rolle schließen, von der ersten Idee bis zur tatsächlichen Nutzung im Alltag.
Warum ist diese Rolle spannend?
Das ist ein echter Spielplatz für jemanden, der mitgestalten will, wie ein ganzes Unternehmen mit KI arbeitet. Du bekommst kein enges Briefing und kein festes Backlog. Du entscheidest mit, welche Probleme es wert sind, gelöst zu werden, entwickelst Ideen, baust MVPs gemeinsam mit dem AI Solutions Team und begleitest sie bis zur echten Nutzung. Dein Team besteht nicht nur aus dem AI Solutions Team, sondern im Alltag aus dem ganzen Unternehmen. Jede Abteilung ist offen für Entdeckungen, und jeder Erfolg wird schnell sichtbar. Es ist eine klassische Startup-Rolle in dem Sinn, dass du einen Titel bekommst, der eine Verschmelzung aus drei zentralen Disziplinen ist: Product Management, Change Management und AI Solutions Engineering.
Was sind die größten Herausforderungen?
Die größte Herausforderung ist die Bandbreite an Menschen, die du mitnehmen musst, und das Tempo, mit dem du zwischen ihnen wechselst. Ein Gespräch führst du vielleicht mit einer Designer:in, das nächste mit jemandem aus der Verwaltung, der noch nie über Automatisierung nachgedacht hat, und jedes Mal passt du deinen Ansatz an, ohne deine Neugier und Empathie zu verlieren. Gleichzeitig brauchst du die Zuversicht, eine Entscheidung zu treffen und andere davon zu überzeugen, sowie die Widerstandsfähigkeit, damit umzugehen, wenn die Adoption langsamer läuft als erwartet oder Tools nicht genutzt werden. Ein großer Teil dieser Rolle bewegt sich in Unsicherheit, denn wir wissen selbst nicht alles, was du herausfinden sollst. Du musst dich also auch wohlfühlen, wenn du ohne fertigen Plan weitermachst.
Was möchten wir in 6 Monaten erreicht haben?
In den ersten 30 Tagen lernt diese Person zunächst, was das AI Solutions Team bereits gebaut hat und wie das aktuelle Projektboard priorisiert ist. Im Rahmen des eigenen Onboardings führt sie Einzelgespräche mit Kolleg:innen: Nachdem sie die verschiedenen Abteilungen kennengelernt hat, stellt sie Fragen dazu, wie diese KI heute nutzen und wo es hakt. Gleichzeitig beginnt sie, Beziehungen im ganzen Unternehmen aufzubauen. Auch wenn das nebensächlich klingt, ist es einer der wichtigsten Teile der Rolle
Bis Tag 60 möchten wir ein ausführliches Discovery-Gespräch mit mindestens einer Person aus jeder Abteilung sehen, eine Einarbeitung durch das AI Solutions Team in ein bestehendes Tool mit einer kurzen Adoptionsprüfung, sowie ein oder zwei vorgestellte Ideen für die nächsten Schritte, basierend auf dem bisher Gelernten.
Bis Tag 90 sollen ein oder zwei Tools live sein und sichtbar positive Wirkung zeigen.
Innerhalb des ersten Jahres möchten wir eine spürbare Reduktion manueller, wiederkehrender Arbeit im gesamten Unternehmen erreichen. Einen echten Wandel im Mindset, sodass KI-Adoption als Chance und nicht als Bedrohung wahrgenommen wird. Der Erfolg wird gemessen an ungefähr fünf Proj

title: Product Manager (m/w/d)
jd_text:
**In a nutshell:
**
📍 Standort: Berlin (hybrid)
🕓 Start: Ab sofort | Vollzeit | unbefristet
Als Product Manager (m/w/d) verantwortest Du die Weiterentwicklung unserer SaaS-Lösung für die Immobilienverwaltung (App & Plattform) in Deutschland. Du setzt die richtigen Prioritäten, arbeitest eng mit unseren Teams in Deutschland und Paris zusammen und bist die zentrale Ansprechperson für den deutschen Markt.
Aufgaben
Deine Rolle bei Matera:
Du entwickelst die Produktvision und -strategie für Deutschland auf Basis von Daten, Markt- und Wettbewerbsanalysen
Du priorisierst und verantwortest die Produkt-Roadmap in enger Abstimmung mit den deutschen Teams
Du setzt Roadmap-Themen gemeinsam mit Tech, Design, GenAI und Operations um
Du erstellst und pflegst PRDs mit klarer Begründung, Kontext und KPI-Zielen
Du koordinierst Releases, Kommunikation und Abstimmung mit den Fachbereichen
Du misst den Produkterfolg, kommunizierst Ergebnisse und leitest Optimierungen ab
Qualifikation
Das bringst du mit:
Must Have
: Du sprichst
Deutsch
und
Französisch
auf C2 Niveau.
Du lebst in Berlin und arbeitest gerne regelmäßig im Büro mit den Teams
Du hast 1–4 Jahre Erfahrung im Produktmanagement, idealerweise im Start-up-Umfeld
Du nutzt KI selbstverständlich in Deinem PM-Alltag für Analyse, Automatisierung und Prototyping
Du fühlst Dich in einem schnellen, dynamischen Umfeld wohl und treibst Themen eigenständig voran
Du arbeitest strukturiert, kommunizierst klar und kollaborierst teamübergreifend
Bei Matera garantieren wir Chancengleichheit und Vielfalt. Hier bist du so willkommen, wie du bist!
Benefits
Deine Aussichten:
Vergütung: 55.000–75.000 € brutto pro Jahr
, abhängig von Deiner Erfahrung.
Matera Benefits wie vergünstigte Urban Sports Club Mitgliedschaft, Lunch-Gutscheine + monatlicher ÖPNV-Zuschuss
Einen großen Verantwortungsbereich und echte Gestaltungsfreiheit ab dem ersten Tag: Du bist
der Product Manager für das deutsche Produkt
und hast maßgeblichen Einfluss auf dessen Entwicklung.
Ein Umfeld, in dem Du schnell lernst: Ein erfahrenes französisches Product-Team unterstützt Dich, gleichzeitig arbeitest Du direkt mit dem Country Manager und den operativen Teams zusammen.
Ein profitables, stark wachsendes Unternehmen mit einer klaren europäischen Vision.
Ein Büro in Berlin mit mindestens drei Präsenztagen pro Woche sowie gelegentlichen Reisen nach Paris, um Zeit mit dem Product- und Tech-Team zu verbringen.
Ein ambitioniertes Team mit starkem Zusammenhalt und Spaß an der Arbeit 🙂
Unsere Mission:
Unsere Mission bei Matera ist es, Wohnungseigentümer*innen glücklich zu machen! Durch uns können sie ihre Immobilie transparent und einfach selbst verwalten, oder die Verwaltung ganzheitlich in unsere Hände legen und dadurch eine Menge Zeit und Geld sparen. Seit 2017 sind wir auf dem französischen Markt aktiv und machen bereits jeden Tag das Leben von mehr als 100.000 Wohnungseigentümer*innen besser. In Deutschland haben wir seit 2021 ebenfalls einen großartigen Start mit einem ambitionierten Team hingelegt.
Um unser Team kennenzulernen und Eindrücke von unserer Kultur zu bekommen, schaue gerne bei uns auf
LinkedIn
oder auf
Instagram
vorbei!

title: Product Manager – TeamViewer ONE
jd_text:
TeamViewer provides a leading Digital Workplace platform that connects people with technology—enabling, improving and automating digital processes to make work work better. Our software solutions harness the power of AI and shape the future of digitalization.
We believe that our diverse teams and strong company culture are key to the success of our products and technologies, that hundreds of millions of users around the world and around 645,000 customers across all industries rely on. With more than 1,900 employees worldwide, we celebrate the unique perspectives and talents that each individual brings to the table and foster a dynamic work environment where new ideas thrive. Are you ready to join our team and make an impact?
Responsibilities
As a
Product Manager for TeamViewer ONE
, you will play a key role in planning and executing the roadmap for our next-generation digital workplace platform. You will work at the intersection of endpoint management, remote connectivity, and Digital Employee Experience (DEX) to help IT teams stay ahead of modern workplace challenges.
User Advocacy: Develop a deep understanding of IT managers, admins, and MSPs through regular engagement and feedback.
Discovery & Execution: Validate features through user interviews, prototypes, and data analysis to ensure we build what matters most.
Roadmap Management: Contribute to the end-to-end roadmap, taking ownership of specific features from ideation through to delivery.
Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner daily with engineering, design/UX, and marketing to bring high-quality solutions to life.
Data-Informed Iteration: Track KPIs and success metrics to guide continuous improvement and pivot when necessary.
Operational Excellence: Ensure product quality, performance, and reliability are maintained throughout the software development lifecycle.
Requirements
University degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Business, or a related field.
3–5 years experience in Product Management, ideally within a B2B SaaS environment.
Familiarity with (or a strong desire to learn) endpoint management, remote support, or IT operations software.
Comfortable using telemetry and data dashboards to inform product decisions.
Ability to engage in technical discussions regarding APIs, integrations, and security (SSO, RBAC).
Practical experience working in agile or lean product development environments.
Fluency in English (written and spoken).
Experience with ITSM tools like ServiceNow or Jira.
Exposure to automation, orchestration, policy engines or AI/ML-driven insights.
Prior experience working with MSPs or in IT operations
What We Offer
Onsite Onboarding in our HQ office for an optimal start
Great compensation and benefits packages including company achievement bonus or sales bonus and regular salary reviews
Premiums for the private pension plan (BAV) up to the maximum amount are topped up by TeamViewer
Public transport friendly offices
Option to lease an e-bike (Germany only)
Special terms for local gyms
Access to Corporate Benefits platform with many discounts
Regular Team events and company-wide celebrations
Open door policy, no dress code rules, frequent all Hands and Leadership Lunches
Hybrid and Flexible work time
Work From Abroad Program allowing up to 40 days of work outside your contracting country
We celebrate diversity as one of core values, join and drive one of the c-a-r-e initiatives together with us!
TeamViewer is an equal opportunities employer and is committed to building an inclusive culture where everyone feels welcome and supported. We C-A-R-E and understand that our diverse, values-driven culture makes us stronger. As we continue to grow as a company, we also focus on enabling our employees to grow both personally and professionally. We are proud to have an open and embracing workplace environment that will empower you to be your best no matter your gender, civil or family status, sexual orientation, religion, age, disability, education level, or race

title: Senior Product Manager – Retail Media Platform (m//f/d)
jd_text:
Company Description
About Redcare Pharmacy:
As Europe’s No.1 e-pharmacy Redcare Pharmacy is powered by passionate teams and cutting-edge innovation. We strive to create a healthy collaborative work environment where every employee feels valued and inspired to contribute to our vision “Until every human has their health”. If you’re seeking a career that offers purpose and aligns with your values join us and start your #Redcareer today.
About The Role
This is a unique opportunity to shape one of Europe’s fastest-growing retail media platforms at the intersection of health, pharmacy, and e-commerce. You will take ownership of core data and sponsored product capabilities in a high-impact environment, where your work directly influences commercial outcomes and platform scalability.
Working closely with Product, Data, Engineering, and Commercial teams, you will help define how a modern retail media platform leverages first-party data, trusted customer relationships, and advanced experimentation to drive measurable advertiser value.
Job Description
About your tasks:
Own and evolve our Sponsored Products ecosystem, including auction mechanics, bidding logic, and relevance models to continuously improve performance and ad quality
Drive the development of retail media data products, such as first-party audience segments, attribution models, and measurement frameworks in collaboration with Data & AI teams
Shape the advertiser self-service platform, enabling seamless campaign creation, optimisation, and reporting for both marketplace sellers and programmatic buyers
Build and scale experimentation frameworks, including A/B testing, incrementality testing, and platform intelligence, to unlock continuous learning and revenue growth
Partner cross-functionally with Retail Media, Marketing, and Category teams to align product development with business needs and maximise commercial impact
Qualifications
About you:
You bring 7+ years of product management experience with strong exposure to Retail Media, AdTech, or e-commerce monetisation
You have deep understanding of auction dynamics, advertiser ROI, and data-driven product design, and have owned data products end-to-end
You are technically fluent, comfortable discussing data pipelines, model design, and experimentation frameworks with engineers and data scientists
You work in a structured, independent, and highly data-driven way, navigating complex stakeholder environments and identifying dependencies across teams
Additional Information
About your benefits:
Benefits
In order to provide you with the best possible support for your individual needs - whether it‘s living a healthy life, having enough time for your family, or developing your skills - we offer a wide range of different, site-specific benefits:
Welcome days: We want you to feel at home with us from the very first day. Therefore, we welcome you with our comprehensive onboarding program.
Work from Home: If your job does not require you to be present in the office, we can arrange the place you work from individually - even for up to 20 days a year anywhere in the EU.
Mobility: We provide our employees with a fully costed Deutschland Ticket which can be used at any time. Click here to learn more.
Personal development: Grow! We support and encourage your individual development through various in- and external trainings.
Vacation: 30 days paid vacation per annum (plus another day on Rosenmontag) and the option to take unpaid vacation of up to 10 days (after one year working with us).

title: Product Manager for Plastic Modular Belts
jd_text:
Role description
As a Product Manager (m/f/x), you are responsible for the Plastics division, build trusting and long-term customer relationships, and identify individual needs with a strong sense of insight. In close and collaborative partnership with the management team, you develop strategic approaches and implement them in a goal-oriented manner to ensure sustainable revenue and long-term business success.
Key responsibilites
The fact that our customers are satisfied and accustomed to high quality is thanks to you: you maintain personal customer relationships and build new ones. You also know how to analyze specific customer needs and present tailored solutions. We place our trust in you from the very beginning.
You take on product responsibility for the Plastics division.
You make a significant impact by developing appropriate market strategies in direct cooperation with our Head of Business Unit and by monitoring the sales market and competitors. In doing so, you create transparency and structure.
Through your personal commitment, you achieve the planned sales and profit targets in your sales territory - and it shows.
What we look for
You have a degree in industrial engineering or a qualification as a technician/master craftsman (m/f/x) (or comparable), at least one year of experience in a similar position, and are looking for a new long-term challenge where you can learn a lot. Then you’ve come to the right place.
In addition, you enjoy working in a conceptual and strategic way and have a solid technical understanding, combined with an affinity for IT (MS Office, CRM systems, etc.). Great – this know-how will help you and us successfully master upcoming challenges.
You also have very good language skills in German and English - then you are an excellent fit for us!
What we offer
We want you to feel comfortable. Through intensive structured onboarding and training, you will quickly get to know your tasks and our company. You will become part of a strong team.
We value your work. You will receive attractive, performance-based compensation along with many additional benefits such as employee savings schemes, employer contributions to pension plans, additional private health insurance, meal subsidies in our canteen, free parking, bike leasing, free beverages, and more.
You will receive everything you need: office equipment including laptop, mobile phone, and tablet, as well as a neutral company car that you can also use privately.
This is how we keep things moving. You will be able to take on exciting challenges in a company with flat hierarchies and short decision-making paths.

title: Data Product Manager, Dynamic Assortment - (Quick Commerce)
jd_text:
As the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience, fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in around 65 countries worldwide powered by tech, designed by people. As one of Europe’s largest tech platforms, headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index. We enable creative minds to deliver solutions that create impact within our ecosystem. We move fast, take action and adapt. No matter where you're from or what you believe in, we build, we deliver, we lead. We are Delivery Hero.
Job Description
We are on the lookout for a
Product Manager
to join the Dynamic Assortment tribe within the Quick Commerce team on our journey to always deliver amazing experiences.
Be part of redefining how customers experience quick commerce. You’ll help build technology that scales our non-food offerings, reaching new market segments and driving revenue growth. By innovating within our Quick Commerce Team, you’ll make Delivery Hero the go-to platform for a broad range of products, helping us grow faster and deliver more value to customers around the world.
The Dynamic Assortment team operates as a specialized data team within the QC Assortment domain. Our mission is to maximize customer lifetime value and drive profitable growth by transforming our static product catalog into an adaptive and dynamic one. We develop and refine sophisticated data models that intelligently curate and personalize the product assortment, enhancing the customer experience and optimizing business outcomes. Our work is a critical enabler of the Quick Commerce strategy to make every product "easy to list, effortless to discover, and inspiring to convert."
We are seeking a strong Product Manager to own and deliver the data products that power our intelligent digital shelf. You will work closely with data science, engineering, and cross-domain stakeholders to translate our vision into a tangible product roadmap. You will be at the forefront of transforming our static catalog into an adaptive, personalized experience by leveraging unified demand intelligence, deploying advanced machine learning models, and embedding a culture of continuous experimentation and optimization.
This role requires a blend of strategic thinking, technical depth, and a passion for data. You will be responsible for the entire lifecycle of our data products, from ideation and model development to experimentation and performance monitoring, directly influencing key growth metrics such as conversion rates, average order value, and customer retention.
Drive Product Vision & Roadmap: Translate the team's vision into a clear, actionable product roadmap. Define and prioritize features and initiatives based on business impact, customer value, and technical feasibility.
Lead Data Model Development: Partner with data scientists and engineers to develop, refine, and deploy machine learning models for intelligent product curation, personalization, and substitution.
Own Experimentation & Optimization: Embed systematic experimentation and rapid iteration into the product development workflow. Design, run, and analyze A/B tests to validate hypotheses and drive measurable improvements in algorithm performance and business KPIs.
Deliver on Key Initiatives: Lead the execution of strategic projects, such as implementing experimentation with customer-facing Category Tree structure, driving the Category Recommendation engine, and developing models for automated merchandising and cross-listing.
Cross-Functional Stakeholder Alignment: Partner with stakeholders across Merchandising, Catalog, Data Analytics, Platforms Product, and commercial teams to align on strategies and ensure our data products meet business needs. Articulate complex data concepts to non-technical audiences to build consensus and drive decision-making across our global platforms.
Own Metrics & Performance: Est

It involves leading the development of machine learning models to improve personalized recommendations. The PM works closely with data scientists to run A/B tests and optimize business KPIs.

title: Senior Product Manager (m/f/d)
jd_text:
At FORMEL SKIN, we’re redefining how dermatological care is accessed — combining medical excellence with cutting-edge technology to build a truly global online dermatology clinic. Having already delivered over one million treatments and backed by leading European health and consumer investors, we’ve recently joined forces with Voy to create one of the most ambitious health tech platforms globally. As we launch and scale new treatment categories in Germany, our growth ambitions are accelerating — and building exceptional teams is central to making that happen. Based in Berlin, a city known for its vibrancy, we’re committed to expanding our reach and impact, offering you the opportunity to be part of our exciting journey.
Since we are expanding our portfolio of categories for customers, we are looking for an additional (Senior) Product Manager to join us. This role is crucial for driving growth and ensuring that every touchpoint of our customer journey is optimized for clarity, trust, and impact.
You’ll brin
g deep expertise in CRO, ecommerce funnels, and landing page optimizati
on, with a proven track record in industries like cosmetics, beauty, health tech, or other direct-to-consumer models.You’ll be hands-on, highly self-reliant, and experienced enough to bot
h teach us new best practices and close current ga
ps in our CRO approach. You’ll work cross-functionally with Product, Design, Marketing, and Engineering to create meaningful improvements that directly impact revenue and customer experience
Your Responsibilities
Lead CRO initiatives across the entire funnel: landing pages, onboarding flow, basket, and checkout.
Define hypotheses, design and run A/B tests, and systematically improve conversion rates.
Collaborate with Product, Growth, Design, and Engineering to execute changes quickly and effectively.
Develop and maintain a structured experimentation framework, ensuring statistical rigor and actionable insights.
Analyze performance data to identify friction points, drop-offs, and opportunities for optimization.
Bring a strong UX perspective: create smooth, trustworthy, and engaging experiences that convert.
Act as a CRO expert within the company: upskill teams, share knowledge, and push for a culture of testing and learning.
Your Profile
5+ years experience in Conversion Rate Optimization, ideally in
ecommerce, cosmetics/beauty, or health tech
.
Proven track record in
landing page optimization, funnel design, and basket/checkout improvements
.
hands-on experience with
Shopify or similar commerce platforms
.
Strong understanding of
UX, user psychology, and customer behavior
.
Skilled in A/B testing tools, analytics platforms, and experimentation frameworks.
High agency, self-starter mentality: you take ownership, move fast, and drive results.
Excellent communicator with experience working in
cross-functional teams
.
Fluent in English; German is a plus.
Why work with us?
Drive growth across the customer journey:
As Senior Product Manager, you’ll optimize every touchpoint from landing page to checkout, running experiments that boost conversions, revenue, and trust. Collaborate across teams, lead testing initiatives, and shape the experience that turns visitors into loyal customers.
Benefits to perfectly fit you:
every candidate is unique, and we're dedicated to supporting your specific needs and aspirations. To support this, we offer a diverse benefits package including 28 days vacation, a NAVIT or Urban Sports Club membership, employee discounts on our products, a professional development budget, flexible working hours & a remote work allowance.
Building a great team together:
At FORMEL SKIN, we believe that building a great team and fostering strong interpersonal relationships are fundamental to our success. If you're passionate about collaborating with like-minded individuals, you'll appreciate our approach, which includes regular company events, a team event budget, and bonuses for employee referrals.

title: Product Manager - Payments
jd_text:
About IXOPAY
IXOPAY is the enterprise-grade global payment infrastructure platform built for the era of agentic commerce. We equip merchants and enterprises with AI-driven intelligence, payment orchestration, advanced tokenization, and the tools to optimize every stage of the payments journey.
From intelligent routing and compliance to customizable modules and enterprise-scale orchestration, IXOPAY helps businesses integrate faster, improve payment performance, and expand globally with confidence.
At IXOPAY, our people are our greatest strength. Our collaborative culture is built on shared values that guide how we work, innovate, and support our customers.
About The Role
We're looking for a Product Manager to help shape the future of IXOPAY's Payments Intelligence platform.
In this role, you'll work closely with Engineering, Design, Sales, Customer Success, and enterprise customers to build data-driven products that help merchants optimize payment performance, improve conversion rates, manage risk, and make smarter business decisions. You'll balance customer needs, market trends, and business priorities to deliver innovative solutions that have measurable impact.
What You'll Do
Own and prioritize the product backlog for Payment front-end related products and AI Payments Intelligence.
Define product requirements, user stories, and roadmaps based on customer feedback, market trends, and business value.
Partner with Engineering to deliver features including observability, AI-powered insights, anomaly detection, dashboards, and workflow automation.
Collaborate with Design to create intuitive user experiences for analytics, reporting, configuration, and payment management.
Analyze payment and transaction data trends to identify opportunities, validate product decisions, and define meaningful KPIs.
Work closely with customers and internal stakeholders to understand challenges, gather feedback, and continuously improve the platform.
Support go-to-market activities by creating product documentation, training materials, and enablement resources.
Stay current with emerging trends in payments, AI, analytics, and regulatory requirements to inform produc
What You'll Bring
Required
5+ years of Product Management or Payments Management experience with focus on data analytics or payment setups
Strong understanding of payment ecosystems, including payment orchestration, acquiring, payment gateways, PSPs, or financial operations.
Experience defining product roadmaps, managing backlogs, and delivering products using Agile methodologies.
Strong analytical skills with experience using data to guide product decisions and measure outcomes.
Experience working with cross-functional teams including Engineering, Design, Sales, and Customer Success.
Excellent communication, stakeholder management, and prioritization skills.
Preferred
Experience working for a merchant, payment service provider, acquirer, or payments technology company.
Experience with business intelligence or analytics platforms such as Grafana, Tableau, or Metabase.
Experience building AI-powered or data-intensive products.
Familiarity with UX design processes and creating user experiences for analytics or enterprise applications.
Location
This is a remote-friendly position based in Germany or European Union . We welcome applications from candidates across Germany , with a preference for those located in or near Munich. You'll have the flexibility to work remotely or from our Munich office.
Why Join IXOPAY?
At IXOPAY, you'll help define the future of enterprise payments by building products that combine orchestration, AI, and data intelligence into a single platform. You'll work on meaningful industry challenges, collaborate with experienced teams, and have a direct impact on product strategy and customer success.
We Offer
Competitive salary and benefits
Professional growth and development opportunities
A collaborative, international team environment
Flexible remote working with the opti

title: Senior Product Manager – Retail Media Platform (m//f/d)
jd_text:
About Redcare Pharmacy:
As Europe’s No.1 e-pharmacy Redcare Pharmacy is powered by passionate teams and cutting-edge innovation. We strive to create a healthy collaborative work environment where every employee feels valued and inspired to contribute to our vision “Until every human has their health”. If you’re seeking a career that offers purpose and aligns with your values join us and start your #Redcareer today.
About the role:
This is a unique opportunity to shape one of Europe’s fastest-growing retail media platforms at the intersection of health, pharmacy, and e-commerce. You will take ownership of core data and sponsored product capabilities in a high-impact environment, where your work directly influences commercial outcomes and platform scalability.
Working closely with Product, Data, Engineering, and Commercial teams, you will help define how a modern retail media platform leverages first-party data, trusted customer relationships, and advanced experimentation to drive measurable advertiser value.
Job Description
About your tasks:
Own and evolve our Sponsored Products ecosystem, including auction mechanics, bidding logic, and relevance models to continuously improve performance and ad quality
Drive the development of retail media data products, such as first-party audience segments, attribution models, and measurement frameworks in collaboration with Data & AI teams
Shape the advertiser self-service platform, enabling seamless campaign creation, optimisation, and reporting for both marketplace sellers and programmatic buyers
Build and scale experimentation frameworks, including A/B testing, incrementality testing, and platform intelligence, to unlock continuous learning and revenue growth
Partner cross-functionally with Retail Media, Marketing, and Category teams to align product development with business needs and maximise commercial impact
Qualifications
About you:
You bring 7+ years of product management experience with strong exposure to Retail Media, AdTech, or e-commerce monetisation
You have deep understanding of auction dynamics, advertiser ROI, and data-driven product design, and have owned data products end-to-end
You are technically fluent, comfortable discussing data pipelines, model design, and experimentation frameworks with engineers and data scientists
You work in a structured, independent, and highly data-driven way, navigating complex stakeholder environments and identifying dependencies across teams
Additional Information
About your benefits:
In order to provide you with the best possible support for your individual needs - whether it‘s living a healthy life, having enough time for your family, or developing your skills - we offer a wide range of different, site-specific benefits:
Welcome days: We want you to feel at home with us from the very first day. Therefore, we welcome you with our comprehensive onboarding program.
Work from Home: If your job does not require you to be present in the office, we can arrange the place you work from individually - even for up to 20 days a year anywhere in the EU.
Mobility: We provide our employees with a fully costed Deutschland Ticket which can be used at any time. Click here to learn more.
Personal development: Grow! We support and encourage your individual development through various in- and external trainings.
Vacation: 30 days paid vacation per annum (plus another day on Rosenmontag) and the option to take unpaid vacation of up to 10 days (after one year working with us).

title: Product Manager - Data-Viz Startup - B2B SaaS
jd_text:
At Vizzlo, we create tools that make data visualization accessible to everyone.
Creating high-quality, on-brand charts, visuals, and business graphics used to be hard. Vizzlo changes that. We offer the only tool on the market that integrates with Google Slides, PowerPoint, and more. Whether for presentations, business documents, or the Web—with Vizzlo, anyone can visualize their ideas in seconds.
We are a small team of enthusiasts in a SaaS startup. We’re proud of our hands-on culture, we talk plainly to each other, and improve anything we’re working on until it’s perfect.
We are looking for a hands-on and product-obsessed
Product Manager
to join our team remotely within Germany or in our offices in Leipzig. This is a full-time role ideal for someone who geeks out about data visualization, isn't afraid of Markdown specs and nerdy engineers, and is ready to do whatever it takes to ship an awesome product.
Tasks
As our Product Manager, you own the "what" and the "why" behind everything we ship. You talk to customers, dig through usage data, write crisp specs, and sit down with our engineers to turn ideas into features people love. In a small team, no task is "not your job"—and you wouldn't have it any other way.
Your responsibilities are:
Product Roadmap
: Define and prioritize what we build next based on customer feedback, market insights, and business goals.
Specs & Documentation
: Write clear, detailed specs in Markdown—because that's how we roll—so engineering knows exactly what to build and why.
Customer Discovery
: Talk to customers. A lot. Understand their workflows, pain points, and what "mind-blowing" means to them.
Cross-Team Collaboration
: Work
daily
with engineering and design to ship iteratively and fast.
AI Exploration
: Evaluate where AI can genuinely make our product smarter—and where it's just hype.
Data-Driven Decisions
: Track product metrics, run experiments, and use data to validate ideas (or kill your darlings).
Get Shit Done
: Do whatever it takes to move the product forward—whether that's coordinating a launch, triaging a bug, answering a support ticket, or making coffee for the team.
Requirements
Excellent written and spoken English skills
(a must); Good German skills are a plus.
2+ years of experience in product management, ideally in a SaaS or B2B environment.
A genuine interest in data visualization, charts, and making complex information look great.
The ability to write specs that engineers actually enjoy reading. Markdown fluency is a must.
No fear of working closely with nerdy engineers—in fact, you consider yourself one of them.
Curiosity about AI/ML and a pragmatic sense for where it can improve a product.
A bias for action: you'd rather ship something imperfect and iterate than debate for weeks.
Bonus: you've used R, PowerBI, Tableau, D3.js, played with data viz tools, or have opinions about bar charts vs. pie charts.
Benefits
Full-time position with a competitive remuneration package.
Start-up environment where your talents and motivation can make a real impact.
International team of highly motivated doers.
Fully remote position (within Germany), with ultra-nice offices in Leipzig (Plagwitz) for optional in-person collaboration.
Need something else? Ask!

title: Senior Product Manager (all genders welcome)
jd_text:
Deine Rolle
Freshly cooked food. Everywhere. All the time.
With our robots, we’re transforming the way the world cooks and enjoys food. Through technology that supports the foodservice industry, reduces food waste, and elevates culinary quality to a new, scalable level. Accessible to everyone. Everywhere. Anytime. We give our all to make this happen. Across every level, team and function.
As Senior Product Manager –
starting from November 2026
– you own key pillars of Halo: the digital infrastructure connecting our robotic kitchens to operators, customers, and back-office systems. You take end-to-end responsibility for complex product areas, moving fast from discovery to launch. You're execution-heavy. You ship, iterate, and keep the product moving, freeing the Lead PM to focus on strategy.
These are the tasks you will be responsible for:
End-to-End Ownership: You own product areas within our Halo platform – like payment integrations, operator interfaces and guest-facing features for our robotic kitchens. From discovery through launch and post-launch iteration.
Prototype Fast: You move from spec to clickable prototype in days using AI tools (Cursor, Lovable, Claude, Figma Make) to validate assumptions before engineering commits.
User-Driven Discovery: You conduct field research with operators, kitchen staff, and gastronomy partners using our robots. You translate signal into prioritized roadmaps, not just internal alignment.
Cross-Functional Execution: You coordinate with engineering, design, sales, account management, and remote operations. You define scope, push for shippable v1s, and own success metrics.
Requirements & Specs: You gather requirements, interview stakeholders, and build comprehensive specs for engineering teams.
Shape Operating Model: You improve standards, intake processes, and onboarding flows alongside the Lead PM.
Dein Profil
You’re passionate about innovative technologies, excited about the role, and ready to help us revolutionize the worlds of tech and culinary even further? Let’s get cooking!
These are the qualities helping you thrive in this role:
4–6+ Years of Product Management: You've shipped complex B2B or hardware-adjacent products end-to-end – navigating real-world constraints in physical operations, hardware, robotics, or kitchen tech.
Prototype-First & Execution-Heavy: You default to showing, not telling. You produce mockups and specs that engineering can execute on without translation on technical tradeoffs.
Customer-Driven & Field-Oriented: You run interviews, synthesize insights, and talk directly to operators and kitchen staff. You get out of the office.
Cross-Functional Command: You manage stakeholders across sales, operations, design, and leadership without consensus paralysis.
Owner Mindset: You treat your product area like a small business. You cut scope, ship v1 first, stay calm in chaos, and move with high agency and low ego.
Fluent English: Verbal and written – for clear communication across distributed teams.
Über uns
Bei goodBytz bist du mehr als nur ein Teil eines Teams – du wirst eine wichtige Mitgestalterin bzw. ein wichtiger Mitgestalter sein.
Wir suchen Kolleg:innen, die nicht nur mitdenken, sondern auch den Mut haben, kritische Gedanken zu äußern. Denn Respekt, Vertrauen und ein offener Austausch sind für uns die Basis für Erfolg und Zufriedenheit.
Es erwarten dich:
goodBytz Culineers Canteen:
Unser Demo-Roboter felix versorgt dich rund um die Uhr mit frischen Mahlzeiten zu einem vergünstigten Mitarbeiterpreis.
Mental Health matters:
Zugang zur Mental-Health-Plattform OpenUp, auf der du vertrauliche 1:1-Sessions mit zertifizierten Psycholog*innen, Gesundheits- und Finanzexpert*innen buchen kannst – wann immer du sie brauchst. Bis zu zwei Familienmitglieder erhalten ebenfalls einen personalisierten Zugang.
Bikeleasing:
Dein neues Fahrrad wartet schon auf dich! Nach bestandener Probezeit kannst du unser Bike-Leasing-Angebot nutzen und dir dein Traumrad aussuchen.
Flexible Arbei

title: Senior Product Manager - Marketplace
jd_text:
Your Mission
With the power of AI, we're set to revolutionize the way general contractors and subcontractors find and match with each other on our marketplace. As our Senior Product Manager Marketplace, you'll actively shape this product vision — in a small team with big leverage and direct impact on a product used by thousands.
Your Responsibilities
You own the marketplace dynamics between General contractors and Subcontractors on our platform.
You ensure a healthy balance between supply and demand, and build mechanisms that keep liquidity strong on both sides.
You own search, matching and recommendation mechanisms that bring Subcontractors and projects together in a way that increases relevance and bid rate.
You analyze marketplace metrics and translate them into hypotheses and priorities.
You identify AI use cases for matching and recommendations and implement them together with our designers and engineers.
What You Bring
3–5 years of product management experience with two- or multi-sided marketplaces.
Demonstrated experience thinking through supply-demand dynamics: how to create enough supply, enough demand, and how to balance the sometimes conflicting interests of both sides.
Experience with search or matching algorithms, or working closely with data and engineering teams on ranking or recommendation logic.
Strong analytical skills and a solid understanding of marketplace metrics such as liquidity, conversion, and network effects.
You use AI tools such as LLM-based assistants, MCPs, and no-code prototyping routinely in your day-to-day work.
Fluent in written and spoken German and English.
Your Benefits
Help shape a product that solves real-world problems in the construction industry through AI and is revolutionizing the market.
Work in an open-minded, dynamic, and international team.
Remote or from our modern office in Berlin Friedrichshain.
Regular company and team events (offline and online).
Permanent contract and modern equipment.
Open feedback culture with regular 1:1s and development conversations.
About Us
Cosuno – Revolutionizing construction through technology.
We are Cosuno – a fast-growing tech startup that is making the construction industry more efficient and transparent with our digital platform for tenders and procurement processes. Our goal: to solve the most complex challenges in the industry with innovation and simplicity.
Why Cosuno?
Artificial intelligence is the key to our solution: it analyzes millions of price data points and helps construction companies create precise and efficient bids. This not only saves our customers time but also helps them avoid unnecessary costs.
But for us, it's not just about technology – it's about the people who drive it. At Cosuno, you will work with creative minds who are reshaping the construction industry. We believe in diversity because we know that the best ideas come from different perspectives. An integrative and inclusive work environment is a matter of course for us.
Join us – Build the future of construction.

momox

Full Description
Our mission
We are on a mission to save our planet - one second hand product at a time.
As Product Owner - Onsite Experience, you will own momox fashion’s web shop and all associated processes. You will collaborate with a cross-functional team of stakeholders and engineers to provide our customers with a best-in-class shopping experience.
Your mission
Product Strategy & Roadmap: Co-create a data-driven vision and product strategy and translate it into an actionable product roadmap. Define and prioritize initiatives based on business impact, customer value, and technical feasibility

Your profile
Experience: At least 5 years of experience as a Product Owner, Product Manager, or in a similar role, including experience in the e-commerce sector

Technical Expertise: Experience in e-commerce product development, particularly search functionalities, conversion rate optimization, and personalization. Familiarity with backend and service integrations (e.g., recommendation engines, payment providers, search providers), and data-driven decision-making. Familiarity with Shopify is a plus
Methodological Proficiency: Skilled in agile methodologies, user testing, and ideation techniques, with experience using analytics tools like Google Analytics and Hotjar
Interpersonal Skills: Strong communication and presentation abilities, with experience in leading and motivating cross-functional teams
Analytical Acumen: Expertise in utilizing analytics tools to inform decisions and assess performance
Customer-Focused Approach: Ability to apply Design Thinking and user-centric methodologies to create innovative solutions that address customer needs and pain points
You can look forward to

Open work environment: We work together in a bright, open-plan office where even the few existing doors are always open. Our teams are motivated and open to new ideas. Bring your creativity to the table and make things happen! If you need a moment of peace, you can retreat to a cozy corner of

title: Senior AI Product Manager (f/m/d)
jd_text:
Unsere Opportunity
Predium hat in den letzten drei Jahren die führende ESG-Intelligence-Plattform für institutionelle Immobilienunternehmen in der DACH-Region gebaut - mit den stärksten Namen der Branche als Kunden: Asset Manager, Wohnungsbaugesellschaften, Banken. Unsere Kundenbasis, die Daten, unsere Glaubwürdigkeit sind wertvolles Kapital für den nächsten Schritt. Investment-, Kredit- und Asset-Management-Entscheidung werden in der Real Estate Branche noch immer in Excel getroffen, Fall für Fall. Wir bauen jetzt den AI-Analysten, der diese Arbeit übernimmt. Direkt auf den Daten des Kunden, skalierbar ohne zusätzliche Köpfe. Wir sind stark finanziert und bringen KI in die Real Estate Branche.
Deine Mission
Du verantwortest das Produkt auf beiden Ebenen: das bestehende ESG-Produkt weiterentwickeln und die nächste Evolutionsstufe mitgestalten, den AI-Analysten für Asset Manager, Credit Officers und Transaction Manager. Du arbeitest eng mit Engineers, Kunden und unserem Gründer- und Führungsteam zusammen. Du bist verantwortlich für Kundennutzen und Umsatz, nicht für Features. Du gehst zum Kunden mit, verstehst ihre Welt, und übersetzt das in validierte Prototypen und klare Build-Entscheidungen.
Was du konkret tust
Own strategic product development: Du gehst raus zu unseren Kunden, verstehst ihre Prozesse sowie pain points, die sie selbst ohne dein Zutun nicht erwähnen. Du baust eigenverantwortlich Produkte, die die Nachhaltigkeit von Immobilien fördern und einen messbaren finanziellen Impact auf unsere Kunden und unser Produkt haben.
Collaborate closely: Du arbeitest eng mit Gründern / Geschäftsleitung und einem starken Engineering-Team zusammen und erhältst jegliche Unterstützung, um die richtigen Produkte schnell an den Kunden zu bringen.
Iterate fast, finish fully: Du erstellst Prototypen mit AI-Tools und hast die Freiheit, selbständig und schnell zu iterieren, um Ideen in Stunden zu testen statt in Wochen. Speed und Urteilsvermögen sind dein Kompass. „Shipped" bedeutet für dich: der Kunde nutzt es, und es liefert Wert.
Woran wir Erfolg messen
Du hast in den ersten 6 Monaten zu 2-3 vielversprechenden Product Bets durch echte Iteration mit Kundenfeedback beigetragen. Du bist in mindestens einem Kernbereich so tief drin, dass Engineers und Kunden dir vertrauen, wichtige Entscheidungen zu treffen.
Was du mitbringst
Du hast bereits digitale B2B-Produkte end-to-end entwickelt und erfolgreich iteriert. Du verstehst Kundenprobleme, denkst wirtschaftlich und hast nachweislich Mehrwert für Kunden und das Business geschaffen.
Du gehst souverän mit Unsicherheit um. Du definierst Hypothesen, testest schnell, triffst Entscheidungen auf Basis unvollständiger Informationen und übernimmst Verantwortung, statt auf perfekte Anforderungen zu warten.
AI ist fester Bestandteil deiner täglichen Arbeitsweise. Du nutzt LLMs, Agents und moderne AI-Tools wie Cursor oder Claude produktiv und hast bereits AI-Features oder AI-Workflows entwickelt – inklusive der typischen Herausforderungen wie Evals, Latenz, Halluzinationen oder Human-in-the-Loop.
Du kommunizierst klar und überzeugend. Du arbeitest eng mit Kunden, Design, Engineering und Go-to-Market zusammen und kannst komplexe Sachverhalte verständlich vermitteln.
Du glaubst daran, dass außergewöhnliche Produkte durch enge Zusammenarbeit entstehen. Deshalb bist du vier Tage pro Woche mit dem Team im Münchner Büro.
Bonus Punkte
Du hast selbst ein Unternehmen gegründet oder kommerzielle AI Lösungen gebaut.
Erfahrung mit Immobilien-, ESG- oder Finanzdaten.
Dealbreakers
Du willst primär selbst Production-Code besitzen. Diese Rolle entscheidet, was gebaut wird und warum, und misst sich am Kundennutzen, nicht an gemergten PRs.
Du brauchst klare Spezifikationen und abgestimmte Roadmaps, bevor du anfängst. Ambiguität verlangsamt dich, statt dich neugierig zu machen.
AI-Tools sind für dich noch kein Teil deines täglichen Workflows, nicht als Lernbereitschaft, sondern als aktuelle Realität.
Du arbeitest lieber

title: Product Operator – AI-Powered Customer Experiences (m/f/d)
jd_text:
Company Description
Everyone’s story matters. Come shape your story with us at Riverty.
But where does that take you?
To one of our 30 hybrid workspaces – designed for exchanging ideas, learning from others, and shaping the way we work. An international community of over 4,000 people, representing almost 80 nationalities across 12 countries. United by one mission: Combining empathy, advanced technology and data-driven insights to keep people and businesses in flow. With payments made for them. So that they don’t have to worry about it.
And there’s more.
We are part of the family-owned Bertelsmann group. Established. Corporate. In a fast-paced industry. We enable flexible payments in various industries, simplifying the financial management of known brands and helping people repay debt to build financial confidence. In short: shaping FinTech.
Job Description
We are looking for a
Product Manager Conversational AI (m/f/d)
(unlimited, fulltime) Join our team at our locations in Berlin, Verl or Baden-Baden - flexible working conditions available
Millions of people each year end up in difficult financial situations. How they are treated in those moments matters, and technology can make it better.
As a Product Manager for Conversational AI within our BU Collection, you'll define how AI-powered experiences across chat, voice, and digital portals help people resolve debt in ways that are faster, clearer, and less stressful.
This is a builder role: you'll work hands-on with a cross-functional team spanning Platform Engineering, AI Engineering, Operations, Data, Compliance, and Corporate AI leadership, bringing structure, experimentation, and momentum to a domain that's still taking shape.
Your Responsibilities Will Be
Define and drive the product vision, strategy, and roadmap for Conversational AI across all consumer interaction channels
Analyze and prioritize key journeys (payment, installment, dispute, status inquiries) to maximize digital self-service and conversion
Bring clarity and ownership into a fragmented domain by establishing a structured product setup
Design and run experiments to validate use cases quickly, ensuring only the highest-impact bets get built without waiting for perfect conditions on interlinked systems
Ensure smooth end-to-end execution from discovery to launch and performance optimization
Define and track key performance metrics to drive continuous improvement across live channels
Coordinate cross-functionally with Platform Engineering, AI Engineering, Operations, Data, Compliance, Customer Service, and Corporate AI
Qualifications
What you bring:
Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Product Management, Information Systems, AI, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience
3–5+ years of product management experience in AI-driven products, B2C digital platforms within fintech or other regulated industries
Strong stakeholder management skills, with experience navigating complex international organizations
Ability to bring structure and prioritization to ambiguous environments with fragmented ownership
Affinity for conversational AI technologies (LLMs, NLU, chatbots, voicebots) and backend/platform constraints
Business fluent English is required; German skills are a plus
Additional Information
Disclosure requirements pertaining to the collection of your personal data:
Responsible for processing the information provided in your application is the company specified in the job advertisement, with its registered office as indicated. The company processes your data for the purpose of establishing an employment relationship on the basis of Art. 6 (1) b GDPR / Section 26 (1) sentence 1 BDSG.
The retention period for your data is determined by the statutory time limits applicable in the respective country, beginning upon completion of the recruitment process. You can find these here.
You can contact the company’s Data Protection Officer at the above-mentioned postal address.
Further information on data prote

title: Product Lead – Live Strategy Games (m/f/d)
jd_text:
We are looking for a
Senior Product Manager / Product Lead
to own the product vision and roadmap for Goodgame Empire and Empire: Four Kingdoms - two of Europe's most established strategy games with over 200 million players and more than 1 billion € in lifetime revenue. These are live service products with active player bases, real revenue, and decisions that matter immediately.
We need someone who can hold the vision, read the numbers, and make the hard calls on what the product should be - then drive a cross-functional team to get there. If you are a senior product manager looking for a role with real ownership and long-term ambition, this role is for you.
YOUR MISSION
Own the product roadmap for both Empire titles: set priorities, communicate rationale, and ensure strategy translates into shipped results
Be the final product voice on feature scope, economy design, and Live Ops direction
Lead and develop a multi-functional product team covering Game Design, Live Ops, and CRM
WHAT YOU WILL DO
Translate the product strategy into a clear, outcome-driven roadmap and keep all stakeholders aligned on priority and reasoning
Define feature requirements, success metrics, and measurable outcomes; create the structured briefs and documentation that enable efficient execution
Approve concepts from Design, Live Ops, and CRM - and make sure offers, campaigns, and sales calendars are in service of the roadmap and business targets
Maintain deep KPI fluency: read retention, revenue, and player behavior patterns and turn those insights into product decisions
Spot opportunities and risks across the full product funnel - from acquisition to late-game engagement
Provide disciplinary leadership and development-focused feedback to your product team members
YOUR BACKSTORY
Proven track record in senior product management for live F2P games
Strong product sense: you understand what drives player behavior, how F2P monetization and economies work, and how to evaluate feature impact before shipping
Experience with Live Ops and CRM as product levers, not just operational tasks
Ability to create stakeholder alignment and drive confident decisions - you don't need consensus to move
Clear, direct communicator who writes crisp requirements and can translate complex tradeoffs into simple choices for others
Experience leading or developing a product team, with a genuine interest in people development
Comfortable navigating data without being dependent on analysts for every answer
WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS
Product is the core of what the Empire Family does - and this is the role that connects long-term strategy to what ships every week. You report directly to the Product Director, have real decision authority from day one, and work inside the product rather than alongside it. The Empire Family is in an active development phase with meaningful long-term projects on the horizon: a platform transition, a new continent launching in 2027, and a serious bid for renewed growth. There is real work here, and the scope to do it properly.
WHAT WE OFFER
remote work within Germany
30 days of vacation
Flat hierarchies and direct access to senior leadership from day one
A stable, profitable product with real scope for long-term impact
About Goodgame Studios
Goodgame Studios is known for data-driven game development and successful live service strategy titles. We self-publish our games in-house with all relevant disciplines under one roof. Our studio is based in Hamburg, Germany. We welcome applications from international candidates - English is our working language
.
As part of
Stillfront Group
, we combine long-term IP growth with a sharp focus on execution excellence.
