title: Senior Open Market Sales Manager (m/f/d)
jd_text:
About Huawei
Huawei is a leading global provider of information and communications technology (ICT) infrastructure and smart devices. With integrated solutions across four key domains – telecom networks, IT, smart devices, and cloud services – we are committed to bringing digital to every person, home and organization for a fully connected, intelligent world.
At Huawei, innovation focuses on customer needs. We invest heavily in basic research, concentrating on technological breakthroughs that drive the world forward. With approximately 208,000 employees, we operate in more than 170 countries and regions.
Huawei's end-to-end portfolio of products, solutions and services are both competitive and secure. Through open collaboration with ecosystem partners, we create lasting value for our customers, working to empower people, enrich home life, and inspire innovation in organizations of all shapes and sizes.
Job Title: Senior Open Market Sales Manager (m/f/d)
Job Location: Düsseldorf or Munich, Germany
Main Responsibilities:
Build and maintain long-term relationships with channel partners through effective communication and a strong understanding of sales processes and market trends in CE channels.
Develop and implement sales strategies tailored to CE channels. Identify and develop new business opportunities to expand market presence and drive revenue growth.
Identify market opportunities, define sales targets, monitor sales performance, and support the achievement of revenue and profitability objectives.
Negotiate annual commercial agreements, including trade terms and promotional plans, to strengthen strategic partnerships and optimize sales performance.
Collaborate closely with cross-functional teams (e.g., GTM, MTK, Retail) to support product launches, promotional campaigns, and other sales initiatives, while enhancing brand consistency across consumer touchpoints.
Work with logistics and relevant internal teams to support timely product delivery, inventory management, and the resolution of operational issues affecting channel partners.
Requirements:
Bachelor's degree or above.
At least 5 years of experience in B2C sales; experience in a large or multinational organization is preferred.
Experience in channel and/or operator sales is an advantage.
Good understanding of B2C sales processes.
Knowledge of project management principles and processes.
Strong negotiation, communication, and interpersonal skills.
Excellent written and spoken English.
Proactive, results-oriented, and able to work effectively in a fast-paced environment.
What We Offer:
We offer you an exciting professional career in one of the leading and fastest growing multinational telecommunication companies, challenging work and a competitive salary package. Personal development is ensured through many training opportunities across Europe.
A dynamic, fast-paced, and multicultural environment within one of the world’s leading and rapidly growing telecommunications companies.
Meaningful, hands-on work where your ideas and contributions have real impact.
Career growth opportunities with global training, learning support, and access to world-class conferences.
Competitive salary and benefits package, including a transparent performance-based bonus scheme.
Access to major industry events, product exhibitions, and high-level summits, providing exposure and networking opportunities.
Collaboration with international teams, enabling the building of a global professional network.
Modern office in a convenient location, free company parking, employee discounts on company products, and all necessary tools to perform your role efficiently.
Various team-building activities and employee integration events.
We are committed to creating an inclusive and diverse workplace. We welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of gender, nationality, ethnic or social origin, religion or belief, disability, age, or sexual orientation.

jd_text:

Engineering Manager (m/f/x)
KONUX
Administration
Munich, Germany
Posted on Jul 13, 2026
Apply now
About Us
KONUX is a dynamic and fast-growing technology company that is transforming the way the industry works. Our AI-powered solutions empower our customers to make smarter, data-driven decisions, and our culture is fueled by grit, ingenuity, and credibility - we believe that these values are essential to unlocking the potential of technology and making a real impact. At KONUX, we are passionate about driving innovation in the fields of IoT and AI, and we are looking for like-minded individuals to join our team.
Job Description
We are seeking an Engineering Manager to join our Data and Cloud Platform team. This team plays a central enabling role at KONUX: it provides the data and cloud foundations used by our product engineering teams, while also supporting Revenue, Operations, and other business functions in accessing, analysing, and acting on data.
You will lead and grow a high-performing cross-functional team owning a broad platform scope, including:
Data processing frameworks and scalable data platform capabilities
Cloud infrastructure, cost management, and observability
Platform security concepts, tenant configuration, and device management
Process automation, CI/CD pipelines, and developer enablement
In this role, you will set clear priorities, align technical strategy with company goals, and help the team make sound trade-offs across reliability, scalability, security, data quality, cost, and delivery speed. You will combine strong people leadership with hands-on technical judgement: you should be able to assess technical designs, understand implementation risks, guide engineers towards pragmatic solutions, and contribute directly to the codebase or technical delivery where needed. You will also act as an important communication bridge between Engineering, Product, Revenue, Operations, and leadership, ensuring complex technical topics are clearly understood by both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Your Impact
Team Leadership
Effectively lead your cross-functional team consisting of 8 Fullstack, DevOps, and Data Engineers, as well as Technical Service Engineers, fostering collaboration, growth and development
Conduct regular 1:1 meetings to provide coaching and support individual career development
Manage performance reviews and pay adjustments according to the company framework
Support hiring, onboarding, feedback, conflict resolution, and performance management
Technical Delivery
Own the architecture and technical strategy for the Data and Cloud Platform and collaborate with key stakeholders to align with the product roadmap and the needs of Revenue and Operations
Translate company and technical goals into actionable plans for your team and ensure clear priorities and focus
Treat the platform as a product: understand internal users, prioritise high-leverage improvements, and balance reliability, scalability, security, cost, and delivery speed
Contribute directly to technical delivery where needed, leading by example on data and software engineering, cloud infrastructure, and automation of operational processes
Assess technical designs hands-on, identify risks and trade-offs, and guide engineers towards pragmatic, maintainable solutions
Communicate technical decisions, trade-offs, risks, priorities, and delivery constraints clearly to technical and non-technical stakeholders
Financial Management
Own the annual team budget, managing both staff and operational expenses
Control and optimise costs across pre-production and production environments
To be successful in this role, you’ll need to have
Software, DevOps, Data Engineering, or other STEM background, with a minimum of 8+ years of experience and 2+ years in an engineering leadership role, ideally incl. people-management
Strong people leadership skills, including coaching, conflict resolution, hiring, per

title: Product Manager, Marketing (parental cover)
jd_text:
KAYAK/momondo, part of Booking Holdings (NASDAQ: BKNG), is a leading travel search engine. With billions of queries across our platforms, we help people find their perfect flight, stay, rental car and vacation package. We’re also transforming business travel with a new corporate travel solution, KAYAK for Business.
As an employee of KAYAK, you will be part of a travel company that operates a portfolio of global metasearch brands including momondo, Cheapflights and HotelsCombined, among others. From start-up to industry leader, innovation is in our DNA and every employee has an opportunity to make their mark. Our focus is on building the best travel search engine to make it easier for everyone to experience the world.
KAYAK is seeking a
Product Manager, Marketing
to own and optimize our SEM Marketing Landing Pages—the critical first touchpoint for millions of travelers finding their perfect trip through search marketing. In this
fixed-term role
(
12-18 months
), you will bridge marketing performance with user experience, driving measurable improvements through rigorous experimentation and data-driven optimization.
As Product Manager for SEM Landing Pages, you will lead a cross-functional team including Engineering, Design, SEO, and Performance Marketing to develop, test, and iterate on landing page experiences that convert. You will own the hypothesis-to-insight pipeline: identifying opportunities, designing A/B tests, analyzing results, and translating findings into actionable product improvements. This role requires both strategic thinking and hands-on execution, as you'll need to balance long-term vision with the discipline of rapid testing and learning.
This position requires you to commute to our Berlin office 3 times a week.
In This Role, You Will
Own the SEM landing page product roadmap, prioritizing features and experiments based on conversion impact, user experience, and alignment with marketing objectives
Design and execute a continuous testing program, developing hypotheses, running A/B and multivariate tests, and driving statistically rigorous analysis to inform product decisions
Lead cross-functional collaboration with Engineering (who build the pages), SEO (for organic/paid alignment), Design (for user experience), and Performance Marketing (for campaign strategy and goals)
Manage the full product lifecycle for landing page features, from initial concept and user research through technical requirements, sprint planning, launch, and post-launch measurement
Analyze performance data using SQL, analytics tools, and testing platforms to identify friction points, growth opportunities, and winning variations
Champion the user perspective while balancing business goals, ensuring landing pages deliver value to travelers while meeting marketing efficiency targets
Communicate insights and progress to product leadership and marketing stakeholders, translating test results into strategic recommendations
Drive continuous improvement through iterative testing, performance monitoring, and a relentless focus on conversion rate optimization
Please Apply If You Have
Have 3-5+ years of hands-on experience in product management, preferably in a B2C tech, e-commerce, or travel company.
Possess a proven ability to independently manage and ship product features in an agile environment.
Have demonstrated experience owning product features from concept to launch, including managing a backlog and working with engineering squads.
Maintain strong analytical skills, with experience in A/B testing and data analysis to drive strategic decisions.
Have excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to build consensus across technical and non-technical teams.
Bring a strong sense of user empathy and a passion for building products that solve real-world traveler problems.
Hold a Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Business, or a related field, or have equivalent hands-on professional experience.
Thrive in a high-ownership envi

title: Product Manager PIM (m/w/d)
jd_text:
Als 1966 gegründetes und weltweit marktführendes Familienunternehmen entwickeln und produzieren wir innovative Systemlösungen für Wand- und Bodenbeläge in höchster Qualität. Von Iserlohn aus vermarkten wir unsere Produkte sehr erfolgreich und wachsen weltweit kontinuierlich. Dabei bauen wir seit jeher auf langfristige Partnerschaften mit unseren Kunden, Lieferanten und natürlich mit unseren Mitarbeitenden. Die Arbeit bei Schlüter-Systems ist geprägt durch die gesunde Mischung aus Innovation und Tradition in einem modernen Arbeitsumfeld. Wir vertrauen auf die Begeisterungsfähigkeit, Professionalität und Eigenverantwortlichkeit unserer Mitarbeitenden und stärken diese durch intensiven Wissens- und Informationsaustausch sowie Aus- und Weiterbildungsangebote. Werden auch Sie Teil der Schlüter-Familie!
Sie haben Freude daran, unsere PIM-Systeme auf das nächste Level zu bringen? Dann schreiben Sie mit an der Schlüter-Erfolgsstory als
Product Manager PIM (m/w/d)
Ihre Aufgaben:
Entwickeln und Implementieren einer unternehmensweiten PIM-Vision auf Basis einer wertebasierten Produkt-Roadmap in Zusammenarbeit mit dem Produktmanagement, IT und externen Partnern
Vorantreiben und Umsetzen der PIM-Strategie auf Basis von Kundenanforderungen und User-Stories unter Berücksichtigung von Datenqualität und Governance-Richtlinien
Definieren von Nutzergruppen, Richtlinien, Prozessen und Workflows sowie kontinuierliches Weiterentwickeln des PIM-Systems in Zusammenarbeit mit der IT
Festlegen von Taxonomie, Attributen, Hierarchien und Werten sowie Integrieren des PIM-Systems in das technologische Ecosystem (z. B. ERP, CRM, DAM, WMS)
Erfassen und Auswerten von Kennzahlen und Metriken in Bezug auf die Datenqualität
Durchführen von Trainings für unterschiedliche Stakeholder-Gruppen und kontinuierliches Einholen von Feedback der internen Kunden
Ihr Profil:
Abgeschlossenes Studium im Bereich Wirtschaftswissenschaften, Marketing oder eine vergleichbare Qualifikation in Verbindung mit einer mehrjährigen Berufserfahrung in einer vergleichbaren Rolle
Erfahrungen im Umfeld Product Information Management sowie im Umgang mit PIM-Systemen und den zugehörigen Schnittstellen wie z. B. ERP-, CRM- und E-Commerce-Systeme
Analytische Arbeitsweise sowie ausgeprägte kommunikative Fähigkeiten für eine wirkungsvolle Kommunikation mit technischen und nicht-technischen Stakeholdergruppen
Begeisterungsfähigkeit für neue und innovative Technologien und Best Practices in Verbindung mit Sozialkompetenz, Teamfähigkeit und Verantwortungsbewusstsein auf globaler Ebene
Ausgeprägte Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift für die Arbeit in internationalen Projekten
Gelegentliche Reisebereitschaft

Einen zukunftsorientierten Arbeitsplatz

title: Senior Product Manager (m/w/d)
jd_text:
Komm zu uns als Senior Product Manager (d/w/m) bei Candis und gestalte die Zukunft unserer ERP-Integrationen.
Wenn du Lust hast, einen komplexen Produktbereich zu verantworten, eng mit Kund:innen und Partnern zusammenzuarbeiten und etwas aufzubauen, das direkt geschäftlichen Impact erzeugt, könnte diese Rolle perfekt für dich sein. Es ist deine Chance, einen wachsenden Bereich bei Candis zu übernehmen und ihn zu einem erfolgreichen Produktbereich zu entwickeln.
Warum brauchen wir diese Rolle?
Wir haben erste Integrationen mit ERP-Systemen wie Business Central, SAP und Oracle NetSuite aufgebaut und eine frühe Product-Market-Fit erreicht. Jetzt geht es darum, diese zu skalieren, zu stabilisieren und weiterzuentwickeln, da immer mehr Kund:innen sie nutzen.
Gleichzeitig wächst das Feedback, neue Anforderungen entstehen und die Komplexität von ERP-Systemen muss in klare Produktentscheidungen übersetzt werden. Da wir ein API-getriebenes Produkt bauen, bedeutet das auch: schnell, präzise und zuverlässig liefern.
Du übernimmst die Verantwortung für diesen Bereich, arbeitest eng mit Kund:innen und Partnern zusammen und stellst sicher, dass wir die richtigen Dinge bauen und gut umsetzen. Dein Ziel ist es, aus frühem Traction einen strukturierten, skalierbaren Produktbereich zu machen, der über alle Integrationen hinweg konsistenten Impact erzeugt.
Warum ist diese Rolle spannend?
Diese Rolle bietet die Möglichkeit, einen Produktbereich zu gestalten, der sich bei Candis aktiv weiterentwickelt. Du arbeitest in einem Bereich, in dem es viel zu lernen gibt, sowie viel Raum für Verbesserung und Innovation. Durch die enge Zusammenarbeit mit Kund:innen und Partnern gewinnst du tiefe Einblicke in die Praxis von ERP-Systemen und beeinflusst direkt, wie diese Systeme in moderne Finance-Workflows integriert werden.
Mit bevorstehenden Entwicklungen wie der Pflicht zur E-Rechnung befindet sich das ERP-Ökosystem in einer Phase des grundlegenden Wandels. Das schafft die Möglichkeit, nicht nur bestehende Integrationen zu verbessern, sondern auch neu zu denken, wie diese Systeme in Zukunft funktionieren sollten. Du kannst dich als Expert:in in diesem Bereich positionieren, zum Thought Leadership beitragen (z. B. durch Webinare) und eine Schlüsselrolle dabei spielen, wie Candis das ERP-Angebot aufbaut und skaliert.
Was sind die größten Herausforderungen?
Du wirst eng mit internen und externen Stakeholdern zusammenarbeiten, wobei Fortschritt eine starke Abstimmung (insbesondere mit externen Stakeholdern) erfordert. Viele Features können nicht eigenständig gebaut werden, da sie die Koordination mit Partnern erfordern, die nach ihren eigenen Zeitplänen und innerhalb ihrer eigenen Systemgrenzen arbeiten.
Zudem sind ERP-Systeme oft komplex und nicht immer nach modernen Produktprinzipien gestaltet. Das bedeutet, du musst Legacy-Strukturen navigieren und gleichzeitig eine reibungslose und skalierbare User Experience liefern. Diese Anforderungen mit Geschwindigkeit, Klarheit und Produktqualität in Einklang zu bringen, wird eine zentrale Herausforderung in dieser Rolle sein.
Was möchten wir nach 12 Monaten erreicht haben?
Nach 12 Monaten sollen unsere ERP-Integrationen ein stabiler und zuverlässiger Teil unseres Produktangebots sein, der Wachstum unterstützt, ohne die Churn-Rate zu erhöhen. Wir erwarten, dass Erkenntnisse aus einzelnen Integrationen erfolgreich auf andere Systeme übertragen wurden, sodass wir konsistentere und skalierbarere Lösungen statt isolierter Features bauen können.
Schlüsselfunktionalitäten wie das 3-Way-Matching sollen in enger Zusammenarbeit mit unseren Partnern, beispielsweise für Sage 100 und Business Central, erfolgreich ausgerollt werden. Insgesamt soll die ERP-Domain klar strukturiert, kontinuierlich verbessert und im gesamten Unternehmen gut verstanden sein, mit dir als zentraler Anlaufstelle für alle relevanten Themen.
Was dich am besten für die Rolle ausstattet:
Fließendes Deutsch (C2) und Englisch.
Wohnsitz in Berlin (hybrides Ar

The goal is to scale, stabilize, and develop these integrations after achieving early Product-Market-Fit. The PM will navigate complex legacy structures while delivering smooth user experiences.

title: Staff Product Manager
jd_text:
P-1345
You will be the Product Manager for the Databricks Runtime team in
Berlin
. The team works at the intersection of query processing, storage, infrastructure and data governance, all key components of the Databricks data intelligence platform. As the Product Manager, you will develop a deep understanding of modern cloud infrastructure, data governance solutions, and open source components, and gather requirements from customers. You will launch features that will impact how customers process petabytes of data every day and make it simpler for them to draw value from their data.
This role requires coordinating all product activities from vision to implementation, including engaging customers to understand their needs, developing long-term product strategy, defining product roadmaps, working with engineering to build those products, and coordinating with various internal stakeholders (both pre- and post-launch) to ensure product success.
The Impact You Will Have
Own the end-to-end product management process
Gather customer requirements, design features and collaborate with engineering to deliver a polished market-ready product
Drive launches with product marketing through blog posts, release notes, webinars
Define and measure OKRs for your products
Drive the prioritization and development of features, measurably making it simpler and more cost effective for customers to govern and process petabytes of data.
What We Look For
6+ yrs of experience
Bachelors degree in Computer Science or similar field
Experience in Product Management (inbound, outbound, cross-functional processes)
Technical skills
Solid understanding of cloud infrastructure (AWS, Azure, GCP)
Strong data analysis and operationalization skills (SQL, rollups, building operational dashboards)
About Databricks
Databricks is the data and AI company. More than 10,000 organizations worldwide — including Comcast, Condé Nast, Grammarly, and over 50% of the Fortune 500 — rely on the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform to unify and democratize data, analytics and AI. Databricks is headquartered in San Francisco, with offices around the globe and was founded by the original creators of Lakehouse, Apache Spark™, Delta Lake and MLflow. To learn more, follow Databricks on Twitter, LinkedIn and Facebook.
Benefits
At Databricks, we strive to provide comprehensive benefits and perks that meet the needs of all of our employees. For specific details on the benefits offered in your region click here.
Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion
At Databricks, we are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive culture where everyone can excel. We take great care to ensure that our hiring practices are inclusive and meet equal employment opportunity standards. Individuals looking for employment at Databricks are considered without regard to age, color, disability, ethnicity, family or marital status, gender identity or expression, language, national origin, physical and mental ability, political affiliation, race, religion, sexual orientation, socio-economic status, veteran status, and other protected characteristics.
Compliance
If access to export-controlled technology or source code is required for performance of job duties, it is within Employer's discretion whether to apply for a U.S. government license for such positions, and Employer may decline to proceed with an applicant on this basis alone.

It requires a deep understanding of cloud infrastructure and open-source components.

title: Senior Product Manager
jd_text:
Shine is the financial copilot for entrepreneurs and small business owners.
Founded by serial entrepreneurs Rico Andersen and Martin Hegelund, Shine is a leading European fintech unicorn on a mission to restore the joy of running a business, by ending wasted time on financial admin. Shine offers a connected solution for invoicing, accounting, payroll, business accounts, payments, and financing, meaning business owners can focus their energy on growing a healthy business, not held back by manual admin.
Part of something bigger
Today we're part of Cegid, a European leader in cloud software for finance and accounting. Together we're building Europe's leading financial copilot for small businesses and their accountants.
Shine already supports more than 400,000 small businesses. As part of Cegid, we now reach over one million small businesses and 15,000 accountants across Europe.
We're a multicultural team working from France, Germany, Denmark and the Netherlands, contributing to a wider European network that spans Spain, Portugal and Belgium.
Your Hiring Experience Matters
Just as we respect our customers' time, we respect yours. Your experience with Shine and Cegid should feel simple, transparent and genuinely supportive.
If this sounds like somewhere you want to grow, we'd love to hear from you.
The Advisor Collaboration team at Shine
The Advisor Collaboration team builds the workspace where accountants manage and collaborate with their clients across the entire accounting life-cycle — a shared, structured space for documents, client management, and communication — instead of the patchwork of disconnected tools they juggle today. As AI reshapes accounting, our mission is to cut context switching and back-and-forth, reduce the compliance risk that comes from fragmented channels, and make Shine the single place where advisors and their clients work together. It's a core, early-stage product area with real distribution from day one: through Cegid we reach over a million small businesses and 15,000 accountants across Europe, so the challenge is building the right product, not finding users.
Your role as a Senior Product Manager
As Senior Product Manager for Advisor Collaboration, you own the multi-quarter strategy and roadmap for this workspace. You translate market signals and input from accountants into a clear, prioritized roadmap and trade-offs, drive delivery with engineering, design and data science, and own outcomes against our OKRs. You'll go deep on a complex domain of accounting, without needing to be an expert on day one, working closely with a distributed, multicultural team to build products that advisors and their clients love.
Your Responsibilities Will Include
Own the multi-quarter strategy and roadmap for the Advisor Collaboration workspace, aligned to company OKRs
Run discovery with accountants and business owners (along with the designer) to do interviews, synthesis, briefs, success metrics and MVP definitions
Translate qualitative and quantitative signals into a prioritized roadmap and clear trade-offs
Drive delivery with engineering, design and data science, breaking large initiatives into incremental, shippable pieces
Define instrumentation and use cohort and funnel analysis to find opportunities and measure impact on adoption, retention and engagement
Align senior stakeholders and coordinate with adjacent teams
Leverage AI where it creates real value for advisors and their clients
📍 Job located in
Berlin, Germany
with possibility of two remote working days per week.
About You
5+ years of product management experience
Fluent in French and English
Owns multi-quarter product strategy and aligns it to company OKRs
Strong discovery craft: research plans, user interviews, synthesis, MVP definitions, prototypes and experiments
Data fluency: defines instrumentation, builds dashboards, uses cohort and funnel analysis to find non-obvious opportunities
Proven delivery: breaks large initiatives into incremental releases,
summary: This position leads the Advisor Collaboration team to build a workspace for accountants to manage and collaborate with clients. title: Staff Product Manager - Mobile - 100% Remote - EMEA
jd_text:
Job Description
NOTE: This is a FULLY remote role, but the candidate must be within EMEA to collaborate with their team, peers, and internal customers. You do not have to be in the specific country or city shown in this listing, but please only apply if you are physically based within EMEA.
Trusted by 20,000+ property managers worldwide, Hostaway is an industry leading, AI-powered vacation rental management platform designed for professional short-term rental operators. Hostaway brings everything together for greater efficiency and faster growth.
Profitable, high-growth, and the first short-term rental Property Management System unicorn 🦄. Join us at our most exciting stage yet! Learn more about our recent valuation and story here .
About The Role
We are seeking an exceptionally user-obsessed, mobile-native, and execution-driven Staff Product Manager to own the strategy and delivery of the Hostaway Mobile App. This is the product our customers reach for in the field, between cleanings, while resolving guest issues, or on the move between properties.
In this role, you will define the vision, roadmap, and execution for a mobile experience serving multiple personas (property managers, owners, and cleaners), each with distinct workflows. You will work closely with Engineering, Design, Research, and Go-to-Market (GTM) teams to deliver a focused, high-quality, mobile-first product that drives adoption, retention, and daily active usage.
What You Will Do
Become the definitive owner of the Hostaway mobile app within your first few weeks, setting its vision, strategy, and roadmap in step with our Domain product teams.
Run continuous discovery as your default operating mode, spending real time with app users and turning what you learn into clear north stars, opportunities, and scoped requirements.
Prototype your way to conviction: use AI tools to turn ideas into working prototypes you can put in front of users and engineers, and don't be afraid to ship code when it's the fastest path to learning.
Set the app's north-star and supporting metrics, find the drivers that genuinely move them, and ensure instrumentation ships with every feature.
Own adoption, engagement, and retention as outcomes you're accountable for, including identifying where AI can make a real difference for an on-the-go, multi-persona tool.
Partner closely with Product Design to deliver experiences that feel genuinely native on iOS and Android, fast, intuitive, and built for the field.
Drive GTM readiness for every release so Product Marketing, Sales Enablement, CS, and Support are equipped before launch.
Job Requirements
7+ years of experience in product management, with a strong background in B2B SaaS platforms and demonstrated focus on mobile applications.
Proven track record of successfully launching and iterating on mobile products on iOS and Android, including ownership of App Store and Play Store release cycles.
You build with AI as part of your daily workflow, comfortable going from idea to clickable or working prototype with AI coding tools, and excited by the idea of a PM who ships, not just specs.
Demonstrated success building and scaling B2B SaaS products with multiple user personas and complex workflows (e.g. Admins, Operators, Field Staff, Service Providers).
Proven ability to deeply understand and prioritize the needs of diverse user groups, translating insights into impactful product features — with first-hand experience in customer research and field interviews.
Track record of leading cross-functional teams through the full product lifecycle, from discovery to launch and iteration, and of operating with autonomy on both assigned and self-identified problems.
A proactive, problem-solving mindset, comfortable creating clarity in ambiguous, fast-moving environments.
Passion for user-centric design and delivering intuitive, high-value experiences — with a particular love for mobile-first products. Experience in the vacation rental, hospitality, or property
summary: This role involves owning the strategy and delivery of the Hostaway Mobile App, serving property managers, owners, and cleaners. The PM will run continuous discovery, prototype with AI tools, and ensure mobile-first experiences drive adoption and retention. It requires strong cross-functional leadership and deep user research.

title: Founding Product Manager (Y Combinator Startup)
jd_text:
Wir sind
CoCrafter (YC W24)
, ein schnell wachsendes Münchner Startup mit der Mission, die Bauindustrie grundlegend zu revolutionieren.
Die Bauwirtschaft ist das Rückgrat der deutschen und europäischen Wirtschaft – und dennoch wurde sie von der Digitalisierung weitgehend übersehen. Das Ergebnis?
Ineffiziente Prozesse
und
viel ungenutztes Potenzial.
CoCrafter baut das Operating System (OS) für Subcontractor Management und zählt deutschlandweit bereits zahlreiche mittelständische Bau- und Handwerksbetriebe zu seinen Kunden.
Wir sind mit
1.8m€
von
Y Combinator, 10x Founders und weitere Top-Investoren
finanziert – und sind damit bereit für den nächsten großen Wachstumsschritt.
Werde Teil unseres Teams und gestalte mit uns die Zukunft der Bauindustrie! 🚀
Aufgaben
Wir suchen Dich für als unseren ersten
Product Manager #1
– Du solltest schnell lernen können und den Herausforderungen eines Startups gewachsen sein. Du planst und entwickelst aus Kundengesprächen ein Produkt, das unsere Kunden voranbringt. Du wirst eigene Features end-to-end denken, designen und entwickeln, und dabei eng mit unserem Tech-Team zusammenarbeiten. Du bist in CoCrafters kreativstem Team und viel mit Design, konzeptueller Umsetzung und Anwendungsgesprächen mit unseren Usern in Berührung. Du arbeitest direkt mit unserem Co-Founder und CPO Philipp zusammen.
Verantwortlichkeiten
Product Ownership**:** Du bekommst eine end-to-end Verwantwortlichkeit für eigene Product-Streams.
Customer Discovery: Direkter Kundenkontakt im Zusammenspiel mit unserem Customer Sucess. Du wirst eigene Kunden auch in der Implementierung von Features leaden.
Project Management: Da einige Features von uns sehr komplex sind, ist ein gutes Projektmanagement unabdingbar.
Qualifikation
Idealerweise praktische Erfahrungen im Product Management.
Gutes technisches Verständnis (Software Engineering ist aber nicht notwendig).
Von Vorteil: Kenntnisse oder Erfahrung in der Handwerks- und/oder Baubranche.
Proaktive Arbeitsweise und die Fähigkeit, eigenständig Initiative zu ergreifen.
Freude an der Arbeit in einem schnelllebigen und dynamischen Umfeld.
Fließende Deutschkenntnisse auf Muttersprachenniveau (oder vergleichbar).
Start:
Ab sofort.
Benefits
🦸‍♀️
Ein erstklassiges Netzwerk
aus Gründern, Investoren und Mentoren für deine persönliche und berufliche Weiterentwicklung
💸
Attraktive Vergütung
mit unbegrenzter, erfolgsbasierter, variabler Vergütung inklusive
Unternehmensanteilen (ESOP)
– du profitierst von unserem Erfolg
🏖️
28 Tage Urlaub
pro Jahr für deine Erholung
🏋️‍♂️
Fitness-Zuschuss
, damit du dich auch außerhalb des Büros auspowern oder entspannen kannst 😉
⛰️
Team-Events
wie Bowling, Minigolf, Offsites und regelmäßige Feedback-Sessions
☕️
Ein wunderschönes Büro
im Herzen Münchens (Maxvorstadt)
🚀
Warum du Teil von CoCrafter werden solltest
🚀
🔥
Impact:
In weniger als einem Jahr haben wir ein Produkt geschaffen, das essentiell für unsere Kunden ist. Mit deiner Arbeit wirst du eine gesamte Branche neu definieren!
📈
Wachstum:
Der Einstieg in ein Start-up in der frühen Phase ist eine einmalige Gelegenheit. Werde Teil eines schnell wachsenden Teams und übernimm eine Schlüsselrolle in dieser Entwicklung!
🤝
Team:
Wir sind ein
interdisziplinäres, dynamisches und unterstützendes Team
, das mit Leidenschaft etwas Außergewöhnliches aufbaut. Werde Teil von CoCrafter und entwickle dich
sowohl persönlich als auch beruflich
weiter!
🚀
Let’s build the future of construction together!

title: IT Product Manager - Manufacturing
jd_text:
A Snapshot of Your Day.
Join us in revolutionizing manufacturing at Siemens Energy’s Wind business! As the IT Product Manager – Manufacturing, you will take charge of one or more IT products that drive our plants, including MES templates, shop-floor integrations, and quality/traceability capabilities. You will connect real shop-floor needs with secure IT solutions, guiding the vision, roadmap, and value throughout the product lifecycle. Imagine leading the integration of our manufacturing stack, which combines MES with ERP/PLM and real-time data from SCADA/PLC, to enhance execution and transparency in performance.
How You’ll Make An Impact
You will lead end-to-end design across applications, data, interfaces, and services, ensuring adherence to enterprise architecture and cybersecurity policies.
Define and communicate the product vision, features, and release plans while managing upgrades and aligning with plant rollout schedules.
Collaborate with business/process owners to intake, scope, and prioritize demands, balancing immediate operational needs with long-term standardization.
Own the OPEX/CAPEX for your products, support annual planning, and track ROI and KPIs to drive continuous improvement.
Orchestrate MES ↔ ERP (e.g., S/4HANA) ↔ PLM integrations and coordinate testing and hypercare with plants to ensure seamless operations.
What You Bring
You have over 5 years of experience in IT Product Management or a similar role, delivering manufacturing IT solutions at scale, such as multi-site MES rollouts.
Your fluency in the manufacturing stack includes MES (preferably SAP Digital Manufacturing), ERP (SAP S/4HANA), and PLM interfaces, along with familiarity with SCADA/PLC data flows.
You are skilled in enterprise alignment, working with architecture governance, ITSM/CMDB, and security/compliance workflows.
Your strong communication skills enable you to engage confidently with stakeholders from shop-floor teams to senior leaders, guiding external partners to successful outcomes.
You hold a degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Industrial Engineering, or a related field, or possess equivalent practical experience.
You have excellent English proficiency, and knowledge of Spanish, German or Danish is a plus.
About The Team
In the Digital Services Engineering & Manufacturing team, you will collaborate with a diverse group of professionals dedicated to enhancing IT services within Siemens Energy’s Wind business. This team is focused on driving innovation and efficiency through the integration of advanced IT solutions. Together, you will work on optimizing and implementing robust IT solutions that meet the real needs of the shop floor, ensuring seamless operations and compliance with industry standards. Your contributions will play a vital role in shaping the future of manufacturing, as you partner with various stakeholders to deliver measurable outcomes and drive continuous improvement across our plants.
Who is Siemens Energy?
At Siemens Energy, we are more than just an energy technology company. With over 100,000 dedicated employees in more than 90 countries, we develop the energy systems of the future, ensuring that the growing energy demand of the global community is met reliably and sustainably. The technologies created in our research departments and factories drive the energy transition and provide the base for one-sixth of the world’s electricity generation.
Our global team is committed to making sustainable, reliable, and affordable energy a reality by pushing the boundaries of what is possible. We uphold a 150-year legacy of innovation that encourages our search for people who will support our focus on decarbonization, new technologies, and energy transformation.
Find out how you can make a difference at Siemens Energy: https://www.siemensenergy.com/employeevideo
Our Commitment to Diversity
Lucky for us, we are not all the same. Through diversity, we generate power. We run on inclusion, and our combined creative energy is

title: Product Manager - Intelligent Lubrication Solutions
jd_text:
Are you passionate about shaping the future of lubrication through new technologies, connectivity, and intelligent solutions? Do you enjoy identifying and solving real customer problems using data, connectivity, and AI to create tangible business value ?
Then you could be our next...
Product Manager –
Intelligent Lubrication Solutions
We are looking for a forward-looking Product Manager to drive the development of Intelligent Lubrication Solutions. Building on SKF’s established position in automatic lubrication, you will move innovation initiatives forward, focused on creating measurable value for customers, and shape how new technologies evolve automatic lubrication towards intelligent, condition-driven and connected systems.
Key Responsibilities
In this role, you will direct the journey from problem -> solution -> value realization, shaping the technical offering with Product Development to define go-to-market strategies for Intelligent Lubrication Solutions. Furthermore, you will:
Contribute in defining the product roadmap for Innovation & Connected Solutions, ensuring emerging technologies are leveraged to deliver impactful and differentiated lubrication solutions.
Work with Sales and customers to identify opportunities to create customer value through connectivity and AI, and translate these into scalable product and service solutions.
Identify customer challenges, translate them into scalable products and services, and bring solutions to market that deliver measurable business value.
Provide Technology- and Market insights by researching and assessing competitors, market trends, and emerging technologies in lubrication, connectivity, sensing technologies, and applied AI/ML.
Conduct customer value validation by piloting activities for the new Intelligent Lubrication solutions to validate customer value and technical feasibility.
Build strong internal and external networks to combine capabilities and develop integrated solutions to identified customer needs, and evaluate external partners, technology providers, and collaboration opportunities to accelerate innovation.
Key
Requirements
A university degree in Engineering, Product Management, Business, or a related field, with previous work experience in product management, innovation management, or business development.
Demonstrated ability to collaborate and manage stakeholders, with experience working in early-stage or uncertain innovation settings and alongside cross-functional teams across regions.
Experience with connected products, digital solutions, AI/ML, sensing technologies, data-driven services, and related applications in industrial settings.
Experience working with external partners, research institutions, start-ups, or technology providers.
It’s a plus if you also bring knowledge of lubrication systems, industrial maintenance, reliability engineering, or condition monitoring
As this is a global role, fluency in English and solid communication skills are required.
You will enjoy working here if you are
curious about emerging technologies, energized by turning ideas into product opportunities, and motivated by solving real customer problems through innovation.
Our offer
A role with strategic visibility and exposure at the forefront of innovation in lubrication and connected industrial solutions, with the opportunity to shape a new growth area with real business and customer impact.
Close collaboration with product management, innovation, sales and service teams, in a supportive environment that values collaboration, experimentation, and results.
A platform to explore how new technologies and connectivity can transform industrial maintenance and bring true customer value.
What You’ll Love About SKF…
A culture of diversity and inclusion!
SKF is committed to creating a diverse environment, and we firmly believe that a diverse workforce is essential for our continued success. Therefore, we only focus on your experience, skills, and potential. Come as you are – just b

title: Product Manager (Mönchengladbach, DE)
jd_text:
We are looking for you as Product Manager (m/f/d) System Solutions Focus Hardware to support product management at our subsidiary Scheidt & Bachmann Parking Solutions GmbH in Mönchengladbach.
Scheidt & Bachmann - Mobility that connects
Whether you're taking the train, parking, refueling or charging, or using public transport – you've most likely already used our technology. For over 150 years, we've been developing innovative and sustainable systems for a mobility infrastructure that gets people and goods to their destinations safely, quickly, and comfortably worldwide. As a family business, we live by values such as trust, reliability, and solidarity – and together we're shaping the mobility of tomorrow.
The business area
Parking Solutions
Under the brand name "entervo," the company offers integrated, modular system solutions for parking management. Its own service network, numerous subsidiaries, and sales partners ensure optimal customer support worldwide. Tailor-made and flexible solutions appeal to a broad range of customers.
We offer
A permanent position in an innovative, crisis-proof and established company where diversity and equality are part of our DNA.
Work-life balance: Look forward to flexible, family-oriented working hours, home office and part-time options, and our childcare allowance.
Good food : Discover the diverse range of dishes in our modern company restaurant.
Structured onboarding and buddy program: We want you to feel comfortable from the start and be able to contribute your know-how directly.
Personnel development and training programs: Continuously develop yourself to provide your team with new impetus time and again.
We value our employees highly: Attractive compensation is just as important to us as a collegial and respectful working environment.
Your tasks
You are responsible for the entire life cycle of selected hardware and system products in the Parking Solutions sector – from product idea to product discontinuation.
You analyze customer requirements, market trends and technological developments and derive requirements for our product roadmap from them.
Together with us you will define and prioritize product requirements, working closely with our stakeholders and representing the product requirements to customers, partners and suppliers.
You manage product changes, component substitutions, and measures to ensure product quality and profitability.
Evaluate the technical and economic impacts of product decisions and support make-or-buy decisions.
Support us with product documentation, regulatory requirements, and lifecycle management.
This is what you bring with you
Successfully completed studies in the field of electrical engineering, mechanical engineering, industrial engineering or a comparable discipline
Several years of professional experience in product management, systems engineering or in a comparable technical function
Technical understanding of electronic, mechatronic, or software-based products
Experience in product lifecycle management
Fluent German and English skills, valid EU work permit
Analytical thinking and working methods, as well as team spirit
Enjoyment in further developing products in a dynamic international environment and setting new trends.
Please note:
This job description is translated from German.
Apply for this job
summary: This role involves managing the entire lifecycle of hardware and system products for parking solutions, from idea to discontinuation. It requires close coordination with stakeholders to ensure product quality and profitability.

title: Sr. Portfolio Manager, VWR Brand Equipment & Instruments
jd_text:
The Opportunity
Avantor is recruiting for a commercially astute Sr. Portfolio Manager, VWR Brand Equipment & Instruments EU.
Step into a high impact portfolio leadership role where strategy meets innovation. As a Portfolio Manager for VWR Branded (Private label) products, you will shape and execute the product vision for a large, private label brand distribution portfolio across Europe.
You’ll own the full product lifecycle—from concept and development through commercialization and phaseout—using market insight, customer needs, and data driven decision making to prioritize the right VWR Brand opportunities and fuel growth and increase market penetration for the VWR Brand.
In this role, you’ll lead large, cross functional initiatives, partner closely with commercial, marketing, sourcing, operations, e-commerce and regulatory teams, and drive new portfolio development that delivers differentiated value to customers and excellent financial results for VWR. You’ll influence leaders, collaborate with key internal and external partners, and play a critical role in strengthening VWR’s competitive position in a fastmoving, innovation driven market.
MAJOR JOB DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES (List in order of importance)
Develop & Execute Avantor’s VWR portfolio strategy to take market share including, yet not limited to, further penetration of existing products, new product introductions (proprietary ), new supplier acquisition, product line expansion, new market penetration, and expansion in existing customer markets.
Establish, track, and report financial and operational metrics related to assigned VWR Brand portfolio and identify risks and opportunities for improvement, leveraging the Avantor Business System and tools.
Develop strategic marketing programs for the VWR Brand portfolio by providing forward views of trends and emerging technologies for markets, as well as trends & developments in end customer segments.
Manage the product life cycle for product portfolio, including supplier onboarding, product additions & enrichment, sales training & support, price maintenance, & product life cycle management.
Drives collaboration with portfolio team and leadership to achieve revenue and profitability goals for portfolio.
Develops & enhances key strategic supplier relationships to improve competitive positioning.
Drive profitability of each of the product groups within the Product category and developing and executing strategies for improving that profitability.
Makes presentations to senior management regarding markets, competition, and customer segmentation for their portfolio.
Develops the VWR Brand strategy within the assigned portfolio category(ies) and works with the team to drive execution, including design, specifications, packaging, price points, parternering with Sourcing to negotiate costs, inventory investments, and returns.
Assesses the market of innovative technical developments by both suppliers and competitors and determining our customers’ requirements for these innovations.
Implements mix improvement strategies, improving penetration of VWR Brand portfolio to include changeover planning, discontinuance, super-cession, and communications internally and externally.
Attendance at industry trade shows to learn about recent product developments and new market entrants
Performs other duties as assigned.
QUALIFICATIONS (Education/Training, Experience And Certifications)
Intermediate experienced required
Bachelor’s degree required
Master’s degree in business, or equivalent experience preferred.
A minimum of three years of strategic marketing, product management, category management, sales or related experience required.
Safety certifications preferred but not required (ASP, CSP, or others)
KNOWLEDGE SKILLS AND ABILITIES (Those necessary to perform the job competently)
Financial skills – to assess sales, profits, inventory investment, payment terms, return on sales, return on capital employed and EBITDA to improve Avantor’s financia
summary: Portfolio Manager for VWR Branded products managing full product lifecycle from concept to phaseout across Europe. Leads large cross-functional initiatives for product line expansion and new market penetration. Strategically drives market share, supplier acquisition, profitability, and competitive positioning.

title: MEU Sr Process Engineer Amazon Logistics, Amazon Logistics
jd_text:
Description
Amazon opened its virtual doors in 1995 and strives to be the world’s most customer-centric company, where customers can find and discover anything they might want to buy online. The goal of Amazon Logistics is to build a world class last mile operation. Amazon Logistics aims to exceed the expectations of our customers by ensuring that their orders, no matter how large or small, are delivered as quickly, accurately, and cost effectively as possible.
The European Amazon Logistics team is seeking an experienced, innovative, hands on and customer obsessed Process Engineer to drive standardization, integrating the latest technologies in our Delivery Stations and proposing new innovation initiatives.
As a member of the EU Amazon Logistics Engineering team you will be responsible for designing the new Delivery Stations, whilst also supporting process optimization and new technologies integration in the legacy sites, improving safety, customer experience, quality and cost. You will act as a key point of contact for your area of responsibility in the country you support; frequently partnering with local Operations teams as well as other operational support functions.
This position requires deep customer focus, strong self-motivation and exceptional project management skills. It will involve regular communication with customers and project sponsors at both the European and country level, and requires you to be detail-oriented and comfortable partnering across functional business and technical teams. To be successful in this role, you need to pair strong analytical skills and a data-driven outlook with strong design and innovation operations intuition.
This role will involve frequent domestic travel and you will report into the Manager of the Design Engineering team of the country that you will be supporting.
Key job responsibilities
Drive new site designs and legacy site upgrade designs from planning to execution, including automation integration.
Lead and drive technology integration and standardization in EU-wide programs and products, owning all layout integrations and optimizations in the assigned Delivery Stations.
Contribute to network standardization via best practice sharing with EU counterparts.
Provide process expertise on engineering design defining material flow, conveyor positioning, equipment requirements and real estate implications from process requirements
Lead strategic initiatives and identify project opportunities to improve the processes and stations designs, engaging and influencing their key stakeholders.
A day in the life
You will lead end to end design for new delivery stations and work to improve existing delivery station processes. You will work with multiple internal and external stakeholders, some of the internal stakeholder include operations, design and construction, launch and expansion, and safety.
You will produce best in class layouts for new launches, optimize the existing stations and present to leadership for approval. You will work on automation program to integrate new technologies into legacy stations.
You will lead projects to create mechanisms to improve the stations and come up with innovative solutions to the complex design issues.
About The Team
What do we do?
Our team partners closely with AMZL Operations and support functions to identify, design and develop best-in-class process infrastructure and process layouts, and deliver them as EU AMZL network standards.
Mentorship & Career Growth
We have a broad mix of experience levels and Amazon tenures, and we celebrate knowledge sharing and mentorship. We also care about your career growth, so we assign projects based on what will help each team member to continue the development.
Inclusive Team Culture
Our team is intentional about building a climate where respect, equity and positive recognition of differences are cultivated as we think it is the best environment to foster innovation.
Basic Qualifications
Bachelor's degree or equi
summary: Senior Process Engineer for Amazon Logistics aiming to drive standardization, automation integration, and layout optimization in delivery stations. Involves process optimization, legacy site upgrading, and designing new delivery stations across the EU network. Focuses on cost, quality, and material flow improvements.
match_explanation: This is a warehouse operations and process engineering role, fundamentally diverging from software/hardware product management.

title: Manager PIPE (Principal Investors & Private Equity) (all genders) - BCG Platinion
jd_text:
Do you want to turn the challenges of digital transformation into tangible value creation and sustainable competitive advantages? Then join us in tackling critical IT and transaction issues for leading global companies and investors. Unlock our clients’ digital potential—from deal preparation to value creation—with tailored platforms, architectures, strategies, and organizational structures, and drive meaningful innovation.
As a
Manager - PIPE (Principal Investors & Private Equity) (all genders)
, you will work across the entire deal cycle of private equity investors—from the pre-deal phase to portfolio optimization and fund strategy. As part of an international team of tech specialists in EMESA, you will actively help shape technology-driven investment decisions for leading private equity investors.
Shape the world of tomorrow
You will lead and conduct tech due diligence and specifically assess the impact of technology on investment theses, risk profiles, and value potential
You will analyze application landscapes, software architectures, technical AI capabilities, product and tech organizations, and digital business models, and translate these into concrete deal implications You identify and quantify value-enhancement levers (e.g., AI strategies, scaling, platform strategies, tech transformation)
You develop concrete implementation plans and execute them together with our clients post-transaction (e.g., Full Potential Plan, carve-out, post-merger integration, etc.)
You work closely with C-level stakeholders (e.g., deal teams, operating partners, CFO, CTO, CIO) and act as a sparring partner on equal footing
You manage internal and external project teams and ensure fast, precise, and decision-oriented execution—often in highly dynamic transactions
You actively build and develop our PIPE Practice
You Make the Difference
You have successfully completed a degree in (Business) Information Technology, Business Administration, or a related field with above-average grades
You have at least six years of experience in IT consulting and in transactions with a strong IT focus, such as tech (vendor) due diligence, tech full potential planning, carve-outs, post-merger integration, etc.
You consistently think from an investor’s perspective and understand how technology influences enterprise value
You bring a strong, cross-industry understanding of business models, value drivers, and investment logic, and can clearly assess technology in terms of revenue, costs, speed, or scalability
You have experience leading projects, are skilled in efficient resource planning, and serve as a motivator and catalyst within your teams
You know how to present complex issues in an understandable way, calculate business cases, and drive them to a decision
You are fluent in German and English
How You Can Grow With Us
We guarantee you a constant stream of opportunities for growth in an open and supportive work environment. Every day, you’ll tackle challenges alongside a dedicated team of talented colleagues. Personalized professional development opportunities and an open feedback culture provide the perfect framework for discovering and honing your strengths. At work, our modern structures take the pressure off you. This includes hassle-free travel bookings, our excellent back-office team, and a comprehensive Knowledge Center. And with our regular Office Fridays and legendary events, we foster the strong and friendly network of our team, from which you’ll benefit even beyond your actual work.
Join our team
BCG Platinion is an Equal Opportunity Employer and is actively committed to diversity and inclusion. We consider all qualified applicants regardless of ethnic or social background, age, gender, sexual identity, religion, ideology, or disability. Apply now with your resume, a detailed list of projects, and all relevant certificates via our online portal, or feel free to contact Mike Stertz (+49 221 58958-555) if you have any questions.
More info at www.bcgplati
summary: Technology consultant role for private equity deals focusing on tech due diligence, IT architecture, and digital business models. Develops value-enhancement strategies such as AI, scaling, and tech transformation for C-level stakeholders and investors. Oversees post-merger integration and full potential planning.

title: E-commerce Executive (German Speaker)
jd_text:
About Our Brand
Neve Jewels Group is a UK-headquartered, digital-first jewellery company redefining modern luxury through made-to-order design, ethical craftsmanship, and global e-commerce. Our portfolio includes Diamonds Factory, Austen & Blake, and Sacet.
From the highest quality diamonds to unique gemstones and beyond, we offer an extensive range of incredible luxury jewellery pieces, specialising in engagement rings that make that ‘I Do’ moment one they’ll never forget.
Happy customers are at the heart of everything we do, which is why we offer around the clock real-human customer care. We’re always on hand to answer any queries or questions to make sure our customer satisfaction stays at an exceptional level, which can be seen clearly in our high calibre of outstanding reviews.
Known best for being one of the globes leading jewellers, our business is constantly growing and expanding and we’re always on the lookout for creative, highly skilled and motivated individuals to become a part of the team.
Our Values
💫 Celebrate the Moment – We honour life’s milestones by creating meaningful, joy-filled jewellery experiences.
💎 Customer-Centric Approach – Every decision begins with our customer, delivering personalised service at every step.
🤝 Earn Trust & Respect – We build lasting trust through honesty, care, and respect in all relationships.
✨ Experience-Led Innovation – We blend tradition with innovation to elevate every jewellery experience.
🌏 Empathy for People & Planet – We act with compassion, sourcing ethically and operating sustainably.
Role Overview
Reporting to the Global Ecommerce Manager, the Ecommerce Executive will play a key role in executing our online trading strategy, managing the German website, and analysing performance to drive growth. The role involves close collaboration with marketing, merchandising, design and DevOps teams to optimise site performance, enhance the customer journey and increase online sales. This position will cover all German-language sites, including Germany, Switzerland and Austria.
Key Responsibilities
Website Management
: Oversee daily operations of the German e-commerce site, ensuring accurate promotions, product listings, SEO and translations.
Trading:
Support the online trading plan to boost revenue and conversion.
Content Localisation:
Translate and adapt content, product descriptions, and campaigns into fluent German, ensuring accuracy in terminology, spelling, and currency, while meeting local compliance standards.
Sales & Performance Tracking:
Monitor KPIs (traffic, conversion, AOV, sales, etc.), preparing reports with actionable insights using GA4 and HotJar.
Marketing Coordination:
Partner with digital marketing teams to deliver campaigns, SEO initiatives, newsletters and paid media tailored to the German market.
Customer Journey Optimisation:
Identify and implement improvements to UX, checkout and overall customer experience.
Competitor Analysis:
Benchmark competitor activity to uncover opportunities for growth and differentiation.
Collaboration:
Partner with customer service teams to deliver a seamless experience for German-speaking customers and provide support for trade reviews in other regions as needed.
Your Profile
Fluent in German (native-level or equivalent) and English – both written and spoken.
Minimum of
3 years’ proven experience
in e-commerce management, preferably within fashion, jewellery, or luxury goods.
Strong understanding of German online consumer behaviour and digital trends.
Experience with CMS, e-commerce platforms (e.g., Shopify, Magento, or similar), and Google Analytics.
Basic knowledge of SEO, PPC, and digital marketing principles.
Excellent organisational skills with attention to detail and accuracy.
Analytical mindset with ability to interpret data and drive improvements
Flexible, with the ability to
travel once per month to our HQ office in London
.
Your Benefits
Annual Leave: 28 days holiday plus public holidays
Birthday Off: One additional pa
summary: E-commerce Executive managing the German-language websites for a modern luxury jewellery company. Coordinates website operations, content localization, online trading, and marketing campaigns to boost revenue. Focuses on UX optimization, sales tracking, and competitor analysis to improve the customer journey.

title: (Senior) Clinical Trial Manager (m/f/d)
jd_text:
Experience HELM as a unique family business and an attractive employer. Through a constant exchange of ideas, our ultimate goal is to offer our customers future-oriented solutions within our global network. At any time, in any place. This allows us to work proactively and think long-term - in operations, quality management, process optimization, product development and distribution. We are innovation drivers, possibility makers and a reliable partner, all in one.
(Senior) Clinical Trial Manager (m/f/d)
Date: Jul 5, 2026
Location:
Hamburg, DE, 20097
Business Unit: Pharma
Job Function: Product Development / Launch Management
Level of Experience: Mid-Career
At HELM Pharmaceuticals, based in the Headquarter in Hamburg, we develop generic pharmaceuticals.
To strengthen our team in the Finished Dosage Form Programs we are looking for a
Manager Clinical Development (m/f/d)
with a clinical life science background.
In this position, you combine scientific expertise with strategic thinking, you help shape a high‑quality development pipeline and contribute to strong regulatory outcomes. You will work closely with interdisciplinary teams and external partners to drive projects forward efficiently. This role offers you the opportunity to actively influence clinical development decisions and create real value for customers. Become part of a dynamic environment where your work has a real impact!
Your tasks:
You ensure the continuous proof of efficacy and safety for our developed products across all life‑cycle stages.
Furthermore, you also provide qualified responses to scientific and regulatory questions related to trial design, evidence requirements, and portfolio selection
In addition, you will represent our clinical development in interdisciplinary generic development teams with emphasis on clinical trials of medicinal products.
As Trial Manager, you plan, coordinate, and supervise clinical and/or pre‑clinical studies in line with regulatory guidelines and maintain quality management of the entire trial program. This also includes consultation and design of trial concepts with competent authorities and the preparation of scientific advice packages.
Part of your role will also include tasks as a Medical Writer, where you prepare and review scientific documentation on the efficacy and safety of our products. Here, you ensure the timely preparation of non‑clinical and clinical documentation for regulatory submissions, including the commissioning and review of modules 2.4 and 2.5.
In doing so, you respond to deficiency letters during registration procedures and manage corresponding customer inquiries.
Moreover, you identify, select, commission and collaborate with national and international service providers (CROs, monitors, consultants), including auditing and ensuring proper investigational medicinal product handling in line with GCP and GMP requirements.
Lastly, you collaborate with the project teams in due-diligence processes and evaluate dossiers as part of in‑licensing activities.
Your profile:
You hold a university degree in a life‑science discipline.
Moreover, you have a solid understanding of clinical research with more than 3 years of experience.
You combine scientific rigor with a hands‑on mentality and a structured, solution‑oriented way of working.
You communicate effectively with interdisciplinary teams and external partners and feel comfortable managing several projects simultaneously.
You are familiar with common IT applications within the framework of project management (MS Office). In top of that, you already gained experience in working with SAP or another ERP system.
Fluent spoken and written English skills complete your profile.

title: Manager PIPE (Principal Investors & Private Equity) (all genders) - BCG Platinion
jd_text:
Do you want to turn the challenges of digital transformation into tangible value creation and sustainable competitive advantages? Then join us in tackling critical IT and transaction issues for leading global companies and investors. Unlock our clients’ digital potential—from deal preparation to value creation—with tailored platforms, architectures, strategies, and organizational structures, and drive meaningful innovation.
As a
Manager - PIPE (Principal Investors & Private Equity) (all genders)
, you will work across the entire deal cycle of private equity investors—from the pre-deal phase to portfolio optimization and fund strategy. As part of an international team of tech specialists in EMESA, you will actively help shape technology-driven investment decisions for leading private equity investors.
Shape the world of tomorrow
You will lead and conduct tech due diligence and specifically assess the impact of technology on investment theses, risk profiles, and value potential
You will analyze application landscapes, software architectures, technical AI capabilities, product and tech organizations, and digital business models, and translate these into concrete deal implications You identify and quantify value-enhancement levers (e.g., AI strategies, scaling, platform strategies, tech transformation)
You develop concrete implementation plans and execute them together with our clients post-transaction (e.g., Full Potential Plan, carve-out, post-merger integration, etc.)
You work closely with C-level stakeholders (e.g., deal teams, operating partners, CFO, CTO, CIO) and act as a sparring partner on equal footing
You manage internal and external project teams and ensure fast, precise, and decision-oriented execution—often in highly dynamic transactions
You actively build and develop our PIPE Practice
You Make the Difference
You have successfully completed a degree in (Business) Information Technology, Business Administration, or a related field with above-average grades
You have at least six years of experience in IT consulting and in transactions with a strong IT focus, such as tech (vendor) due diligence, tech full potential planning, carve-outs, post-merger integration, etc.
You consistently think from an investor’s perspective and understand how technology influences enterprise value
You bring a strong, cross-industry understanding of business models, value drivers, and investment logic, and can clearly assess technology in terms of revenue, costs, speed, or scalability
You have experience leading projects, are skilled in efficient resource planning, and serve as a motivator and catalyst within your teams
You know how to present complex issues in an understandable way, calculate business cases, and drive them to a decision
You are fluent in German and English
How You Can Grow With Us
We guarantee you a constant stream of opportunities for growth in an open and supportive work environment. Every day, you’ll tackle challenges alongside a dedicated team of talented colleagues. Personalized professional development opportunities and an open feedback culture provide the perfect framework for discovering and honing your strengths. At work, our modern structures take the pressure off you. This includes hassle-free travel bookings, our excellent back-office team, and a comprehensive Knowledge Center. And with our regular Office Fridays and legendary events, we foster the strong and friendly network of our team, from which you’ll benefit even beyond your actual work.
Join our team
BCG Platinion is an Equal Opportunity Employer and is actively committed to diversity and inclusion. We consider all qualified applicants regardless of ethnic or social background, age, gender, sexual identity, religion, ideology, or disability. Apply now with your resume, a detailed list of projects, and all relevant certificates via our online portal, or feel free to contact Mike Stertz (+49 221 58958-555) if you have any questions.
More info at www.bcgplati

title: Consultant Asset Management / Innovation & Digital (m/w/d) in Hannover
jd_text:
Deloitte bietet führende Prüfungs- und Beratungsleistungen in Audit & Assurance, Tax & Legal, Consulting und Advisory - für nahezu 90 % der Fortune Global 500® und zahlreiche private Unternehmen. Wir liefern innovative Denkansätze, lösen komplexe Herausforderungen und fördern nachhaltiges Wachstum. Mit rund 470.000 Mitarbeitenden weltweit eröffnen wir hervorragende Karrierechancen - getragen von einem starken „Wir“ und einer Vielfalt an Perspektiven und Fähigkeiten.
Du willst im Bereich
Consulting – Banking & Capital Markets
Finanzdienstleister bei vielfältigen Banking-Projekten begleiten? Unser Team entwickelt Konzepte für (IT-) Transformationen, die Optimierung von Geschäftsprozessen und treibt mittels neuer Technologien sowie Strategien die Neuausrichtung des Finanzsektors voran. Sichere unseren gemeinsamen Erfolg und mach mit uns den Unterschied: als
Consultant Asset Management (m/w/d).
Standorte:
Frankfurt (Main), Berlin, Düsseldorf, Hamburg, Hannover, Köln, München und Stuttgart.
Dein Impact:
Als Consultant Asset Management (m/w/d) berätst du zu aktuellen Fragestellungen zur operativen und strategischen Ausrichtung unserer Kunden, zu individuellen ESG- und Nachhaltigkeitsstrategien sowie der passgenauen Umsetzung – und trägst so zu innovativen Lösungen, mehr Effizienz und Effektivität sowie Zukunftsfähigkeit bei.
Beratung: Du unterstützt mit deinem Team bei der strategischen Konzeption und begleitest die ESG-Integration in allen Unternehmensbereichen und Portfolien.
Innovation: Zudem entwickelst du neue Ansätze zur Effizienzsteigerung mithilfe von Automati­sierungstools, künstlicher Intelligenz oder durch Digitalisierung und Cloudlösungen.
Analyse: In deinen Projekten analysierst du Marktpotenziale und entwickelst auf dieser Basis Strategien für neue Anlageklassen wie Alternative Investments oder Krypo Assets.
Optimierung: Im Rahmen deiner Tätigkeit wirkst du bei der Verbesserung von Prozessen entlang der Wertschöpfungskette mit und hilfst bei der Umsetzung regulatorischer Anforderungen.
Interdisziplinär: Um neue, innovative Market Offerings - z. B. digitale Währungen - zu entwickeln, arbeitest du funktions- und industrieübergreifend mit anderen Deloitte-Teams zusammen.
Dein Skillset:
Sehr guter Abschluss in Wirtschaftswissenschaften, (Wirtschafts-) Informatik, Wirtschaftsin­genieurwesen, Physik, Mathematik oder vergleichbar, CFA oder ähnliche Zertifikate von Vorteil
Erste Berufserfahrung bei einem Asset oder Wealth Manager, FinTech oder Beratungs­unternehmen, Grundverständnis von IT-Architekturen / Datenstrukturen im Asset Management
Gute Kenntnisse der verschiedenen Geschäftsmodelle im Asset Management, Gespür für Branchentrends und Herausforderungen, z. B. ESG, Regulatorik, Margendruck, Konsolidierung
Hohes Engagement, sicherer Umgang mit MS Office, Eigenverantwortung und zielorientierte, agile Arbeitsweise, analyse- und konzeptionsstark
Reisebereitschaft sowie Freude an der Kommunikation und Präsentation auf Deutsch und Englisch (sehr gut in Wort und Schrift), weitere Sprachen von Vorteil
Deine Chance:
Spannende Aufgaben in einem innovativen Umfeld mit hochmotivierten Kolleg:innen, die sich schon jetzt auf deine Impulse freuen
Gute Rahmenbedingungen mit attraktivem Gehalt und zahlreichen Zusatzleistungen, z. B. Firmen-Smartphone zur privaten Nutzung, Fahrradleasing und Corporate Benefits
Work-Life-Balance durch Mobile Working & Teilzeit, EU Remote Working, Sabbaticals, Mitarbeiter:innen-Events, Well-being Angebote wie z. B. Gesundheitstage, Kooperationen mit Fitness-Anbietern und den Familienservice, der dich bei privaten Herausforderungen unterstützt
Exzellente Weiterbildung an der Deloitte University, in On- und Offline-Seminaren und im Rahmen der Berufsqualifikation, z. B. durch, z. B. durch SAFe, Blockchain oder Cloud Pass
Vielfältige Gestaltungsspielräume und aktive Förderung einer inklusiven Unternehmenskultur – u. a. durch unsere Diversity & Inclusion Mitarbeiter:innen-Netzwerke

title: Produktmanager*in (m/w/d)
jd_text:
Produktmanager*in (m/w/d) in Hamburg
Produktstrategie • technische Produktentwicklung • Portfolio & Go-to-Market
Als Produktmanager:in gestals du die Weiterentwicklung unserer Produkte an der Schnittstelle von Markt, Kund:innen, Vertrieb, Entwicklung und Produktion. Du übersetzt Kundenanforderungen und Marktbedarfe in klare Produktanforderungen, priorisierst Features und begleis Produkte von der Idee bis zur erfolgreichen Umsetzung.
Dein Fokus liegt auf einem zukunftsfähigen Produktportfolio, belastbaren Business Cases und einer engen Verzahnung zwischen Strategie und operativer Umsetzung. Dabei arbeis du eigenverantwortlich, datenbasiert und pragmatisch – mit dem Ziel, unsere Produkte technisch, wirtschaftlich und marktseitig kontinuierlich weiterzuentwickeln.
Darauf hast du Lust? Dann komm zu ENQT!
Wir entwickeln und fertigen hochwertige Testgeräte für Mobilfunknetze (4G, 5G, WLAN, IoT) und unterstützen europaweit über 500 Kunden bei der sicheren Vernetzung von Energieinfrastruktur und Industrieanwendungen. Unsere Kunden verlassen sich auf unsere Expertise, wenn es darum geht, moderne Mobilfunklösungen effizient umzusetzen.
