title: Bioprocess Development Leader – Germany
jd_text:
Bring more to life.
Are you ready to accelerate your potential and make a real difference within life sciences, diagnostics and biotechnology?
At Cytiva, one of Danaher’s 15+ operating companies, our work saves lives—and we’re all united by a shared commitment to innovate for tangible impact.
You’ll thrive in a culture of belonging where you and your unique viewpoint matter. And by harnessing Danaher’s system of continuous improvement, you help turn ideas into impact – innovating at the speed of life.
Working at Cytiva means being at the forefront of providing new solutions to transform human health. Our incredible customers undertake life-saving activities ranging from fundamental biological research to developing innovative vaccines, new medicines, and cell and gene therapies.
At Cytiva you will be able to continuously improve yourself and us – working on challenges that truly matter with people that care for each other, our customers, and their patients. Take your next step to an altogether life-changing career.
Learn about the Danaher Business System which makes everything possible.
The
Bioprocess Development Leader
is responsible for driving customer process development workflow optimization by leveraging Cytiva USP/DSP consumables technologies and / or FastTrak™ Process Development services (PDS).
This position reports to the
Regional Clinical Workflow Leader
and
will be
working remotely to cover Germany.
What You Will Do
Identify, qualify and develop new workflow and PDS opportunities within assigned territories through active prospecting
Deeply understand customer strategies, challenges, and operational processes. Acting as a Process Development Consultant both internally and externally
Define and coordinate Workflow Strategic Action Plans
Support tender, proposal, and negotiation processes with workflow and value-based input
Collaborate with Account Managers (AM), Sales Specialists (SS), and Field Application Specialists (FAS) for technical support to meet customer and process development requirements.
Represent Cytiva process development capabilities at conferences & events
Provide feedback to commercial teams on market trends, competitive threats, unmet needs, and new opportunities to enhance value by expanding the company’s offerings
Who You Are
MS or PhD in biology/biotechnology/biochemistry or equivalent
Strong commercial/applications experience, preferably in the Biotechnology industry.
Experience in USP/DSP Process Development
Commercial experience in biotech, biopharma or related technical area
Good communicator, comfortable in cross-functional/matrix environments
Travel, Motor Vehicle Record & Physical/Environment Requirements
Ability to travel – 50% within Germany
Must have a valid driver’s license with an acceptable driving record
Cytiva, a Danaher operating company, offers a broad array of comprehensive, competitive benefit programs that add value to our lives. Whether it’s a health care program or paid time off, our programs contribute to life beyond the job. Check out our benefits at Danaher Benefits Info.
At Cytiva we believe in designing a better, more sustainable workforce. We recognize the benefits of flexible, remote working arrangements for eligible roles and are committed to providing enriching careers, no matter the work arrangement. This position is eligible for a remote work arrangement in which you can work remotely from your home. Additional information about this remote work arrangement will be provided by your interview team. Explore the flexibility and challenge that working for Cytiva can provide.
Join our winning team today. Together, we’ll accelerate the real-life impact of tomorrow’s science and technology. We partner with customers across the globe to help them solve their most complex challenges, architecting solutions that bring the power of science to life.
For more information, visit www.danaher.com.

title: Team Lead AI Engineering (all genders)
jd_text:
Aufgaben
Du möchtest nicht nur an innovativen AI-Lösungen arbeiten, sondern auch ein Team führen und weiterentwickeln? Dann übernimm Verantwortung für ein Team sowie für die erfolgreiche Umsetzung datengetriebener und intelligenter Projekte.
Dabei warten die folgenden Aufgaben auf dich:
Du führst ein Team von Data Scientists, AI-Engineers und Computer-Vision-Spezialist:innen fachlich und disziplinarisch
Du leitest AI-, Computer-Vision- und Physical-AI-Projekte und verantwortest deren erfolgreiche Umsetzung – von Use Case bis produktiven Einsatz
Du steuerst den operativen Projekteinsatz deines Teams und stellst eine hohe Auslastung sowie Delivery-Qualität sicher
Du unterstützt Architekturentscheidungen und stellst die Einhaltung technischer Standards (AI-Engineering, MLOps, Vision Pipelines) sicher
Du bringst dich aktiv in Presales ein, entwickelst Demos und unterstützt bei technischen Lösungsdesigns
Du entwickelst dein Team fachlich und disziplinarisch weiter und baust gezielt Kompetenzen in den Bereichen ML und Physical AI auf
Anforderungen
Um für deinen Weg gewappnet zu sein, hast du folgende Qualifikationen im Gepäck:
Abgeschlossenes Studium sowie mehrjährige Erfahrung im Bereich Data Science, AI-Engineering oder Machine Learning sowie in der fachlichen und disziplinarischen Führung von Teams
Leidenschaft für die Umsetzung von AI-Projekten sowie ein gutes Verständnis für technische Lösungsdesigns
Expertise in AI/ML-Technologien sowie Erfahrung mit Frameworks wie TensorFlow oder PyTorch und Programmiersprachen wie Python und Erfahrung mit modernen Datenplattformen sowie Cloud-Technologien (AWS, Azure, GCP)
Deine Arbeitsweise ist geprägt von analytischem Denken, Führungsstärke, Pragmatismus sowie ausgeprägter Team- und Kundenorientierung
Wissenswertes vor der Abfahrt:
Beginn:
nach Vereinbarung – immer zu Beginn eines Monats
Arbeitszeit:
in Vollzeit (40h) und/oder Teilzeit möglich; 30 Urlaubstage
Arbeitsverhältnis:
Unbefristet
Bereich: Consulting
Sprache:
sicheres Deutsch & Englisch
Flexibilität & Reisebereitschaft
Sonstiges:
eine gültige Arbeitserlaubnis; bei Bedarf können wir die Arbeitserlaubnis im Rahmen unseres Recruiting-Prozesses beantragen. Das Verfahren benötigt Zeit und hat Einfluss auf das Startdatum
summary: Lead a team of AI engineers and Data Scientists to deliver AI projects. Manage technical standards and operations for AI and Computer Vision initiatives. Support presales and technical solution designs.

title: Junior Innovation Coach (m/w/div)
jd_text:
Ort: Berlin Eintrittsdatum: Sofort
Bewerbungsfrist: 04.08.2026
Ausschreibungsnummer: 11-089-2026
Beschäftigung: Vollzeit (Teilzeit ist grundsätzlich möglich)
Vergütung: E11 TV EntgO-DRV
Tätigkeitsbereich
Die "DRV Bund IT" ist der Service-Provider für die Deutsche Rentenversicherung
Bund und entwickelt und betreibt in enger Kundenabstimmung eine komplexe
IT-Systemlandschaft. Das Innovation Lab der DRV Bund IT arbeitet an der
Schnittstelle zwischen Fachseite und IT daran, die Innovationsfähigkeit des
Bereichs voranzutreiben. Dazu bieten wir diverse Services an, durch die unsere
stakeholder verschiedenste Innovationsmethoden nutzen können. Wir arbeiten
dabei mit agilen und nutzer*innenzentrierten Methoden, durch die unsere Coaches
den Teilnehmenden neue Arbeitsweisen näherbringen.
Ihre Aufgaben
Planen, Vorbereiten und Durchführen von Services des Innovation Labs (unter anderem Workshops, Design Sprints, Trainings oder Events) als Co-Coach
Unterstützung beim Einholen und Auswerten von Feedbacks für Workshops inklusive Dokumentation von Workshopergebnissen
Unterstützen unserer Senior Innovation Coaches bei Nachbesprechungen mit den Kund*innen
Mitwirken beim Weiterentwickeln der Innovation Lab-Services, einschließlich dem Evaluieren neuer Methoden im Umfeld innovativer und interdisziplinärer Arbeitsweisen
Ihr Profil
Erfolgreicher Abschluss einer Hochschulbildung (Bachelor, Diplom (FH)) beispielsweise Informatik, Wirtschaftsinformatik, Sozial-, Ingenieur-, Kommunikations- oder Wirtschaftswissenschaften (zum Beispiel Betriebswirtschaftslehre, Personalmanagement / HR, Organisationsentwicklung, Psychologie), Produktentwicklung oder Technologiemanagement
Aktuelle und mehrjährige aufgabenrelevante Berufserfahrung, idealerweise auf dem Gebiet der Organisationsberatung und des Veränderungsmanagements
Eine systemische Zusatzausbildung und/oder Zertifizierung als Agile Coach, Scrum Master, Trainer*in für agile Methoden oder Mediator*in, Design Thinking Facilitator oder Change Manager
Erste praktische Erfahrungen in der Anwendung agiler Prinzipien und Frameworks und nutzerzentrierter Vorgehensweisen und -modelle und entsprechenden Tools wie Jira, Confluence, Miro oder ähnlichen
Erste Arbeitserfahrungen im Einsatz von Innovationsmethoden und -modellen (beispielhaft Design Thinking)
Ein hohes Maß an Kund*innenorientierung und hervorragende mündliche wie schriftliche Kommunikationsfähigkeiten werden vorausgesetzt
Sehr gute organisatorische Fähigkeiten, Lösungsorientierung sowie Einfühlungsvermögen runden Ihr Profil ab
Wir bieten
Die Möglichkeit die Weiterentwicklung unserer Services im InnovationLab mitzugestalten
Einen breiten Bildungskatalog, individuelle Lernpfade sowie Mentoring durch erfahrene Kolleg*innen, um Knowhow kontinuierlich auf- bzw. auszubauen und voneinander zu lernen
Fach- und kompetenzorientierte Fort- und Weiterbildungsangebote für nachhaltige Entwicklungs- und Aufstiegsmöglichkeiten
Ein familienfreundliches Unternehmen mit flexiblen Arbeitszeiten und Gleitzeitregelung
Die Möglichkeit, nach erfolgreicher Einarbeitung tageweise im Homeoffice zu arbeiten
Vielseitige Serviceangebote sowie Kooperationspartner*innen zur Vereinbarkeit von Beruf und Familie für Eltern und pflegende Angehörige
Die Gesundheit unserer Beschäftigten ist uns wichtig. Wir unterstützen die praktische Umsetzung des Betrieblichen Gesundheitsmanagements in den Arbeitsalltag.
Weitere Informationen
Zur Besetzung der Position werden wir mit Bewerbenden, die sich in der engeren
Auswahl befinden, Gespräche führen.
Im Rahmen der Tätigkeit kann die Teilnahme an einer Sicherheitsüberprüfung (SÜ)
gemäß § 9 Sicherheitsüberprüfungsgesetz (SÜG) erforderlich sein; hierfür wird die
entsprechende Bereitschaft sowie ein erfolgreicher Abschluss vorausgesetzt.
Die Tätigkeit erfordert fließende Deutschkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift.
In dem ausgeschriebenen Bereich sind Frauen im Sinne des
Bundesgleichstellungsgesetzes unterrepräsentiert. Als Arbeitgeberin, die die
berufliche Förd

title: AI Transformation Manager
jd_text:
Job Summary
Please enter your job advertisement. If you are using Workday recruitment this is what will be published on the website above the job description. This cannot be formatted with bullet points etc. so please use reader friendly paragraphs.
Job Description
About Modaxo
Modaxo is a global organisation bringing together businesses that collectively focus on advancing new technologies and innovations for people transportation. With more than 40 companies representing over 30 brands, Modaxo comprises over 3,300 people operating from almost 40 countries around the world.
"Modaxo's expertise, global bench strength, and singular focus on People Transportation make it a great home for technology companies that share our vision to efficiently move the world's people." — Bill Delaney, CEO, Modaxo
The Role
As the AI Transformation Manager, you will be a hands-on partner to our Business Units (BUs), working directly with BU leaders and their teams to identify where AI creates commercial value, get initiatives off the ground, and build lasting capability.
The role connects Modaxo's AI ambition to what happens in practice: finding the right opportunities, removing the obstacles that slow things down, and helping our people use AI with real confidence. This is a full-time role reporting to the Group Leader.
You don't need to be a developer or data scientist, however, you should be a confident, knowledgeable AI practitioner who can credibly engage with both business leaders and technical teams.
Core Responsibilities
AI Opportunity & Roadmap
Work with BU (Business Unit) leaders to identify and prioritise AI use cases based on commercial impact, customer experience, feasibility, and strategic fit.
Translate priorities into actionable roadmaps and help teams build the business cases to move forward.
Share successful approaches across BUs — when something works in one business, help others benefit from it.
Implementation Support
Act as the practical implementation partner when BUs are using AI tools and workflows to drive their AI initiatives from conception to deployment.
Guide and advise Business Units on cost efficient tool utilisation and synergy creation across the Group.
Identify and remove blockers — process friction, data gaps, change resistance — to keep projects moving from pilot to production.
Run AI pilots designed to generate transferable learnings and scalable outcomes.
Capability Building
Support BU’s AI Champions day-to-day with guidance, escalation paths, and access to Modaxo AI Center of Excellence resources.
Run practical trainings — especially prompt crafting and real-world AI use — to build skills at team level.
Help BU AI Champions grow into confident, independent leads so BUs don't rely on central support for every question.
Group Leader Partnership
Maintain a live group AI roadmap to define key themes and initiatives to be rolled out across all BUs.
Keep the Group Leader informed on progress, blockers, and decisions that need their input.
Make AI adoption a visible part of normal operational performance conversations.
Define AI Governance approach for the Group.
Who We're Looking For
The strongest candidates will have made complex change happen inside real businesses, not just planned it. Backgrounds in consulting, transformation, or senior business partnering tend to work well.
Essential
Track record of driving adoption of significant change programmes in complex, multi-site organisations.
Comfortable managing multiple stakeholder relationships and competing priorities across business units simultaneously.
Background in business transformation, change management, or senior commercial/operational roles with meaningful change exposure or management consulting with relevant transformation experience.
Used to getting things done through people who don't report to you.
A genuine, hands-on user of AI in a professional context — credible, curious, and up to date.
Understands the realities of running a Business Unit: Com
summary: Partner with Business Units to drive commercial value through AI implementation and capability building. Guide AI pilots, remove operational blockers, and foster shared learnings across the group. Ensure structured AI adoption and governance.

title: Proposition Manager EMSP
jd_text:
Shape the future of e-mobility across Europe
Do you want to create seamless charging experiences for electric vehicle drivers and help accelerate the transition to sustainable mobility?
As a
Proposition Manager EMSP (E-Mobility Service Provider)
, you will play a key role in shaping and evolving our offering across multiple European markets.
You will work at the intersection of strategy, product development, and customer experience—collaborating with international teams to deliver innovative, customer-centric solutions.
Your Role
Develop and evolve our EMSP proposition
Define and continuously improve our EMSP offering for different customer segments and use cases
Analyze market trends, customer needs, and competitive developments
Contribute to the strategic roadmap, including pricing, tariffs, and loyalty features
Support key partnerships and fleet solutions
Design tailored offers for key accounts, fleet customers, and EMSP partnerships
Collaborate with local sales teams to identify and assess business opportunities
Build and strengthen partnerships with outbound roaming partners
Go-to-market, pricing & commercial setup
Define go-to-market approaches for new and existing propositions
Collaborate closely with pricing experts
Support contract terms and enable sales teams through training and guidance
Drive product development
Represent customer and market needs in the product development lifecycle
Set priorities and support implementation of features such as apps, charge cards, and automated charging
Work closely with product owners and technical teams
Enhance Customer Experience
Monitor and act on customer feedback
Help ensure a smooth and consistent customer journey across markets
Enable and align internal teams
Train and support sales, marketing, operations, and customer service teams
Act as a bridge between commercial and technical stakeholders
Location: Stockholm (Solna), Amsterdam, Berlin or Hamburg
Qualifications
Your profile
Master’s degree (e.g., MSc or MBA)
Experience in e-mobility or a complex product environment (approx. 5+ years)
Strong customer focus and commercial mindset
Structured, analytical, and quality-driven way of working
Comfortable collaborating with and influencing diverse stakeholders
Confident communicator and presenter
Enjoy working in an international and cross-functional environment
Proactive, curious, and solution-oriented mindset
Fluent in English (written and spoken)
Additional Information
Our offer
Good remuneration, a challenging and international work environment, and the possibility to work with some of the best in the field. You will be working in interdisciplinary teams and you can always count on support from committed colleagues. We offer attractive employment conditions and opportunities for personal and professional development.
More Information
We welcome your application in English no later than 16th.07.2026.
We kindly request that you do not send applications by any means other than via our website as we cannot guarantee that we will be able to process applications that are not made via our website.
For more information about the recruitment process you are welcome to contact our recruiter Maryana Halushko, maryana.halushko@vattenfall.de.
At Vattenfall we are convinced that diversity contributes to build a more profitable and attractive company and we strive to be good role model regarding diversity. Vattenfall works actively for all employees to have the same opportunities and rights regardless of gender, ethnicity, age, transgender identity or expression, religion or other belief, disability or sexual orientation.
The security of Vattenfall and its employees is essential. For that reason, a pre-employment screening will be part of your recruitment process. The screening is based on the role you will fulfill within Vattenfall. The screening will be performed by a third party, DISA.
We look forward to receiving your application!
Company Description
Vattenfall is a European energy com

title: Product Strategy Lead
jd_text:
The Opportunity:
The Product Strategy Lead is the end‑to‑end owner of integrated Product Strategy for an assigned portfolio of products, accountable for translating commercial ambition and R&D strategy into clear, executable manufacturing and operations strategies across the full product lifecycle.
This role serves as the primary strategic integrator across Manufacturing and Technical Operations, Commercial, R&D, Business Development, Quality, Regulatory, Supply Chain, Finance, and Network Strategy. The Product Strategy Lead ensures product‑level decisions regarding investment, capacity, capability, and lifecycle trade‑offs are coherent, value‑driven, and aligned to enterprise priorities, enabling safe, reliable supply of CSL products to patients.
Acting as the product’s representative in senior governance forums, the Product Strategy Lead shapes and anchors product‑level strategic decisions, ensuring leadership has a clear, fact‑based view of options, risks, trade‑offs, and value implications across time horizons.
The Role:
Product Strategy Ownership and Integration
Owns the single, integrated Product Strategy for assigned products, ensuring end‑to‑end alignment across Commercial, R&D, BD, and Operations.
Acts as the product‑level strategy decision owner within Operations, accountable for framing options, recommending trade‑offs, and anchoring decisions adopted through governance.
Ensures product strategies balance cost, risk, speed, supply resilience, and long‑term enterprise value.
Portfolio & Lifecycle Strategy
Leads end‑to‑end product lifecycle strategy, including development, launch strategy, growth, optimization, and end‑of‑life decisions.
Actively manages portfolio trade‑offs across assigned products, including prioritization, sequencing of investments, and capacity or capability allocation.
Ensures lifecycle decisions are grounded in both current performance and future portfolio needs.
Financial & Investment Framing
Owns the financial and value framing of product‑level strategy decisions, including investment rationale, scenario analysis, and long‑term value impact.
Provides product inputs into Long‑Range Planning (LRP), ensuring commercial assumptions, manufacturing strategy, and capacity plans are tightly aligned.
Partners with Finance to ensure leadership decisions are supported by clear economic logic and value realization expectations.
Cross Functional Alignment and Orchestration
Leads and orchestrates cross‑functional stakeholders across TechOps, Network Strategy, R&D, Commercial, Supply Chain, Quality, Regulatory, Finance, Procurement, and BD.
Drives alignment and resolves conflicts across competing priorities, operating effectively without direct authority.
Ensures execution ownership and decision rights are clear following governance outcomes.
Executive Governance & Communication
Develops and presents decision‑ready recommendations to senior governance bodies including GLT members, NRB, ORB, IBP, and other executive forums.
Distills complex technical, regulatory, manufacturing, and financial information into clear executive narratives.
Acts as a trusted strategic advisor to senior leaders on product‑level strategy matters.
Product Lifecycle and Portfolio Strategy
Leads end‑to‑end product lifecycle management, including development, launch, growth, optimization, and end‑of‑life decisions.
Ensures portfolio strategy balances near‑term performance with long‑term enterprise value creation.
Owns product‑level inputs into Long‑Range Planning (LRP)
Converts commercial forecasts, Business Development intent, and lifecycle scenarios into clear demand, volume, timing, and risk assumptions
Ensures assumptions are internally consistent and defensible across functions
Acts as the single source of truth for product demand logic in planning cycles
Integrates LRP and capacity decisions into product lifecycle strategy
Shapes capacity and network strategy decisions through influence, not ownership
Ways of Working & Strategy Ma

title: Industrial Strategy Consultant (all genders)
jd_text:
Berate Kund:innen aus dem Industrial‑Bereich in ihrer Geschäftsstrategie bei der Entwicklung maßgeschneiderter Lösungen und deren Umsetzung unter Einbindung modernster Technologien.
Deine Mission
Als Industrial Strategy Consultant begleitest du weltweit agierende Industrieunternehmen dabei, ihre Geschäftsstrategien neu auszurichten und die Digitalisierung gezielt voranzutreiben. Zusammen mit einem dynamischen und motivierten Team erlebst du aus erster Hand, wie sich innovative Strategien und die neuesten Technologien auf Branchen und Geschäftsmodelle auswirken.
Wir haben dein Interesse geweckt? Dann werde Teil unseres Industrial Strategy Teams in Deutschland!
Bei Accenture gestaltest du dir selbst das Umfeld, in dem du aufgehst – mit Arbeitsweisen, die zu dir passen. Du bleibst flexibel. Und wirst Teil eines Teams voller einzigartiger Menschen, die gemeinsam etwas bewegen.
Das erwartet dich
Gestalte die digitale Industrie von morgen. Du entwickelst Strategien rund um die Themen digitale Transformation, Data & AI, Organisationstransformation und Industry X für global führende Industrieunternehmen. Dazu zählen z.B. traditionelle Maschinen- und Anlagenbauer, Hersteller von Haushaltswaren, sowie Automobilzulieferer.
Begleite komplexe Projekte und optimiere Geschäftsprozesse. Du arbeitest eng mit dem Top‑Management des Kunden zusammen und stellst die Weichen für deren langfristigen Erfolg. Mit Deiner ganzheitlichen Beratung von der Konzept- und Strategieentwicklung bis zu ihrer Implementierung schaffst Du für unsere Kund:innen echten Mehrwert.
Biete erstklassige Kundenbetreuung. Du nutzt dein Fachwissen und deine innovativen Ideen, um potenzielle Kund:innen von einer Zusammenarbeit mit uns zu überzeugen
Darauf freuen wir uns
Du hast ein abgeschlossenes Masterstudium an einer Universität mit Schwerpunkt Betriebswirtschaft, Ingenieurwissenschaft, Naturwissenschaft oder Technik
Mindestens 2–4 Jahre Erfahrung im Industrial-, Manufacturing‑ oder Maschinen‑/Anlagenbau‑Umfeld oder in einer Unternehmensberatung
Starke konzeptionelle Fähigkeiten sowie Freude daran, neue Lösungsansätze für komplexe Fragestellungen auszuarbeiten
Sicherer Umgang mit KI‑Tools und der Fähigkeit, diese gezielt zur Produktivitätssteigerung einzusetzen
Aufgeschlossene, kommunikative Persönlichkeit mit kreativer und innovativer Denkweise
Sehr gute Team‑ und Kommunikationsfähigkeiten in Deutsch und Englisch
Was wir dir bieten
Individuelles Arbeitszeitmodell mit flexibel anpassbaren Arbeitszeiten
Arbeiten, wo du gebraucht wirst – an einem der Accenture-Standorte, remote oder beim Kunden vor Ort
Umfangreiche Angebote für Fort- und Weiterbildung
Attraktive Aufstiegschancen
Eine Arbeitskultur, die Diversität und Inklusion fördert
Du und Accenture
In dieser Rolle bist du Teil von
Accenture Strategy & Consulting
. Der aktuelle Wandel eröffnet Unternehmen zuvor nie dagewesene Chancen. Mit ganzheitlichen Strategien helfen wir unseren Kunden, diese umfassend auszuschöpfen und sich neu zu erfinden.
Die Accenture-Kultur: Hier machst DU den Unterschied
Deine Fähigkeiten, dein Naturell und deine Ambitionen machen dich einzigartig. Und wenn die unterschiedlichsten Talente, Stärken und Sichtweisen aufeinandertreffen, entsteht etwas Großartiges: Ideen, die in der Welt etwas bewegen. Deshalb setzen wir bei Accenture auf Diversität und freie Entfaltung. Schaffe dir ein Arbeitsumfeld, in dem du aufgehst. Mit Aufgaben, die dir am Herzen liegen. Mit einem Workload, der zu dir passt. Und mit einer Arbeitsweise, die sich an deinen Zielen orientiert. Wie du dich vernetzen, weiterbilden und persönlich wachsen willst, liegt bei dir. Es ist deine Karriere. Wir helfen dir, sie so zu gestalten, wie du es dir vorstellst.
Dein Kontakt
Noch Fragen offen? Schreib uns an recruiting_germany@accenture.com.
Wir freuen uns auf dich!

title: (Senior) Product Manager (m/w/d)
jd_text:
At
GALVANY
, our goal is to make climate-neutral living a reality for everyone. We focus on execution – developing concrete, smart solutions and making heat pumps, battery storage, and smart metering easy to access, reliable, and affordable. GALVANY-Tech drives the energy transition through software. We're building an AI-based Operating System for sales, planning, installation and operation of heat pumps and integrated energy systems – from single-family homes to multi-unit buildings, and across our Energy Community as a Virtual Power Plant.
We are a profitable green-tech startup and believe that sustainable impact and long-term growth are only possible when grounded in a healthy business model. To implement our ambitious tech roadmap, we secured €10 million in seed funding from Set Ventures and AENU in the spring 2026. Driven by customer value, clarity, and responsibility, we stand for high-quality heating solutions and an environment where people take responsibility and drive lasting impact.
The Role
As Product Manager at GALVANY, you'll define and execute the product strategy for our customer app. You'll shape features that make energy management engaging and intuitive for homeowners – turning complex systems into products people actually want to use.
Responsibilities:
Own vision and roadmap for your product area – discover, translate, prioritize, and deliver.
Deeply understand customer needs through interviews, data analysis, and collaboration with Sales and Support.
Measure success, orchestrate sprints and releases, and navigate the four product risks (customer value, usability, feasibility, business value).
Design engagement loops and incentives that make the product sticky and valuable.
Support go-to-market strategy in collaboration with Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success.
Requirements
Experience. 5+ years of experience in high-performance environments (e.g. fast-scaling startups). Demonstrable end-to-end responsibility in software development is a plus.
Track Record. Proven end-to-end responsibility in product development. From ideation to release.
Background. Strong background in relevant fields, e.g. Business, Design, Communication, Computer Science, or similar.
Technical Skills. Agile thinking, hands-on mentality, willingness to take responsibility. Fluent in German and English.
Mindset. Self-driven problem-solving mindset – no need for micromanagement or specific tickets.
Technical Aptitude. Technical mindset with a passion for understanding systems, data flows, and integrations, coupled with enthusiasm for continuous learning and problem solving.
Language. Fluent in English (C1) and German (C1).
Skills And Qualities That Are Important To Us
🚀
Outcome-Led.
You focus on value over volume. You embrace iteration, adapt quickly to new information, and prioritize what moves the needle for business and user.
🧠
Systems Thinker.
You see the bigger picture. You understand how your work connects to the wider ecosystem – ensuring features contribute to a cohesive, scalable whole.
⚡
Pragmatic.
You choose the simplest effective path to solve problems – especially by leveraging AI tools.
🏡
Customer Champion.
You keep the end-user central in all decisions. You seek direct exposure to how customers experience the product.
🎮
Engagement Designer.
You think in loops, incentives, and behavioral nudges. You make products sticky without being manipulative.
📊
Data-Informed Prioritizer.
You use qualitative and quantitative signals to prioritize ruthlessly. You can articulate why
not
to build something.
Benefits
Strong Growth & High Impact. A unique opportunity to join during a hypergrowth phase and actively contribute to company success.
Compensation. Competitive salary and flexible perks (sports, mobility, learning) tailored to your needs.
Real-World Impact. Your work drives decarbonization – measurable in CO₂ savings, energy efficiency (kWh), and cost reductions (€).
Office. Prime location in Berlin Charlottenburg, regular company e

title: Product Charging Solutions Manager
jd_text:
Chery Automobile is a leading Chinese auto company and an international brand whose business covers more than 80 countries and regions.
For our Engineering Center in Raunheim, near Frankfurt, we are looking for a
Product Charging Solutions Manager
We welcome you to join a growing team of extraordinarily talented and enthusiastic people, developing tailor-made cars for the European market.
We want to work with a flexible and enthusiastic individual wo will be required to support the development of our European vehicles locally as well as globally - jointly with our teams in our headquarter in China.
Responsibilities:
1. Responsible for planning the full product line of residential AC charging stations, public DC ultra-fast chargers, and commercial and industrial charging equipment in Europe, covering the localized needs of Germany, the UK, and other EU countries.
2. Research competitors, distribution channels, user habits, installation standards, and property access requirements in the European charging market to produce quarterly market insights.
3. Define interaction requirements for charging hardware, backend cloud systems, and mobile apps, balancing cost, energy efficiency, outdoor protection, and aesthetic integration with home interiors.
4. Oversee the entire new product lifecycle—from requirements definition, R&D, and certification through mass production to overseas market launch—managing NPI milestones and localization adaptation.
5. Collaborate with European charging station operators and residential customers to identify pain points in equipment operation, installation, and O&M, and drive improvements to the hardware and software user experience. 6. Develop Go-to-Market (GTM) strategies to support overseas marketing, trade shows, and channel product training
Qualifications:
1. Bachelor’s degree or higher from an accredited institution in Power Electronics, Energy and Power Engineering, Industrial Design, or related fields
2. At least 5 years of experience as a product manager for AC/DC charging stations, with a proven track record of successfully launching products in the EU market
3. Familiarity with European charging station safety standards, grid connection regulations, IP outdoor protection ratings, and residential installation regulations
4. Understanding of home energy storage + charging station integration systems; mastery of the basic logic of BMS and PCS in energy replenishment equipment.
5. Fluent in English with the ability to independently handle external business communications; proficiency in German is a plus; proficient in using Axure, XMind, and the Office suite.
6. Capable of cost estimation, competitive benchmarking, and product iteration planning; experience with on-site assignments or business travel overseas is preferred.
Join our dynamic team and be a part of our company's growth and success!

title: Associate Product Manager (m/f/d)
jd_text:
Interested in a career that matters?
The
Associate Product Manager (APM)
supports the Product Portfolio Manager within the Product Management Team (PMT) , the role is anchored in Global Marketing. The role focuses on ensuring operational excellence across the product lifecycle by coordinating cross-functional activities, supporting product portfolio management, and driving timely execution of product-related processes in alignment with the company's product range and industry strategies.
In this role you’ll make an impact by:
Support PMT Operations: Participate in PMT meetings, support agenda and target follow-up, and drive cross-functional actions to ensure timely execution.
Manage Product Establishment & Sampling – Coordinate product establishments, sampling activities, approvals, documentation, and stakeholder communication.
Maintain the Product Portfolio – Support product range analysis, product pruning, Product Change Notifications (PCNs), Product Data Sheets, and portfolio management tools.
Coordinate Cross-Functional Activities – Work with Engineering, Supply Chain, Marketing, and other stakeholders to resolve operational issues and support business requests.
Support Market & Product Development – Coordinate Near-to-Market activities, manage product variant requests, and support execution of new product initiatives.
Drive Operational Excellence – Contribute to business growth and continuous improvement by supporting quality, cost, supply, capacity, and customer-focused product management activities.
You will thrive in this role if you bring some of the following:
Bachelor's degree in Engineering or a related technical discipline.
Minimum 2 years of relevant experience in Engineering, Supply Chain, Marketing, or Product Management-related roles.
Strong communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to coordinate and influence cross-functional teams.
Structured, analytical, and detail-oriented, with solid project coordination and problem-solving skills.
Proactive, curious, and accountable, demonstrating ownership and the ability to drive tasks through to completion.
Working Model:
This role follows a hybrid working model, with 3 days per week in the office and up to 2 days working remotely, subject to business needs. The role also requires regular travel to our Denmark headquarters. Travel is expected approximately 6–12 times per year (typically monthly to every other month), with each visit usually lasting up to one week. Travel frequency is generally higher during the first year and may reduce over time
Our purpose guides the way
In Novonesis, we know that solutions rooted in biology can help solve humanity’s biggest challenges. Since we began more than a century ago, this has been our guide. It’s how we've gotten so far. And it’s how we'll impact the future. Now, more than ever, the world needs change. And with biosolutions, the possibilities for transformation are endless. If our purpose resonates with you, we encourage you to apply.
Opportunities for everyone
We are committed to an inclusive recruitment process and equal opportunity for all applicants. Therefore we ask you to apply without a cover letter or photo and instead include a few sentences in your CV explaining your motivation for applying.
We make all employment decisions based on business needs and welcome candidates with a wide range of backgrounds, including ethnicity, religion, gender, sexual orientation, age, disability, or veteran status.
Explore Other Openings
If this position isn’t the right fit, you may still find other opportunities with us. You’re welcome to explore open roles or create a job agent to stay connected with future opportunities at novonesis.com/Career

title: (Senior) Product Manager (all genders)
jd_text:
Across Europe, fleets are under enormous pressure. Fuel costs are rising. Regulatory requirements are tightening. The shift to low-emission vehicles is accelerating. And yet around 70% of European businesses still manage their fleets manually, with spreadsheets, disconnected tools, and processes that were never built for this moment.
Shiftmove
exists to make this simple.
We are a profitable B2B SaaS company and the fastest-growing fleet management platform in Europe, born from the merger of Avrios and Vimcar, and now serving tens of thousands of corporate customers across Germany, France, Spain, Switzerland, Poland, and beyond. Most recently, we acquired Optimum and Ocean in France, expanding our footprint and doubling down on one of Europe's largest fleet markets. Our AI-powered platform connects real-time vehicle data with intelligent software so that businesses can procure smarter, control costs, stay compliant, and lead the transition to cleaner mobility, all from one place.
About the role:
As our
(Senior)
Product Manager,
you will take over one of the most strategically important product domains at Shiftmove – the platform and ecosystem layer,
aka the connective tissue between Avrios, Vimcar, Optimum, Océan, and external partners.
You will own the product direction for this layer: deciding what becomes a reusable platform capability, what should stay market-specific, and where the team focuses first. You will work directly with Engineering, senior leadership, and cross-functional stakeholders across Germany and France to bring clarity to an ambiguous domain and turn complex problems into focused product direction.
This is a build and define phase, with a large scope and direct impact on Shiftmove's ability to expand across Europe, grow cross-sell revenue, and establish the platform foundation behind the leading fleet mobility ecosystem on the continent.
What you'll do:
Platform & Ecosystem Strategy:
Own the product direction for our platform and integration scope, connecting our products, external partners, market-specific systems, and customer workflows across Europe.
Ambiguity into Direction:
Create structure where ownership, priorities, and system boundaries are not yet fully defined. Decide what becomes a reusable platform capability, what should stay market-specific, and where the team focuses first.
Discovery and Delivery:
Lead discovery and delivery for core platform capabilities: shared workflows, reusable integration patterns, APIs, data flows, and interoperability between products.
European Market Expansion:
Build internationalisation capabilities that help our core products scale beyond one market at a time.
Cross-Product Portfolio:
Shape initiatives that bring Avrios, Vimcar, Optimum, and Océan closer together, reducing friction for customers and unlocking stronger cross-sell opportunities across the portfolio.
Stakeholder Alignment:
Work closely with Engineering, GTM, Sales, Customer Success, country teams, and senior leadership to align priorities and move complex topics forward across products and markets.
Focus and Ownership:
Protect the team from reactive request intake by setting clear priorities, defining ownership boundaries, and keeping focus on long-term platform value over one-off solutions.
What the first 6 months might look like:
Months 1 to 2:
Focus on onboarding, learning about our product portfolio, stakeholders, and ways of working.
Months 3 to 4:
Build trusting relationships with your team, translating your learnings into a focused product strategy and roadmap, and working on first challenges.
Months 5 to 6:
You're driving your topics, delivering reusable integration and internationalisation capabilities that make our platform more scalable across markets.
What you'll need to be successful:
Scaleup Mindset:
You possess a fierce ownership mentality, thrive in ambiguity, and enjoy building structure where there isn't a playbook.
B2B Product Background:
You have a solid track record in B2B produ

title: Product Manager (m/w/d)
jd_text:
Zur Verstärkung unseres Teams suchen wir Dich zunächst auf zwei Jahre befristet als
Product Manager (m/w/d)
DEINE AUFGABEN
Du entwickelst gemeinsam mit dem Product Lead die digitalen Produkte und User Journeys Deiner Marke weiter – von der Idee über das Konzept bis zum Live-Betrieb und zur kontinuierlichen Optimierung
Du übersetzt die Marken- und Produktstrategie in konkrete Initiativen, Features und Experimente
Du analysierst Nutzerbedürfnisse und -verhalten anhand von Daten, Research und Feedback und leitest daraus Hypothesen sowie Produktmaßnahmen ab
Du erstellst und pflegst Epics und User Stories in enger Zusammenarbeit mit Tech, Business und Redaktion
Du steuerst Discovery- und Delivery-Prozesse - von MVPs über A/B-Tests bis hin zu iterativen Verbesserungen - und optimierst definierte User Journeys kontinuierlich
Du behältst relevante Produkt-KPIs wie Reichweite, Engagement, Conversion Rates und Feature Adoption im Blick und reportest die Ergebnisse für Deine Marke
DEIN PROFIL
Du verfügst über mehrjährige Erfahrung im Digital Product Management oder in einer vergleichbaren Rolle, z. B. als Product Owner oder im Bereich Produktentwicklung bzw. Growth/Conversion Management
Du denkst konsequent aus Nutzerperspektive und arbeitest mit Hypothesen, MVPs und iterativen Produktentwicklungsprozessen
Du hast Erfahrung in der Datenanalyse (z. B. Web-/App-Analytics und Dashboards) und nutzt Kennzahlen als Grundlage für Entscheidungen
Du verstehst digitale Geschäftsmodelle und weißt, welche Rolle Produkte für Reichweite, Engagement und Monetarisierung spielen
Du bringst eine hohe Affinität zu Marken und Content mit und bist souverän in der Kommunikation mit vielen Stakeholdern
Du arbeitest strukturiert, selbstständig und pragmatisch:„Done & gelernt“ ist Dir lieber als „perfekt & nie live“
Deine Benefits
Innovation trifft Tradition: Du gestaltest die digitale Transformation unserer Mediengruppe mit
Entwickle Dich mit uns: Wir ermöglichen Dir die Teilnahme an individuellen Coachings, Workshops oder (Online)-Trainings im Rahmen unserer FUNKE Akademie
Flexibel arbeiten: Wir leben ein hybrides Arbeitsmodell, weil uns Deine Work-Life-Balance wichtig ist
Teamwork: Dich erwarten ein dynamisches Team, eine offene Feedbackkultur, kurze Kommunikationswege und ein herzliches Miteinander
Corporate Benefits: Wir bieten Dir ein Benefit-Portal mit attraktiven Rabatten und Abos
Halte Dich fit und gesund: Profitiere von unseren Kooperationen mit verschiedensten Fitnessstudios und lasse Dich für Deine mentale und körperliche Gesundheit individuell beraten
Haben wir Dein Interesse geweckt? Dann gestalte mit uns die Zukunft und sende uns Deine Bewerbung an Jennifer Bay.

title: Lead Product Manager, B2B Trade Platform
jd_text:
About Andercore
Andercore is the AI-native supplier of industrial materials for energy, infrastructure, and construction in wholesale and beyond.
We trade with global suppliers and distribute to European customers on our own account. Our AI runs the full trade and distribution end-to-end: sourcing, quality, pricing, sales, logistics, and embedded financing. For the customer, it feels like buying from their preferred local supplier; for our partners, it is the most convenient and safe way to do business across borders. Behind it, our software and agentic AI do the heavy lifting that used to take an asset-intensive supply chain with four or five intermediaries and weeks of manual coordination.
Where We Are Today
Strong triple-digit-million euro turnover
Seven European markets live
80+ people across Berlin (HQ), offices in London, Mumbai, and Shanghai
$40M Series B just closed, $75M raised to date from Atomico, Project A, and Inven Capital, institutional financing from international banks.
We are building the world's first and last industrial-grade AI operating system for materials, redefining how global trade works in one of the largest and most essential industries on earth.
About This Role
We're hiring a Director Product Management to own a core product area end to end. This is a hands-on leadership role: you'll set direction and ship it yourself, reporting directly to the founder.
What You Will Own
A P&L-relevant product area (logistics automation, pricing, or embedded finance) from discovery to shipped outcomes
Concrete targets, not vibes: we run on automation and retention metrics, and your area will have a number attached
Discovery with real users: traders, logistics operators, and buyers, weekly and in person
Prioritization and delivery with your engineering squad, including the calls on what not to build
Post-launch iteration driven by usage data and margin impact, not opinion
What You Bring
5+ years in product management with at least 2 at a B2B scale-up, owning a product area through significant growth
Shipped products in an operations-heavy domain: logistics, marketplaces, supply chain, fintech, or similar
Fluency with data: you pull your own numbers, define your own metrics, and change course when they tell you to
A track record of fast MVP cycles: built, measured, iterated or killed, and you can walk us through the decisions
Experience shipping AI-powered features to production is a strong plus
Fluent English; German is a plus, not a requirement
What this role is not
Not a people-management role on day one. You'll lead through the product, with a path to building a team as the org scales.
Not a strategy-deck job. If your best work lives in slides rather than shipped product, this isn't the fit.
Who You Are
You are slef-motivated, iterating fast, and eager for product success
You are a self-starter who takes ownership of a problem and makes sure it’s solved
You are comfortable with giving and receiving feedback for continuous improvement
A lifelong learner, someone who can dive into a domain quickly and become an expert
Data-informed, business-oriented and relentless in execution
We are an equal-opportunity employer and welcome applicants from all backgrounds, regardless of race, ethnicity, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, religion, age, disability, or any other characteristic. We believe that diversity drives innovation, creativity, and collective strength.

title: Senior Product Manager (m/w/d)
jd_text:
Deine Mission
Lieferplattformen versprechen Reichweite, binden Restaurants aber häufig in teure Abhängigkeiten mit hohen Provisionen und nehmen ihnen den direkten Kontakt zu ihren Stammkunden. Unsere Mission ist klar: FREE THE RESTAURANTS.
Mit unserem Webshop, unserer Plattform robin-cook.de und intelligenten Automatisierungen stellen wir die Restaurants wieder in den Mittelpunkt. Wir helfen ihnen, ihre eigenen Kundenbeziehungen zu stärken, unabhängiger zu arbeiten und profitabler zu werden.
Als Senior Product Manager (m/w/d) übernimmst du die Produktverantwortung in unserer entscheidenden Aufbauphase. Du verantwortest drei Themen parallel: Conversion auf Shop und Plattform, Akquise neuer Bestellungen pro Restaurant und Retention über unser Angebotssystem. Du startest mit voller Verantwortung für alle drei Outcomes gleichzeitig und gestaltest aktiv mit, wie wir diese Bereiche in drei eigenständige Produkt-Streams trennen. Dein nächster Entwicklungsschritt ist von Anfang an mitgedacht: Du übernimmst einen der Produkt-Streams als dedizierter Product Manager. Welcher Bereich das ist, entscheiden wir gemeinsam nach Stärke und Affinität.
Du arbeitest direkt mit Ben Reimer (Director Product und Operations) zusammen, bist zentrale Schnittstelle zwischen Engineering, Customer Success, User Experience und Geschäftsführung und sorgst dafür, dass jede Wette ein messbares Outcome liefert. Damit stärkst du direkt unsere Mission FREE THE RESTAURANTS.
Deine Aufgaben
Du verantwortest drei messbare Outcomes: A/B-Test-bewiesenen Conversion-Uplift, neue Bestellungen pro Restaurant pro Monat und Wiederbestellungen pro Restaurant-Besteller-Beziehung
Du steuerst mehrere parallele Initiativen über drei Sub-Streams hinweg, ohne den Fokus zu verlieren
Du führst Discovery-Prozesse von der unscharfen Beobachtung bis zur validierten Hypothese und übersetzt sie in A/B-Tests, deren Outcome du selbst misst
Du arbeitest in der Tiefe mit Daten: SQL auf ClickHouse, Cohort-Analysen, A/B-Test-Setup. Du delegierst das nicht, du machst es selbst
Du bereitest die strukturelle Trennung in drei eigenständige Streams aktiv vor: Sub-Outcomes separat messen, Code-Module sauber trennen, Backlogs parallel halten
Du arbeitest eng mit Customer Success, um Restaurant-Realität in Produkt-Hypothesen zu übersetzen, und mit dem User-Experience-Lead an Conversion-tauglichen Flows
Dein Profil
Mehrjährige Erfahrung als Product Manager in einem datengetriebenen B2C-Produkt, idealerweise im E-Commerce, Marktplatz oder Lieferdienst-Umfeld
Nachweisbarer Track-Record mit mehreren abgeschlossenen A/B-Test-Programmen und mindestens einem klar gemessenen Conversion-Hebel
Tiefe Daten-Kompetenz: SQL selbst schreiben, eigene Hypothesen in Daten validieren, Outcomes selbst messen
Discovery-Praxis aus Kundeninterviews, Datenanalysen und Hypothesen-Tests
Erfahrung in paralleler Steuerung mehrerer Initiativen mit unterschiedlichen Outcomes
Strukturelles Denken: Sub-Outcomes trennen, Code-Architektur lesen, Roadmap-Konflikte auflösen
Klare, direkte Kommunikation und die Bereitschaft, Entscheidungen verbindlich zu treffen
Restaurant-Domain-Affinität oder hohe Lernbereitschaft
Fließende Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse
Ob du regelmäßig bei uns im Büro in Bremen arbeitest oder überwiegend remote, können wir gemeinsam besprechen. Wichtig ist uns, dass du im Remote Modell ca. zweimal pro Monat bei uns vor Ort bist.
Diese Rolle passt zu dir, wenn …
du Erfolg an verändertem Kundenverhalten misst, nicht an gelieferten Features
du Probleme selbst findest, statt zu warten, bis sie auf deinem Tisch landen
du Daten und A/B-Tests selbst in die Hand nimmst, statt sie zu delegieren
du Aufbau-Phasen magst und Strukturen lieber selbst baust, als sie vorgefunden zu haben
Diese Rolle passt nicht zu dir, wenn …
du ab Tag eins einen klar abgegrenzten Produktbereich suchst
du lieber in bestehenden Strukturen arbeitestt, statt sie selbst mit aufzubauen
du Product Management eher über Features und Roadmaps definierst als

title: Senior Product Manager (d/f/m)
jd_text:
Personio's intelligent HR platform helps small and medium-sized organizations unlock the power of people by making complicated, time-consuming tasks simple and efficient. Our team of 1,500 Personios is building user-friendly products that delight our 15,000+ customers and their 1.5 million employees. Ready to make an impact from day one?
This role requires
2 days per week in the office
and is based in
Munich or Berlin
.
Join us to shape the future of software in the underserved and high-impact HR technology industry. Your work will have a direct and tangible impact on customers, offering ownership and the chance to make a meaningful difference. As we prepare for significant growth, you'll face exciting challenges and have the opportunity to influence our path toward becoming one of the world's leading tech companies.
We are looking for an experienced Senior Product Manager within Personio’s Payroll Product area. This area is responsible for correct calculation and communication of payroll data to German authorities, while ensuring user-friendly and compliant experience to support companies in a highly regulated and complex environment.
As a Product Manager for Payroll at Personio, you will shape the vision, strategy, and execution of our product’s automation, keeping Personio at the forefront of HRM and Payroll software in Europe. We believe in a low level of managerial overhead where our senior product people are hands-on in building. This is a strategic, cross-functional role where you will work closely with Engineering, Design, Compliance, Sales and Payroll Operations building a world-class Payroll software in Europe.
What You'll Do
Develop a deep understanding of the market as well as our customers, their pains and jobs-to-be-done
Systematically analyze user feedback and usage data gathered qualitatively through customer interaction and from quantitative sources
Create and align teams around strategic goals, relevant KPIs and a respective product roadmap often across Domain boundaries
Plan, enable, and execute against a product roadmap based on an objectively prioritized backlog that spans multiple teams
Lead specific complex topics within the product that extend across domain boundaries
Decompose complex problems in close collaboration with Engineering and Design counterparts and write clear specifications that drive direction
Manage product delivery in an agile setting and guide your team in all sprint activities
What You Need To Succeed
You speak German (native)
You have minimum of 5 years in product management within a B2B or B2C multi-product company, specializing in the development of global customer-facing products
Background in fintech, accounting software, or other compliance-heavy product domains is a plus but not required
You have a proven track record of navigating complex product areas, utilizing data, competitive intelligence, and user insights to define effective strategies and roadmaps
You have demonstrated success in conceptualizing, developing, launching, and scaling platform-backed products within a fast-paced environment
You have strong analytical and quantitative skills, with the ability to leverage data and metrics to support assumptions, formulate business cases, and establish OKRs
You have the ability to think big yet pay attention to details, exhibiting a strong bias for action and the capacity to manage multiple competing priorities, fostering a sense of urgency in a fast-paced, dynamic environment
You have deep sense of accountability and ownership, fostering collaborative, result-oriented work
Why Personio
Personio is an equal opportunities employer, committed to building an integrative culture where everyone feels welcomed and supported. We embrace uniqueness and understand that our diverse, values-driven culture makes us stronger. We are proud to have an inclusive workplace environment that will foster your development no matter your gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, rel

title: Product Owner - Onsite Experience (m/f/d)
jd_text:
Our mission
We are on a mission to save our planet - one second hand product at a time.
As Product Owner - Onsite Experience, you will own momox fashion’s web shop and all associated processes. You will collaborate with a cross-functional team of stakeholders and engineers to provide our customers with a best-in-class shopping experience.
Your mission
Product Strategy & Roadmap: Co-create a data-driven vision and product strategy and translate it into an actionable product roadmap. Define and prioritize initiatives based on business impact, customer value, and technical feasibility
Customer Centricity: Develop user-focused solutions using Design Thinking methodologies and collaborate with UX/UI teams to deliver intuitive, visually appealing interfaces
Testing and Analytics: Implement testing frameworks (e.g., A/B testing) and leverage analytics to optimize performance and identify opportunities for improvement
Collaboration and Lateral Leadership: Partner with cross-functional teams, including Engineering, Pricing, and Marketing, to ensure a seamless and consistent customer journey
Product Development: Manage technical requirements for product changes in close alignment with the Engineering teams
E-commerce Optimization: Oversee critical e-commerce functionalities, including product discovery, search, cart, checkout and payments, ensuring they meet localization and compliance needs
Growth Support: Collaborate with marketing to enhance customer engagement and retention through personalized experiences and data-driven strategies
Your profile
Experience: At least 5 years of experience as a Product Owner, Product Manager, or in a similar role, including experience in the e-commerce sector
Strategic Capability: Ability to align product strategies with business goals, particularly for internationalization, customer retention, and conversion
Technical Expertise: Experience in e-commerce product development, particularly search functionalities, conversion rate optimization, and personalization. Familiarity with backend and service integrations (e.g., recommendation engines, payment providers, search providers), and data-driven decision-making. Familiarity with Shopify is a plus
Methodological Proficiency: Skilled in agile methodologies, user testing, and ideation techniques, with experience using analytics tools like Google Analytics and Hotjar
Interpersonal Skills: Strong communication and presentation abilities, with experience in leading and motivating cross-functional teams
Analytical Acumen: Expertise in utilizing analytics tools to inform decisions and assess performance
Customer-Focused Approach: Ability to apply Design Thinking and user-centric methodologies to create innovative solutions that address customer needs and pain points
You can look forward to
Flexible working hours
We follow a hybrid work model with 20 in-office days per quarter to foster important personal interaction while enabling a modern, flexible way of working. The remaining days can be worked from home, and you also have the option to spend up to 6 weeks per year working from countries within EU. You’ll also benefit from flexible working hours to fit your individual needs.
Open work environment
We work together in a bright, open-plan office where even the few existing doors are always open. Our teams are motivated and open to new ideas. Bring your creativity to the table and make things happen! If you need a moment of peace, you can retreat to a cozy corner of the medimops library in the break room, or recharge with a game of foosball, table tennis, or PlayStation.
Training
momox is only as good as you. That's why you will find room and budget for your individual professional development with language classes, internal training, conferences, (online) courses and more.
Individual work environment
Design your ergonomic workspace just the way you like it – with height-adjustable desks and the laptop of your choice (MacBook or ThinkPad). If things get a bit no

title: Senior Software Product Manager (w/m/d)
jd_text:
Jobbeschreibung
Willkommen bei YPOG
Du hast Freude daran, in-house Software Produkte vom "green field" bis zur fertigen Software zu entwickeln und zu formen? Du arbeitest gerne in interdisziplinären Teams, um Lösungen zu schaffen, die die Bedürfnisse der Nutzer:innen erfüllen? Du willst die digitale Transformation der LegalTech Branche aktiv mitgestalten? Bei uns entstehen innovative Softwarelösungen, die echten Mehrwert für Mandant:innen und Kolleg:innen schaffen.
YPOG ist eine moderne Wirtschaftskanzlei, die ihre Rechts- und Steuerberatung mithilfe von künstlicher Intelligenz (KI) und Prozessautomatisierung transformiert. Mit KI verändern sich nicht nur Prozesse, sondern auch Prioritäten. Entscheidend ist, wie klar Anforderungen gesteckt, Ergebnisse bewertet und Expertisen früh eingebunden wird. KI unterstützt, der Mensch setzt den Rahmen. Als Teil unseres Tech + Automation Teams trägst du dazu bei, die Zukunft unserer Beratung durch die Implementierung und Fortentwicklung von künstlicher Intelligenz und Prozessautomatisierung neu zu denken.
Bei YPOG arbeiten wir in einer voll digitalisierten Umgebung, ein wertschätzendes Miteinander auf allen Ebenen steht im Mittelpunkt und flexible Zusammenarbeit gehört zu unserem Alltag. Wenn du eine Leidenschaft für Technologie und Produktmanagement hast und in einem dynamischen Umfeld arbeiten möchtest, freuen wir uns auf deine Bewerbung.
In dieser Position wirst du Teil unseres dynamischen Tech + Automation Team, das sich durch eine enge Zusammenarbeit und einen regen Austausch auszeichnet. Du wirst für mehrere Projekte gleichzeitig verantwortlich sein und dabei die Möglichkeit haben, deine Produktstrategie und Vision in die Tat umzusetzen. Im Team rund um Sabine kannst du an unseren Standorten in Berlin, Hamburg, Köln oder München durchstarten.
Dein Daily Business
Projektleitung und Kommunikation:
Leitung und agiles Projektmanagement in der Entwicklung und Implementierung unserer in-house KI-Applikationen, von der Konzeptphase bis zum internen Launch.
Stakeholder-Management, Kommunikation und Zusammenarbeit mit interdisziplinären Teams, um über den Projektfortschritt zu informieren und Feedback einzuholen.
Erstellung und Verwaltung von Projekt- und Zeitplänen sowie Budgets, um sicherzustellen, dass alle Meilensteine termingerecht erreicht werden.
Unterstützung bei der Weiterentwicklung von weiteren digitalen Produkten
Produktstrategie und -vision:
Entwicklung einer einheitlichen Produktstrategie und -vision für neue und bestehende in-house Software Produkte, die den Bedürfnissen der Endnutzer:innen entspricht.
Ausarbeiten von innovativen Lösungen in Zusammenarbeit mit den technischen Stakeholdern, u.a. dem Development Lead.
Durchführung von Marktanalysen und Wettbewerbsrecherchen, um Trends und Chancen zu identifizieren.
Erfolgsmessung und kontinuierliche Verbesserung:
Definition und Überwachung von KPIs, um den Erfolg unserer Entwicklungen zu messen und kontinuierliche Verbesserungen vorzunehmen.
Regelmäßige Erhebung und Analyse von Benutzer:innenfeedback (z.B. via Tracking), um zukünftige Produktverbesserungen zu planen.
Dein Weg zu uns
Technische Fähigkeiten:
Du hast fundierte Kenntnisse im Umgang mit Projektmanagement-Tools , bist aber gleichzeitig agil und offen für unbürokratische und kurze Entscheidungswege.
Du solltest ein gutes Verständnis für Benutzererfahrung (UX) und Benutzeroberflächendesign (UI) haben, um sicherzustellen, dass die Webanwendungen benutzerfreundlich und ansprechend gestaltet sind.
Von Vorteil ist Erfahrung in Projekten mit KI-Werkzeugen oder -Systemen, idealerweise mit einem Verständnis für Evaluierung von KI-Outputs, Akzeptanzkriterien und die Gestaltung von Human-in-the-Loop Prozessen.
Berufserfahrung:
Du hast mehrere Jahre Erfahrung im Software-Produktmanagement oder in einer ähnlichen Rolle, idealerweise in einem technologiegetriebenen Unternehmen.
Du hast mehrere Jahre Erfahrung in einer Führungsrolle (z.B. fachliche Führung) gesammelt und deine Fähig

title: Product Manager / Product Owner Charge Point Management System
jd_text:
Univers provides the world’s most comprehensive energy management and decarbonization system.
We help companies and countries optimize their energy systems and reduce carbon emissions with accurate, reliable, and actionable decarbonization data. Our EnOS (Energy and Environment Operating System) platform connects on-the-ground operational technology and in-the-cloud intelligence to deliver real-time energy control, monitoring, reporting, and carbon savings.
With 400 million devices connected, 1050GW of renewable energy under management, and a community of over 500 customers, we’re helping the world’s leading businesses get the world to net zero—and what comes after it.
For more information, please visit
https://univers.com/
Product Manager Charge Point Management System:
As Product Manager for our Charge Point Management System (part of the EnOS suite), you own a product at the center of Univers' EV charging business — from workplace and fleet operators to public CPOs and multi-country enterprises. You will translate market signals, customer needs, and regulatory shifts into a clear product roadmap, and you will drive delivery with an international R&D organization.
Responsibilities
:
Product Strategy & Roadmap: Own the long-term vision and prioritize the development backlog based on market and customer needs, regulatory requirements (OCPP, OCPI, ISO 15118, MaStR, etc.) and business objectives.
Domain Expertise: Act as the subject matter expert for charging infrastructure standards, driver applications, control interfaces and backend integrations.
Delivery: Manage international R&D resources across geographies, keep specifications and timelines current, and ensure features land on time and to spec.
Documentation and Coordination: Maintain accurate and up-to-date documentation related to product specifications, requirements, and project timelines. Coordinate communication and meetings between various stakeholders to ensure alignment and progress. Manage international R&D resources and ensure feature delivery.
Go-to-Market Execution: Develop pricing and packaging strategies, and enable sales and customer support teams prior to product releases.
Customer Loop: Run close feedback cycles with sales, operations, and customer support across pre-sale, commissioning, and post-sale phases. Turn field issues into product improvements.
Competitive Analysis: Monitor competitor products and industry trends to understand market dynamics and identify potential areas of differentiation or improvement for our products.
Analytics & Performance: Define and track KPIs for uptime, transaction volumes, revenue, user adoption and general product performance.
Qualifications & Experience:
Essential:
Master's degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, Energy Systems, or Industrial Engineering (Wirtschaftsingenieurwesen). Equivalent professional experience in EV charging or energy software considered can be considered
Proficiency and strong background in cloud-based backend systems and charging relevant protocols
Proven track record as a Product Manager or Product Owner, shipped software products, not just documented them.
Excellent written and spoken English; strong communication across engineering, commercial, and executive audiences.
Ability to operate across cultures and time zones (our R&D is international).
Analytical mindset with the ability to interpret data and make data-driven decisions
Passion for technology and innovation, with a desire to contribute to the success of cutting-edge products
Desirable:
EV charging market experience (CPO, eMSP, OEM, or platform side).
Familiarity with ISO 15118 / Plug & Charge, Hubject roaming, or smart-charging use cases (PV, BESS, dynamic tariffs).
German language skills.
Why Join:
You'll shape a product operating at the intersection of EV charging, energy management, and AI-driven optimization with real customers, deployments, and a roadmap that includes advanced Energy Management integrations. If yo

title: Technical Product Manager
jd_text:
📍 Location:
Berlin (Remote)

We're partnering with an innovative games company looking for a
Technical Product Manager
to take ownership of the frontend game engine that powers multiple live titles. This is a highly technical product role where you'll shape the roadmap, work closely with engineering teams, and influence the future of the company's game technology.
What you'll be doing:
• Own the product roadmap and backlog for the Frontend Game Engine
• Drive product development from concept and MVP through to production release

• Experience delivering technical products from idea through to successful release

• Remote working with flexible hours
• Modern office in central Berlin
• Structured onboarding programme
• Ongoing learning and development opportunities
• Company health insurance and wellbeing initiatives
• Bike leasing scheme
• Collaborative culture with genuine opportunities to influence technical direction
If you're interested in building the technology behind successful games rather than working on a single title, I'd love to tell you more.
📩 Apply directly or send me a message for a confidential discussion.

title: Product Manager Software Intelligence
jd_text:
Your mission
Join us on a journey where you can thrive in both your career and personal life.Own the day-to-day product work for Matrix42 SaaS Management (Viio), and help shape the broader Software Intelligence vision that unifies our SAM portfolio. It's a high-ownership role at the intersection of SaaS and software asset management, hands-on product craft, and modern AI — focused on a live, growing product today, with a clear path to influence the platform we're building next.
Drive prioritization and roadmap for Matrix42 SaaS Management (Viio), and contribute to the broader Software Intelligence portfolio
Work directly with enterprise customers and partners to shape product direction
Explore, define, and validate features through rapid, AI-enabled prototyping and experimentation
Partner closely with engineering, design, data, marketing, and go-to-market teams
Define success metrics and build ROI narratives that resonate with IT leaders and decision-makers
Help shape what Software Intelligence looks like in an AI-native world
Your profile
Experience in SaaS Management, ITAM, or SAM software
Customer-facing product management experience in enterprise B2B SaaS
Strong product sense and creative problem-solving, with a hands-on approach to prototyping
Ability to communicate fluently across C-level, sales, and engineering audiences
Familiarity with asset management platforms and the use cases they serve
Why us?
This is a great opportunity to contribute to the future of a leading Service Management software vendor and drive innovation in a cloud-first, AI-powered ecosystem. If you have the technical expertise, architectural vision, and collaborative mindset to make an impact, we’d love to hear from you!
By using our platform, customers can manage IT and business processes, assets, endpoints, and identities for improved productivity, agility, security, and employee experience. By enabling digital working environments and IT self-service through holistic integration and automated processes, we digitalize and automate our customers’ everyday tasks. Join our diverse team of 600 professionals spread across Europe.
Our Benefits
Our culture is built on strong values, with customer success as our top priority. We support your personal and professional growth, helping you reach your full potential through personalized goals.
We could start by mentioning the fundamentals – 30 vacation days, flexible self-managed working hours, and a hybrid setup that supports remote work. But what truly sets this role apart is the opportunity to be part of a distinct European growth journey – shaping the European choice in Service Management that is a trusted and loved player in the market, while contributing to a company that is scaling with clear purpose, ambition, and customer focus.
Your contact person
Send us your application
IN ENGLISH
, including your salary requirements and earliest possible starting date, directly through our online portal via the
"APPLY NOW"
button. If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact
Diane Djongoue.
We would like to inform you that MATRIX42 can only accept applications online via the applicant portal in connection with our applicant management system because of the currently valid EU data protection regulations.
We are an equal opportunity employer. We welcome applications from all genders and do not discriminate on the basis of gender, gender identity, age, disability, ethnic origin, religion or belief, or sexual orientation.

title: Senior Product Manager (m/f/d) Incremental Revenue & Personalization
jd_text:
The booking is confirmed — and that’s when the real sales opportunity begins. As Product Manager (m/f/d) Personalization, you shape how SIXT increases revenue after the booking by delivering the right offer to the right customer at the right moment. You own the personalization strategy across three core revenue streams: vehicle upsells, protection products, and extras. This complex field involves rich reservation data, diverse customer segments, and multiple revenue streams to orchestrate. Your challenge is to simplify this complexity into clear strategies and sharp concepts that succeed on a global scale. Join Team Orange in our headquarters in Pullach close to Munich!
YOUR ROLE AT SIXT
You own the personalization sales strategy across vehicle upsells, protection products, and extras — defining which offers reach which customers, when, and through which post-booking touchpoints
You develop segment-specific concepts that reflect real differences between family trips, partner bookings, corporate rentals, leisure travelers, and groups — because the right upgrade or protection depends entirely on context
You think through the full post-booking journey — confirmation, pre-arrival, check-in, on-rent — identifying the perfect moment for each revenue stream to engage each segment
You collaborate closely with other Product Managers to align personalization logic across web and app post-booking touchpoints
You distill complex data signals, customer segments, and revenue streams into clear, actionable strategies that teams can understand and execute
You work with business stakeholders across sales, revenue management, and operations to keep your concepts commercially solid and operationally feasible
YOUR SKILLS MATTER
Experience
Several years in digital product management or e-commerce with proven success driving revenue through personalized or segment-based product strategies
Product Craft
You understand digital product development and user experience — able to read customer journeys, detect offer mismatches or flow issues, and communicate what good looks like
Clarity of Thought
You absorb complex, data-rich environments and translate them into simple, structured, easily understandable strategies that are straightforward to implement
Commercial Thinking
You grasp how vehicle upsells, protection products, and extras generate revenue and naturally think about growing each via smarter, relevant offers
Segment Intuition
You quickly understand that different customer types have fundamentally different needs and build strategies that tailor to those distinctions
Data Literacy
You work confidently with data scientists and analysts, engaging with reservation data and customer behavior at a level that makes you a credible partner, even without technical expertise
Collaboration
You communicate clearly and confidently with PMs, UX Designers, and business teams, bringing people along on complex topics
AI Fluency
You integrate AI tools into daily work — using large language models to explore strategies, draft concepts, prototype ideas rapidly, and leverage AI-driven personalization logic without building it yourself
WHAT WE OFFER
Extra benefits
Enjoy discounts on SIXT rent, share, ride and SIXT+, attractive vehicle leasing offers, and exclusive deals with partners for travel, tech, fashion and more
AI at your fingertips
From day one, you'll have access to Claude by Anthropic and SIXTgpt, SIXT's own AI platform, to supercharge your work. We invest in every team member's AI journey: encouraging experimentation, building knowledge, and making sure you're ahead of the curve
Mobility Boost
You will receive a monthly allowance from us as a contribution to your individual mobility
Future security
We contribute to your retirement plan and support you with capital-forming benefits to ensure you are well covered
Feel-good atmosphere
Stay active with our modern SIXT gym, various leisure activities like the gaming area or the SIXT choir, and enjoy our high-qua
