title: Business Growth Analyst (Madrid based)
jd_text:
Hey there!
We’re Fever, the world’s leading tech platform for culture and live entertainment.
Our mission? To democratize access to culture and entertainment. With our proprietary cutting-edge technology and data-driven approach, we’re revolutionizing the way people engage with live entertainment.
Every month, our platform inspires over 300 million people in +55 countries (and counting) to discover unforgettable experiences while also empowering event creators with our data and technology, helping them scale, innovate, and enhance their events to reach new audiences.
Our results? We’ve teamed up with major industry leaders like Netflix, F.C. Barcelona, and Primavera Sound, presented international award-winning experiences, and are backed by several leading global investors! Impressive, right?
To achieve our mission, we are looking for bar-raisers with a hands-on mindset who are eager to help shape the future of entertainment!
Ready to be part of the experience?
Now, let’s discuss this role and what you will do to help achieve Fever’s mission.
Meet the Team
The
Marketing & Growth
team is one of the largest and most dynamic at Fever, playing a central role in driving visibility, engagement, and business impact across the company. We work closely with nearly every department, combining strategy, creativity, and data to connect millions of people with unforgettable experiences.
Join us if you want to drive global impact, master data-driven creativity, and help millions of people discover their next unforgettable experience.
The Role
As a
Growth Analyst
, you will independently lead high-impact global projects and strategies to drive Fever's entertainment market expansion across up to 40 countries. By designing end-to-end solutions and managing new business streams, you will collaborate across departments to enhance overall company performance.
What You'll Do
In this role, you will contribute to one of Fever's key markets, focusing on its growth and development.
Being a Growth Analyst at Fever means working independently and managing your own pipeline of projects within a business line where you can showcase the value you bring.
Take part in the exponential growth of fever by taking the lead on global projects that will define the future of the entertainment industry
Enhance the overall company performance across different business departments which work together in the creation of new plans and events
Define and establish new strategies to increase performance using analytical, business and strategic skills
Design end-to-end solutions with daily high impact
Create, develop and manage new projects like new business streams, workflows, strategies, and specific departments' growth
Work with people and projects across different markets, with a special focus on developing new experiences across markets, in a big international atmosphere (up to 40 countries)
Who You Are
At least 2 years of experience in an analytics & business role.
Background in Engineering or Maths/Statistics/Data Analytics/Strategy.
Leverage big data and analytics tools to make strategic business decisions.
Excellent communication skills and proven team leadership experience.
Excited about working in a fast-paced and constantly evolving environment.
Being an advanced English speaker is a must, and if you speak Spanish, French, Italian, or Portuguese, it will be a plus.
Passionate about industry trends and eager to explore new growth opportunities.
Join us and be a part of a dynamic and innovative team that leverages cutting-edge technology and industry trends to drive growth and success!
Why You'll Love It Here
Attractive compensation package with room to grow.
40% discount on all Fever events and experiences + free Candlelight voucher.
Flexible remuneration with Cobee: meals, transport, training, and nursery support (tax-exempt).
Payflow: option to access part of your salary in advance.
Taxdown: help optimizing and filing your taxes.
Private health & dental insuranc

title: Commercial Strategy Manager (gn) bei SAMSUNG
jd_text:
Für unseren Kunden SAMSUNG 🌍📱 suchen wir einen
Commercial Strategy Manager (gn)
in Eschborn📍
Nutze deine Chance bei einem der größten Elektronikkonzerne der Welt! ⭐
Das sind deine Aufgaben:
Planung, Steuerung und Koordination nationaler und kanalspezifischer Promotions zur Steigerung des Abverkaufs und Erreichung der KPIs
Analyse von Promotions (ROI, Uplift, Effizienz, Effektivität) und Ableitung konkreter Optimierungsmaßnahmen
Unterstützung performance- und marktbasierter Projekte (z. B. Planung von Produktneueinführungen)
Erstellung eines kennzahlbasierten Quartalsplans auf Produkt- und Kundenebene unter Einbindung von Produktmanagement, Vertrieb und SCM
Analyse und Planung relevanter KPIs (Brutto-/Nettoumsatz, Sell-In/Sell-Out, Bestand, Investment Rate) sowie Präsentation an Stakeholder inkl. Top-Management
Identifizierung von Performance-Potenzialen in Accounts und Empfehlungen für Vertriebsaktivitäten
Enge Zusammenarbeit mit Vertrieb, Marketing und externen Partnern
Das bringst du mit:
Betriebswirtschaftliches Studium oder vergleichbare Ausbildung mit statistischem/datenanalytischem Hintergrund
Mind. 5 Jahre Berufserfahrung in Finance, Sales Planning, SCM, Trade Marketing, Marktforschung, Datenanalyse o. Ä.
Erfahrung in der Analyse komplexer Daten sowie überzeugende Präsentation der Ergebnisse
Strukturierte, eigenverantwortliche und lösungsorientierte Arbeitsweise; proaktiv und kommunikationsstark
Sehr gute MS-Office-Kenntnisse, insbesondere Excel und PowerPoint
Deutsch und Englisch fließend in Wort und Schrift
Fähigkeit, bereichsübergreifende und strategische Fragestellungen schnell zu erfassen und für Entscheidungsträger aufzubereiten; konzeptionell und inhaltlich stark
Ausgeprägte Kenntnisse im kommerziellen Umfeld Consumer Electronics sowie technisches Grundverständnis der zugeordneten Produktpalette
Erfahrung in der Zusammenarbeit in virtuellen, bereichsübergreifenden Teams (international von Vorteil)
Hohe Eigenmotivation, sehr gutes Zahlenverständnis, zuverlässig und teamorientiert
Situativ angemessene Kommunikation über verschiedene Hierarchie- und Kompetenzlevel, auch in Konfliktsituationen
Dich erwartet:
⏳ Ein spannender Einsatz für 18 Monate (Arbeitnehmerüberlassung)
🕒 37 Stunden Woche
🌴 30 Urlaubstage
💶 Vergütung 66.000 € - 73.000 € Jahresgehalt
🏠 Bis zu 2 Tage Homeoffice/ Woche
🎁 Corporate Benefits
🅿️ Mitarbeiterparkplatz
Neugierig geworden?
Dann bewirb dich jetzt mit deinem Lebenslauf.
Wir freuen uns auf deine Bewerbung!
Dein Ansprechpartner bei Grafton:
Melanie Roya - Senior Talent Acquisition Specialist
📧melanie.roya@grafton.com

title: Business Analyst / Product Manager (all gender)
jd_text:
Wir sind die Gestalter von heute für die Welt von morgen.
Als einer der weltweit führenden Entwicklungsdienstleister setzen wir uns bei ALTEN dafür ein, die Zukunft unserer Partner, die Karrieren unserer Mitarbeiter und die Herausforderungen für unsere Gesellschaft und Umwelt positiv zu gestalten.
Mehr als 50.000 Mitarbeiter in 30 Ländern arbeiten schon heute an innovativen Lösungen in verschiedenen Ingenieur- und IT-Branchen wie Luft- und Raumfahrt, erneuerbare Energien, Medizintechnik, Bahntechnik und Automobil.
Stellenbeschreibung
DU…
fungierst als zentrale Ansprechperson für alle SPDM-Themen
koordinierst die Zusammenarbeit zwischen Fachbereichen, Plattformteams und weiteren Stakeholdern
stellst sicher, dass die richtigen Ansprechpartner in Entscheidungen und Aktivitäten eingebunden werden
analysierst und optimierst Prozesse im Umfeld von 3DEXPERIENCE (3DX) und SPDM
verstehst ARDs und Prozessdiagramme und leitest daraus fachliche Anforderungen ab
schaffst Transparenz über Abhängigkeiten, Anforderungen und laufende Aktivitäten
Qualifikationen
DU…
hast ein erfolgreich abgeschlossenes Studium der Wirtschaftsinformatik, Informatik, Wirtschaftsingenieurwesens, Maschinenbaus, Systems Engineering oder einer vergleichbaren Fachrichtung
verfügst über fundierte Kenntnisse der 3DEXPERIENCE-Plattform (3DX)
hast idealerweise Erfahrung im Umfeld von SPDM
besitzt ein gutes Verständnis für End-to-End-Prozesse sowie deren Analyse und Optimierung
überzeugst durch starke Kommunikations- und Stakeholder-Management-Fähigkeiten
verfügst über fließende Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift
arbeitest strukturiert, analytisch und eigenverantwortlich
Wir wertschätzen Vielfalt und begrüßen daher alle Bewerbungen – unabhängig von Geschlecht, Nationalität, ethnischer und sozialer Herkunft, Religion / Weltanschauung, Behinderung, Alter sowie sexueller Orientierung und Identität.
Schwerbehinderte werden bei gleicher Eignung bevorzugt berücksichtigt.
Bewirb dich jetzt über unser Online-Bewerbungsformular.
Zusätzliche Informationen
Talent Management – wir entwickeln deine Karriere
Work Life Balance – flexible Arbeitszeiten und mobiles Arbeiten möglich
Fit and relaxed – mit EGYM Wellpass
Enjoy biking – immer on tour mit Dienstradleasing
Green Mobility – mit uns bist du vergünstigt unterwegs
…zudem bieten wir einen unbefristeten Arbeitsvertrag, Corporate Benefits und Teamevents.

title: Business Development Manager – Energy & Flexibility
jd_text:
You turn charge points into valuable flexibility. Every contract you close makes the grid a little more balanced, and Deftpower's Virtual Power Plant a little bigger. Join Deftpower!
About Deftpower
At Deftpower, we’re shaping the future of electric charging every single day. Our SaaS-platform helps businesses across Europe make charging simpler, smarter and more sustainable. With AI and intelligent software, we make sure charging works seamlessly with the energy grid, creating a greener and more stable system for everyone.
We’re growing fast, thanks to a recent major investment, and there’s never a dull moment! We’re expanding, improving our technology, and teaming up with companies in over ten countries.
About The Team
You join the commercial side of Deftpower, where Business Development, Sales, Product and Operations work closely together to accelerate the adoption of smart charging across Europe. This role sits on the demand side of our flex ecosystem: you win CPOs (AC, DC/HPC and home/private) as flex-demand partners, turning the flexibility already sitting on their networks into something we can monetise and share back with drivers. You report to Lucas, Head of Sales.
For you
At Deftpower you’ll find more than cutting-edge tech, you’ll find a team that actually enjoys the ride! Colleagues say we are ambitious, friendly and flexible, with just the right amount of chaos to keep things interesting. Working at Deftpower is both rewarding and challenging. There are many moving parts, partners, suppliers, clients and we are a fast growing company that is still learning.
You get support from talented colleagues and a management team that’s close, approachable and ready to roll up their sleeves. You get the space to shape your role, to take initiative, to make real decisions, test ideas, and yes, sometimes break things. We call it 'learning fast'...
Salary range: between €60.000,- and €90.000 on an annual basis
25 vacations days
Choose your own laptop to work with (€1.750,-)
A flexible setup: hybrid working, short decision lines and trust
€2.000,- training budget a year
About The Job
As Business Development Manager for Flex Partnerships, you turn charge point operators, DSOs and TSOs into flex-demand partners. You identify which players have the most to gain from steerable load and you use Deftpower's existing flexibility playbook, cashbacks, off-peak rewards, OCPI steering, to build the commercial case that gets contracts signed.
This is a role for someone who enjoys long, technical sales cycles with multiple stakeholders and who can hold their own across commercial, technical and energy-market conversations. You bring a strong narrative, sharp negotiation instincts, and the patience to time demand-side growth against what our driver base can actually deliver.
Map and prioritise partners with the most to gain from a deal
Build the commercial case and negotiate the contract that gets them on board
Represent Deftpower in industry conversations, from first pitch to signed agreement
Share what CPOs and flex buyers actually need with Product, so you keep selling something we can deliver
Explore flex buyers (BRPs, aggregators, energy traders) as a next market for aggregated flexibility
Manage signed accounts together with Account Management, keeping the partnership on track
About You
You know how to get a contract signed with a technical, commercially cautious counterpart. Not just the pitch, the actual negotiation: pricing structures, milestones, mutual commitments. You combine that with enough energy-market literacy to speak credibly about grid constraints, dynamic tariffs and demand charges.
You have 5+ years of experience in business development, partnerships or commercial negotiation, ideally in energy, e-mobility or a related regulated market
You understand energy-market and regulatory complexity (grid-operator requirements, balancing markets, BRP relationships) well enough to translate it into a commercial proposition
You have

title: Business Development Manager (m/f/d)*
jd_text:
About Us
Within SCHOTT’s Strategic Business Field Specialty Glass Solutions, you will drive systematic business development for high-performance glass applications worldwide. By translating market insights, technologies and proven concepts into scalable growth initiatives, you will play a key role in strengthening SCHOTT’s global market position reporting directly to the division manager.
Your Contribution
Drive the strategic development of new business opportunities, markets and applications in close collaboration with the division management, Sales, Marketing and Product Management
Conduct and synthesize market analyses, market studies and sales data to identify growth levers, risks and opportunities
Develop business cases, business models and go-to-market strategies for new products, technologies or regions
Lead cross-functional, international projects from concept to implementation (e.g. market entry, product expansion, regional growth initiatives)
Transfer successful business development concepts and methodologies across countries, regions and applications
Monitor project progress and success using defined KPIs, providing structured reporting to management and key stakeholders
Your Profile
Academic background in Business Administration, Engineering or a comparable field, ideally combining business methodology with strong technical affinity
Extensive professional experience in business development, product management, technical sales or a related strategic function
Proven ability to work in a highly structured and systematic manner, translating complex topics into scalable concepts
Strong analytical skills with experience in market studies, data analysis and strategic planning
Ability to engage and align diverse stakeholders across functions, cultures and regions
Excellent command of English; confident working in an international environment
Willingness to travel internationally
Your Benefits
SCHOTT’s openness to promote your further development is just as great as our additional benefits. Ranging from active health management via flexible working hours and hybrid work all the way to corporate pension plans: everything geared to suit your goals and needs. Discover a special corporate culture where everyone has the opportunity to develop their entire potential.
At SCHOTT, you can expect a unique corporate culture where we emphasize equity, diversity, and inclusion. We know: motivated and committed employees are the precondition for the success of our company.
Subsidized pension plan; Holistic corporate health management; International assignments; Talent development
Please feel free to contact us
You can expect interesting tasks and challenging projects, as well as motivated and friendly teams in fields that influence our future. Do you want to be successful with us? Then, please send us your application: SCHOTT AG, Human Resources, Eva Lemke,
At SCHOTT, it’s your personality that counts – not your gender, your identity, or origin.

jd_text:
About Us
We are seeking a dynamic (
Junior) Strategy Manager
with a strong focus on the development and operationalization of our
strategic agenda

market
and

,
internal performance analysis
, and
high-level financial modeling

levering
AI tools

potential solutions

seamless communication
and
collaboration across all levels
. You will play a key role in ensuring the successful execution of our strategies. As a trusted sparring partner to the CPSO and senior leadership team, you will contribute to shaping the future direction of Allianz Direct.

Support the operationalization of Allianz Direct’s strategic agenda, both in terms of long-term ambitions and short to mid-term priorities
Drive and/or coordinate strategic initiatives jointly with the senior leadership team, with a focus on performance analysis (external, internal), scenario modelling, and synthesizing results on top management level to support their decision making

Qualifications / Key Skills
High analytical and conceptual aptitude combined with strong project management skills

Ability to analyze complex information and data, translating insights into actionable recommendations

Education & Experience

Confidence in daily interaction with senior management, demonstrating expertise in managing strategic projects and initiatives
Strong proficiency in PowerPoint and Excel
Experience in using AI tools across the entire spectrum of tasks, i.e. from research activities to final delivery creation

We’re Fever, the world’s leading tech platform for culture and live entertainment.
Our mission? To democratize access to culture and entertainment. With our proprietary cutting-edge technology and data-driven approach, we’re revolutionizing the way people engage with live entertainment.
Every month, our platform inspires over 300 million people in +55 countries (and counting) to discover unforgettable experiences while also empowering event creators with our data and technology, helping them scale, innovate, and enhance their events to reach new audiences.
Our results? We’ve teamed up with major industry leaders like Netflix, F.C. Barcelona, and Primavera Sound, presented international award-winning experiences, and are backed by several leading global investors! Impressive, right?
To achieve our mission, we are looking for bar-raisers with a hands-on mindset who are eager to help shape the future of entertainment!
Ready to be part of the experience?
Now, let’s discuss this role and what you will do to help achieve Fever’s mission.
Please submit CV in English**
Meet The Team
At Fever, our
Business Development team
is at the heart of the marketplace, making the discovery of amazing things to do effortless. We are responsible for
sourcing, curating, and growing top experiential content
across global markets, from immersive pop-ups to headline cultural events.Our mission is to bring the
best experiences
to the platform while helping
partners grow their portfolios
, increase turnover, and achieve long-term success. This means working closely with creators, producers, agencies, and cultural leaders to scale their projects through Fever.
Collaboration is key: we partner daily with Marketing, User Support, Category Management, Lead Generation, Partner Support, and Sales Ops to ensure every experience added to the marketplace reaches its full potential.
Join us if you want to source world-class content, build long-term partnerships, and help
scale the most exciting experiences
across global markets.
The Role
We are looking for an exceptional, entrepreneurial
Senior Market Strategy & Partnerships Manager
to lead and scale our presence in the
DACH
region
. This is a high-impact role at the intersection of business development, operations, and strategy. You will
own the market end-to-end
: from originating and structuring landmark partnerships to ensuring flawless execution on the ground.
What You'll Do
Create and cultivate deep business relationships with the biggest producers, creators, venues and key stakeholders in the entertainment industry.
Develop and deliver a growth strategy for the market, with an innovative and effective approach to scaling Fever's presence in the region
Expertly manage commercial strategy with large partners ensuring value creation for both parties, and a sustainable strategic partnership between Fever and event organizers
Evidence strong organizational skills to effectively balance and grow a large base of clients
Collaborate closely with cross functional stakeholders to successfully deliver on partner growth across the DACH region and internationally
Keep up to date with everything cool that’s happening in the entertainment; think of yourself as the expert for new and trending experiences
Who You Are
5+ years of total professional experience, with at least 3 years in top-tier strategy consulting (MBB) or a strong entrepreneurial / commercial background (founder, early-stage operator, or senior business development manager)
Proven track record leading high-stakes commercial initiatives, partnerships, or revenue-driving projects in fast-paced environments
Strong sales and partnership development ability - comfortable owning a pipeline, negotiating high-value deals, and managing senior stakeholders
Exceptional strategic thinking, structured approach, and analytical skills
Self-starter, highly autonomous, and thrives in a dynamic and high-growth environment
Excellent communication skills in German an

title: Senior CRM Strategy Manager, Reactivations (all genders)
jd_text:
The role
Are you passionate about re-engaging customers and driving growth through personalized marketing? As the
Senior Audience Manager for Reactivations
, you will play a critical role in re-engaging the HelloFresh cancelled base globally. Between leveraging customer segmentation or overlooking market performance, you will be designing customer journeys that nurture and rebuild relationships with cancelled customers, while being accountable for driving reactivation results that contribute to Customer Lifetime Value (CLV).
This role goes beyond crafting and optimizing campaigns, it’s about
taking part in the development of the overarching reactivation strategy
, to help shape the vision for customer-centric journeys, and ensuring the delivery of industry-leading segmented campaigns that nurture, strengthen, and rebuild relationships with our canceled customer base.
You will create a seamless and impactful reactivation experience, collaborating closely with Creative, Marketing Analytics, CRM Operations, Pricing & Discounts, and Brand - with particular emphasis on creative development and innovation - to develop a comprehensive communication strategy for your segment(s).
Above all, we are looking for people who will
make HelloFresh
better.
We believe there are many different ways of developing skills and we love diverse experiences! So even if you don’t “tick all the boxes” but think you’d thrive in this role, we would really like to learn more about you.
What You’ll Do
Customer Journey Ownership: Focus on reestablishing a relationship with cancelled customers, using personalized and data-driven approaches to create tailored E2E customer journeys.
Communication Strategy: Develop detailed communication strategies for different segments, including channel definition, message timing, and creative direction. Our channel pool includes Email, Push Notifications, SMS, Direct Mail and Call Centre Outbound.
Managing Market Performance: Own a defined set of markets and be accountable for reaching Business Plan targets across Conversion, spend, and CVA. Monitor performance regularly, identify gaps, and plan incremental initiatives to achieve targets.
Performance Optimization: Track and optimize key KPIs including Click-Through Rates, Conversion Rates, and overall profitability. Use data insights to continuously refine the effectiveness of customer journeys with a focus on driving Incremental Reactivations and CLV.
A/B Testing: Design and execute A/B testing strategies to refine messaging, timing, and offers, continuously improving performance metrics.
Collaboration: Work closely with Creative, Product Marketing, Marketing Analytics, CRM Operations and local teams to align strategies, measure performance, and develop creative materials tailored to the customer.
Creative Oversight: Partner with the Creative team to ensure all communication touchpoints deliver product value and resonate with the target audience.
Innovation: Drive and collaborate with teams exploring Automations, Machine Learning and AI applications. Share insights to help identify areas where these tools can improve efficiency and personalization.
What You’ll Bring
Proven Experience: 5+ years in a marketing role, with a focus on CRM, lifecycle marketing, customer journey design or audience segmentation, preferably working with subscription business models.
Strategic and Commercial Thinking: Ability to design and execute customer journey strategies while owning business targets across markets. You're comfortable monitoring performance, identifying gaps, and driving initiatives to hit Conversion, spend, and CVA goals.
Data-Driven Execution: Deep understanding of CRM performance metrics (CTR, Conversion Rate, ROI) and Experimentation, with a track record of A/B testing, segmentation, and continuous optimization.
Collaboration and Communication: Proven success working with creative, analytics, operations, and brand teams to deliver results. You can translate strategy into clear

title: Product Manager (m/f/d)
jd_text:
Company Description
Everyone’s story matters. Come shape your story with us at Riverty.
But where does that take you?
To one of our 30 hybrid workspaces – designed for exchanging ideas, learning from others, and shaping the way we work. An international community of over 4,000 people, representing almost 80 nationalities across 12 countries. United by one mission: Combining empathy, advanced technology and data-driven insights to keep people and businesses in flow. With payments made for them. So that they don’t have to worry about it.
And there’s more.
We are part of the family-owned Bertelsmann group. Established. Corporate. In a fast-paced industry. We enable flexible payments in various industries, simplifying the financial management of known brands and helping people repay debt to build financial confidence. In short: shaping FinTech.
Job Description
We are looking for a
Product Manager Conversational AI (m/f/d)
(unlimited, fulltime) Join our team at our locations in Berlin, Verl or Baden-Baden - flexible working conditions available
Millions of people each year end up in difficult financial situations. How they are treated in those moments matters, and technology can make it better.
As a Product Manager for Conversational AI within our BU Collection, you'll define how AI-powered experiences across chat, voice, and digital portals help people resolve debt in ways that are faster, clearer, and less stressful.
This is a builder role: you'll work hands-on with a cross-functional team spanning Platform Engineering, AI Engineering, Operations, Data, Compliance, and Corporate AI leadership, bringing structure, experimentation, and momentum to a domain that's still taking shape.
Your Responsibilities Will Be
Define and drive the product vision, strategy, and roadmap for Conversational AI across all consumer interaction channels
Analyze and prioritize key journeys (payment, installment, dispute, status inquiries) to maximize digital self-service and conversion
Bring clarity and ownership into a fragmented domain by establishing a structured product setup
Design and run experiments to validate use cases quickly, ensuring only the highest-impact bets get built without waiting for perfect conditions on interlinked systems
Ensure smooth end-to-end execution from discovery to launch and performance optimization
Define and track key performance metrics to drive continuous improvement across live channels
Coordinate cross-functionally with Platform Engineering, AI Engineering, Operations, Data, Compliance, Customer Service, and Corporate AI
Qualifications
What you bring:
Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Product Management, Information Systems, AI, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience
3–5+ years of product management experience in AI-driven products, B2C digital platforms within fintech or other regulated industries
Strong stakeholder management skills, with experience navigating complex international organizations
Ability to bring structure and prioritization to ambiguous environments with fragmented ownership
Affinity for conversational AI technologies (LLMs, NLU, chatbots, voicebots) and backend/platform constraints
Business fluent English is required; German skills are a plus
Additional Information
Disclosure requirements pertaining to the collection of your personal data:
Responsible for processing the information provided in your application is the company specified in the job advertisement, with its registered office as indicated. The company processes your data for the purpose of establishing an employment relationship on the basis of Art. 6 (1) b GDPR / Section 26 (1) sentence 1 BDSG.
The retention period for your data is determined by the statutory time limits applicable in the respective country, beginning upon completion of the recruitment process. You can find these here.
You can contact the company’s Data Protection Officer at the above-mentioned postal address.
Further information on data prote

title: Senior Product Manager - PDF Expert
jd_text:
This is a role that Atlas, our AI Recruiter, is hiring for on behalf of one of our customers. He'll pick the best candidates from Nova's network.
The next step is to speak to Nova.
The Role
Job Title:
Senior Product Manager - PDF Expert
Location:
Berlin, Germany
Why this role matters
With over 30 million users globally, PDF Expert is a flagship product poised for its next major evolution: integrating powerful, intuitive AI-driven features. We need a Senior Product Manager to own this critical product area end-to-end, driving high-impact discovery and shipping features that redefine mobile and desktop productivity. Without you, we cannot move fast enough to capture the massive opportunity of AI-powered document workflows across iOS, Mac, and Web.
What You'll Do
End-to-end product development for a core PDF Expert product area across iOS, Mac, and Web.
Lead discovery and identify high-impact product opportunities based on user research and analytics.
Run a dedicated cross-functional squad of 5 engineers, a designer, and a product analyst.
Spearhead the strategy, prototyping, and shipping of next-generation AI-powered productivity features.
Design and execute A/B tests and experimentation frameworks to optimize user funnels and engagement.
Align engineering, design, and analytics stakeholders to drive initiatives from raw idea to launch.
What success looks like
Month 1:
You will onboard with your squad, establish your relationship with the Head of Product, and dive deep into PDF Expert's existing mobile UX, user research, and data funnels.
Month 2-3:
You will take full ownership of your squad's backlog, design your first core product experiments, and begin prototyping high-impact AI features or workflow enhancements.
By end of Q1:
You will have successfully shipped your first major feature or experiment to our 30 million users, establishing a clear, data-backed roadmap for the next wave of AI-powered productivity tools.
Who This Is a Fit For
Proven B2C mobile-first product manager who has shipped highly engaging consumer apps.
Experienced leading cross-functional squads of engineers, designers, and data analysts.
Highly analytical, fluent in A/B testing, user funnels, and product analytics tools.
Expert in user research, translating deep customer empathy into actionable product requirements.
Adept at using AI-prototyping, vibe-coding, or productivity tools to automate workflows.
Thrives in fast-paced, autonomous environments without needing corporate structure or hand-holding.
What it's like to work here
At the company, the team balances focused, high-ownership execution with a collaborative, customer-centric culture. The day-to-day work rhythm relies heavily on asynchronous communication to respect focus time and streamline collaboration, keeping meetings intentional. Structured touchpoints like weekly team standups and regular product demos keep everyone aligned on goals. Decision-making is collaborative yet decisive, empowering individual owners to move quickly while utilizing user-research, analytics, and experiment results to resolve disagreements.
The offer
Based in Berlin, Germany with a highly collaborative, international team.
True ownership of a flagship product with a massive scale of 30M+ global users.
A focus-friendly culture prioritizing asynchronous communication, intentional meetings, and high-ownership execution.
This probably isn't for you if
You prefer slow-moving corporate environments, heavy committee-based alignment, and endless meetings.
Your product experience is exclusively B2B, enterprise, or back-end, rather than consumer-facing (B2C) mobile.
You prefer executing on pre-defined specs rather than navigating ambiguity, conducting user research, and setting your own roadmap.
Who are Atlas & Nova?
I'll go first. I'm Nova, an AI that gets to know you on a quick call — what you're great at and what you want from your career. Then I help you land your dream job: surfacing roles you'd never want to miss, and ba

The PM will lead a cross-functional squad to identify high-impact opportunities through user research and A/B testing. The role requires navigating ambiguity and delivering actionable product requirements for a B2C application.

title: Team Lead Product (all genders)
jd_text:
About Us
Our mission at Avelios is to unlock clinical data to power seamless healthcare operations for better patient care. To do so, we have built a modular software platform that digitizes and optimizes workflows in hospitals with cutting-edge technology in a user-friendly way. With our software, we enable hospitals, doctors & nurses to provide their patients with the best possible care.
We are growing fast and want to keep expanding our team and business to fundamentally digitize healthcare for the better. We appreciate different backgrounds and see diversity as one of our strengths.
THE TEAM AND ROLE
As Team Lead Product you will take ownership of a key product area within our platform. Your will lead an interdisciplinary team that combines Product Managers and Medical Managers who bring together product expertise and deep clinical domain knowledge. Your role is to build a high-performing, well-organized team, establish clear standards for discovery, specification, and release processes, and ensure that clinical workflows, customer requirements, and regulatory obligations translate into a coherent, cross-customer roadmap. You set the operational and strategic foundation for scalable, high-quality product development in one of the most critical parts of our platform.
Your Responsibilities
Lead, coach, and develop a team of Product Managers and Medical Managers
Own and maintain an integrated roadmap across the modules you are assigned to and set the vision for the future of intelligent patient care
Ensure overall consistency in function and user experience across integrated modules
Introduce and oversee review processes and quality gates to ensure clinical requirements, workflows, internal and external subject matter expertise and regulatory inputs are systematically converted into high-quality product artefacts
Drive alignment with Engineering, Design, and the Project team to ensure consistent delivery, structured handovers, and coherent release planning to increase efficiency, quality and planning reliability
Coordinate and drive product launches and enablement to ensure successful adoption
Your Qualifications
Degree in a STEM field (Science, Technology, Engineering (incl. Computer Sciencce), or Mathematics) or Medical Informatics, Health Sciences, Medicine
5+ years of experience in product management roles ideally in a B2B SaaS or platform environment, including roadmap ownership, prioritisation and successful product launches
2+ years of disciplinary leadership experience of a team >4
Experience in building teams (scaling, establishing roles and processes and creating a culture of trust and ambition)
Ability to incorporate the expertise of others rather than imposing one's own “professional authority”, breakdown complexity into manageable workstreams, and help junior team members to approach problems
Nice to have: Experience working in regulated industries (healthcare, MedTech, clinical software), Knowledge of clinical workflows and requirements for documentation
Strong command of English
Your Benefits
Variety in your tasks and the opportunity to take ownership
An international, informal team structure in a motivating start-up atmosphere, allowing you to participate directly in our customer growth story and the overall success of Avelios
Ability to gain valuable hands-on experience in a startup backed by leading international VCs
Support for your personal development through continuous learning and feedback
Set your own rhythm with flexible working hours and trust-based time tracking
A virtual stock option package (VSOP) that lets you participate in Avelios’ long-term value creation
A monthly meal allowance up to 75 euros, full coverage of the Deutschlandticket, Urban Sports Club or a subsidized Wellpass membership, a bike leasing program and Corporate Benefits that give you access to discounts from a wide range of providers in various areas
summary: A Team Lead Product role managing a team of Product Managers and Medical Managers to build a modular software platform for hospitals. Responsibilities include defining the roadmap, ensuring consistent user experience, and managing release processes. The position emphasizes cross-functional collaboration to translate clinical workflows into high-quality product artifacts.

title: Senior Product Manager, Supply Incentives
jd_text:
About Bolt
Bolt is shaping a future where cities are built for people, not cars. What started as an ambitious project of a 19-year-old in 2013 has grown into a global mobility platform used by more than 200 million customers and 4.5 million driver and courier partners across 50 countries.
From ride-hailing and food delivery to scooters, e-bikes, and car-sharing, Bolt helps people move through cities every day. Small, autonomous teams drive this work, combining the speed and ownership of a startup with the scale of a global technology company backed by more than €1bn in funding.
And we're just getting started.
Why join Bolt?
Build the future of mobility and help transform how people live and move in cities.
Tackle challenges at scale that span technology, operations, regulation, and growth.
Take real ownership early. Small, lean teams mean your decisions matter from day one, and you'll learn fast alongside some of the best people in the industry.
Join one of Europe's leading technology companies, backed by world-class investors including Sequoia.
About The Role
As a Senior Product Manager, Supply Incentives, you will define and execute the roadmap for supply-side incentive programs, working with a cross-functional team of engineers, data scientists, designers, and operations partners. You will analyse driver behaviour and market dynamics to identify opportunities, design incentive structures that are economically sustainable and motivating, and ship the features that bring those programs to life.
You will contribute to the broader Rides group strategy and define measurable success metrics, including P&L-level business impact. You will serve as the internal expert on supply economics, regularly presenting insights and recommendations to senior leadership. You will also oversee the delivery of cross-functional initiatives, taking responsibility for implementation and identifying areas for improvement across the business.
Main Tasks And Responsibilities
Defining product strategy and roadmap for supply incentives, aligned with Rides group objectives and driver needs.
Analysing supplier behaviour, market dynamics, and competitive landscape to identify opportunities and shape incentive design.
Designing and running experiments with analytics partners to evaluate incentive effectiveness and drive product decisions.
Collaborating with cross-functional teams — engineering, data science, growth, operations, and design — to take products from concept to launch.
Prioritizing features based on driver needs, business economics, and data insights, tracking key metrics to measure performance and guide iteration.
Building scalable incentive systems and lead product development processes, while serving as the go-to expert on supply economics and incentive strategy.
About You
You have 5+ years of product management experience, with at least 2 years in marketplace, mobility, platform, or incentives-related domains.
You have strong analytical skills — comfortable building business cases, interpreting experiments, and collaborating with data teams; SQL/Python is a plus.
Your track record includes launching and scaling products, shipping 0→1 and driving meaningful outcomes in fast-paced tech environments.
You understand marketplace dynamics and can design systems that balance competing stakeholder needs, such as driver satisfaction versus business economics.
Your communication skills are exceptional, enabling you to align diverse stakeholders: from engineers to senior leadership.
You have a bias toward action, thrive in ambiguous environments, and use AI tools fluently as a multiplier for speed and quality.
Why you’ll love it here
Accelerate your professional growth with unique career opportunities.
Enjoy a rewarding salary and stock options, knowing that as Bolt succeeds, so do you.
Take care of your physical and mental health with our wellness perks.
Celebrate 5 years at Bolt with a 1-month paid sabbatical to recharge.
Connect with colleagues at an
summary: A Senior Product Manager role at Bolt focused on defining the roadmap for supply-side incentive programs. The PM will analyze driver behavior, run experiments, and design incentive structures to balance marketplace dynamics. Cross-functional collaboration with engineering, data science, and operations is critical to product success.

title: Lead Product Management (all genders)
jd_text:
Want to make a difference?
Als
Lead Product Manager (all genders)
übernimmst Du die Verantwortung für eine zentrale Produktdivision und gestaltest aktiv mit, wie unsere Kollektionen Marke, Kund*innenbedürfnisse, kommerziellen Erfolg und Verantwortung zusammenbringen. Gemeinsam mit Design, Merchandise Planning, Supply Chain, Sales und Marketing entwickelst Du Produktstrategien, die unsere Brand stärken und nachhaltiges Wachstum ermöglichen.
Mit Deinem Gespür für Konsument*innen, Märkte, Sortiment und Performance triffst Du Entscheidungen, die weit über einzelne Produkte hinaus Wirkung entfalten. Du denkst strategisch, handelst unternehmerisch und führst Dein Team mit Klarheit, Ownership und dem Anspruch, starke Produkte erfolgreich auf den Markt zu bringen.
Wer wir sind?
Seit 2007 beweisen wir bei ARMEDANGELS, dass Mode auch anders geht: innovativ und zeitlos, produziert nach verantwortungsvollen Standards für Mensch und Erde. Keine Lohnsklaverei, keine schädlichen Chemikalien, keine billige Massenware. Aus der einfachen Idee, T-Shirts zu bedrucken und über den Verkauf Spendengelder für Projekte und Organisationen zu sammeln, ist ein Unternehmen gewachsen, das heute zu den größten nachhaltigeren Lifestyle Labels Europas zählt. Mit einem 190-köpfigen Team und vier Kollektionen im Jahr zeigen wir bei ARMEDANGELS, dass sich Verantwortung und gutes Produktdesign nicht ausschließen.
Mit diesen Aufgaben trägst Du zum Erfolg bei
Du entwickelst und verantwortest die Produktstrategie sowie den kommerziellen Erfolg Deiner Division und steuerst Sortimente über alle Kollektionen hinweg.
Du führst und entwickelst Dein Team und arbeitest eng mit Design, Merchandise Planning, Supply Chain, Sales und Marketing zusammen.
Du bringst Consumer Insights, Marktverständnis und Performance-Daten in Produktentscheidungen ein und steuerst Forecasting, Sortiment, Pricing und Margen.
Du analysierst Performance über alle Kanäle, identifizierst Wachstumschancen und leitest Maßnahmen ab.
Du entwickelst Sortimentsarchitektur und Produktstrategien kontinuierlich weiter und beobachtest relevante Markt- und Konsument*innentrends.
Das solltest Du mitbringen
Mehrjährige Führungserfahrung im Product Management, Buying, Merchandising oder Category Management – idealerweise in der Fashion- oder Lifestylebranche.
Starkes kommerzielles und analytisches Verständnis sowie Erfahrung in der Entwicklung erfolgreicher Produktstrategien.
Fundiertes Know-how in Sortimentsplanung, Pricing, Margen, Forecasting und Product Lifecycle Management.
Erfahrung in Produktentwicklung, Sourcing und der Zusammenarbeit mit internen und externen Stakeholdern.
Sicherheit im Umgang mit verschiedenen Vertriebskanälen sowie ausgeprägte Kommunikations- und Führungsstärke.
Erfahrung im Menswear-Bereich ist ein Plus, aber kein Muss.
Leidenschaft für nachhaltige Mode und den Wunsch, Verantwortung zu übernehmen.
Darauf kannst Du Dich freuen
Inspirierendes Arbeitsumfeld: Arbeite in einem innovativen Unternehmen, das so nachhaltig wie möglich agiert und ständig neue Maßstäbe setzt.
Flexibilität: Arbeitszeiten, die auf Vertrauen basieren, und die Möglichkeit, hybrid zu arbeiten.
Teamgeist: Freue dich auf fulminante Feste mit großartigen Kolleg*innen, ein Büro mit viel Geschichte und Liebe zu Hunden (die im Büro herzlich willkommen sind).
Benefits: 50€ Sachbezug pro Monat über guudcard, Einkaufsgutscheine bei ARMEDANGELS, tolle Mitarbeitendenrabatte, ein Jobrad und/oder Deutschlandticket sowie eine betriebliche Altersvorsorge.
Support: Unser Employee Assistant Program steht dir unabhängig vom Arbeitgeber in jeder Lebenssituation beratend zur Seite.
Come as you are: Kein Dresscode – komm einfach, wie du bist (Kleidung ist natürlich notwendig!).
Ein paar Tipps zur Bewerbung
Vielfältige Interessen, Mut und Drive, die Dinge anzupacken, sind bei uns sehr willkommen.
Bewirb Dich bitte online über den Bewerbungs-Button und ergänze in Deiner Bewerbung die Infos:
Deine Pronomen & ggf. Wunschname
Deinen CV
Dein fr

title: Product Owner / Product Manager (m/f/d) (AI Factory)
jd_text:
29 countries. Around 49,000 colleagues. 70,000 pharmacies supplied and millions of patients: Join PHOENIX! Support us in supplying the people of Europe with medicines and healthcare products quickly and reliably - and experience a job that is as diverse as it is important. Where you can proactively master challenges, implement ideas and drive change. Look forward to pure variety at a successful, future-proof family company, the market-leading pharmaceutical wholesaler in Germany and at the same time the best integrated healthcare provider in Europe.
In our Competence Center IT& Digital we are looking for a
Product Owner / Product Manager (m/f/d) (AI Factory)
. This role can be filled
at our headquarters in Mannheim
.
Your Tasks
You own the product vision & roadmap for your data and AI products and ensure alignment with PHOENIX business strategy and AI Factory goals.
You are responsible for the financial performance, value delivery, and growth of your data and AI products, including accountability for P&L impact where applicable.
You manage multiple AI use cases/products at varying stages (ideation, MVP, scaling), ensuring alignment with customer needs and business priorities.
You proactively promote and position AI solutions internally, engaging with new business units and helping secure buy-in for use case collaboration.
You define and prioritize the product backlogs, manage epics and user stories with a focus on delivering measurable outcomes.
You establish and maintain productive relationships with your customers, ensuring business value is central throughout the development lifecycle.
You collaborate with agile delivery teams (Agile Coaches, Data Scientists, Engineers) to drive product development.
You ensure transparency and effective communication about your data and AI products, both within the team and with key stakeholders.
You support governance activities, by contributing to portfolio reviews, stakeholder updates, and AI Factory reporting.
Your Profile
Proven experience as a Product Owner or Product Manager, ideally in corporate AI/ML environments or data-driven products.
Strong understanding of Agile methodologies, including Scrum or Kanban.
Experience managing multiple products or workstreams simultaneously in complex, matrixed organizations.
Strong stakeholder management and internal consulting skills, with the ability to build trust and influence across diverse business units.
Experience with AI/ML product development is a strong advantage (but not mandatory).
Communication Skills: Excellent verbal and written communication abilities, capable of conveying complex concepts to diverse audiences.
Analytical Mindset: Proficient in analyzing data to drive product decisions and improvements.
Educational Background: Bachelor's or master's degree in business, Economics, Computer Science or a related field; advanced degrees or certifications (e.g., CSPO, PSPO) are a plus.
Fluency in English; German is a plus.
Our Benefits:
Future-proof: We are growing and expanding. We are the market leader and offer you a permanent employment contract, vacation pay as well as 30 days of vacation and much more. (job bike, company pension scheme, etc.)
Reconciliation of private and professional life: We offer mobile office and flexible working time models.
Career: We promote you! There are great opportunities within the PHOENIX Group.
Continuing education: We would love to - we are happy if you are eager to learn. The PHOENIX Learning World offers you an exclusive range of further training courses.
Team: This is how we work. We promote team cohesion through small and large events.
Please apply online including your salary expectation and your earliest possible starting date. Your contact person is Erik Sabo.
We are looking forward to your application!

title: (Senior) Product Manager (m/f/d)
jd_text:
Experience HELM as a unique family business and an attractive employer. Through a constant exchange of ideas, our ultimate goal is to offer our customers future-oriented solutions within our global network. At any time, in any place. This allows us to work proactively and think long-term - in operations, quality management, process optimization, product development and distribution. We are innovation drivers, possibility makers and a reliable partner, all in one.
(Senior) Product Manager (m/f/d)
Date: Jun 21, 2026
Location:
Hamburg, DE, 20097
Business Unit: Chemicals
Job Function: Product Management
Level of Experience: Mid-Career
For our Business Unit Chemicals within EU Industrial Chemical Solutions Europe, we are looking for a motivated Product Manager (m/f/d). In this role, you will become part of a versatile team with an international focus across the European region.
Your tasks:
Together with the team, you will be responsible for the strategy and expansion of marketing activities in Europe. The portfolio includes products that we source globally, such as diethyl oxalate, adipic acid, or para tertiary butylphenol, as well as commodities like methanol or acids & alkalis. Your task is to further develop these products and contribute your own ideas about markets, products, and industry trends in order to generate additional business opportunities for the company.
In addition, you will take on the modeling and monitoring of purchasing and sales activities, as well as the management of key markets and major key accounts together with our European sales teams.
In this role, you act as an interface between our customers and suppliers and, thanks to your networking and relationship‑management skills, you are able to create added value for our company.
Furthermore, you will support the global budgeting and strategy development process by preparing market‑ and product‑specific analyses and market information.
You will also actively contribute to the implementation of HELM’s sustainability strategy (decarbonization, circular economy, and bio‑based chemicals).
Your profile:
You hold a bachelor’s degree in business administration or have completed a comparable qualification. You have already gained experience in trading, procurement, marketing, or sales — ideally in the chemical industry or distribution.
You possess knowledge in negotiating purchasing or sales contracts and understand the risks and opportunities associated with a contract portfolio.
You are a highly flexible and open‑minded person who easily adapts to a changing environment.
Various stakeholders consider you a reliable and trustworthy personality, as you combine strong entrepreneurial thinking with the ability to make quick decisions.
You are open to existing and new business contacts and demonstrate a team‑oriented, responsible, and resilient personality.
Professional English skills are essential, and you are familiar with common IT applications (MS Office, Salesforce, SAP S4).
Every day, each one of us contributes to HELM's success with competence, the highest expertise and unique service. We reward this commitment - not only with international opportunities, possibilities for seminars and further training but also with benefits that offer optimal conditions to reach the perfect work-life balance.
Interested?
Join our motivated team and help shaping the future of HELM!
Even if you do not fulfil all of the requirements in our profile, we encourage you to apply.
It is important to us to find the right people who will enrich our team with their motivation and personality!
We look forward to receiving your application with all the necessary documents via our application portal.

title: Junior Technical Product Owner (f/m/d) - Data
jd_text:
Remote in our day-to-day work. On-site when it matters.
We work remote by default – focused, efficient, and with full ownership. For larger features, architectural decisions, and real brainstorming sessions, we come together in Berlin – fast, hands-on, and without unnecessary meeting overhead.
We use AI to accelerate – not to replace thinking.
We design the system, steer the output, and take responsibility for what we ship.
Fast where it makes sense. Careful where it matters.
Your mission
Own the backbone of our data services. You don’t need to know everything – but you make sure things work, scale, and stay reliable.
As a
Junior Technical Product Owner (f/m/d)
, turn complexity into clarity and help us build stable, scalable software.
Translate business needs into clear, actionable technical requirements for engineering teams
Collaborate closely with Data Engineers: refine requirements, define test cases, and continuously learn about architectures and data flows
Keep the bigger picture in mind: guide features from initial idea through development, rollout, and ongoing operation
Use your digital toolkit (Git, Jenkins, monitoring dashboards, etc.) to maintain visibility and control over system health
Bring structure to complexity and help move services from “working” to “reliable at scale”
Your skills
We value the right mindset over perfect experience – bring curiosity, ownership, and a willingness to learn.
Hold a degree in (Business) Informatics or a related field
Bring initial experience as a Technical Product Owner / Product Manager – or demonstrate strong technical affinity and the ambition to grow into the role
Develop a solid technical understanding: concepts like TDD, ETL, and monitoring tools (e.g. Grafana, Graylog) should not be unfamiliar
Communicate effectively in an agile environment: understand developers, speak their language, and translate problems into clear solutions
Work independently, take ownership, and focus on pragmatic solutions
Communicate clearly and honestly – and don’t shy away from asking the right questions
Nice to have:
Experience working with data, exposure to microservices, or familiarity with the AdTech environment — if not, we’ll help you get there.
Why you’ll love working with us
Ownership over tickets:
You’re trusted with real responsibility, not just tasks. No unnecessary bureaucracy, no micromanagement – we rely on you to take things forward.
Build > Talk:
We test what works – not what sounds good. Fail fast, learn faster.
High standards, low ego:
We take our work seriously, but not ourselves. Direct feedback, honest collaboration, no drama.
Stay sharp:
Hackathons, conferences, community – we invest in your growth and keep you at the cutting edge.
And yes – the fundamentals are covered too:
30 days of vacation + Dec 24 & 31 off, Smart Fridays (4 days week possible), mobility (Germany ticket & JobRad), sports & health offerings, mental health support, corporate benefits, RTL+ access, and more.
Your CV is just the starting point.
What matters more to us than your resume: a portfolio, a side project, a demo repo – anything that shows you don’t just talk about code, you ship it. Production-ready. Thought through. Done.
Welcome to smartclip! We’re just your average group of techies, you know, trying to keep the internet open and defend the European TV industry from Silicon Valley’s giants. Join us!
About smartclip
smartclip is the adtech development unit of RTL Group — Europe’s leading free-to-air broadcaster group. Our proprietary advertising technology is custom-built for the needs of European broadcasters and publishers — enabling media owners to implement smarter monetisation strategies. We are committed to delivering the most innovative ad experiences spanning in-stream, out-stream, addressable TV, connected TV, audio, and gaming — ultimately empowering brands with true cross-screen storytelling opportunities on all devices.
For more information: Please visit www.smartclip.tv and follow us

title: Senior Product Manager, Routing & Live Traffic
jd_text:
About Bolt
Bolt is shaping a future where cities are built for people, not cars. What started as an ambitious project of a 19-year-old in 2013 has grown into a global mobility platform used by more than 200 million customers and 4.5 million driver and courier partners across 50 countries.
From ride-hailing and food delivery to scooters, e-bikes, and car-sharing, Bolt helps people move through cities every day. Small, autonomous teams drive this work, combining the speed and ownership of a startup with the scale of a global technology company backed by more than €1bn in funding.
And we're just getting started.
Why join Bolt?
Build the future of mobility and help transform how people live and move in cities.
Tackle challenges at scale that span technology, operations, regulation, and growth.
Take real ownership early. Small, lean teams mean your decisions matter from day one, and you'll learn fast alongside some of the best people in the industry.
Join one of Europe's leading technology companies, backed by world-class investors including Sequoia.
About The Role
We're looking for a technical and strategic Senior Product Manager to own Bolt's Routing and Live Traffic domain - the core infrastructure that powers accurate ETAs, fair pricing, and efficient dispatch across every ride.
Routing and traffic prediction must be fast, precise, and reliable for millions of trips across dozens of markets. You will own Bolt's routing APIs, ETA models, and live traffic products: the shared infrastructure that matching, pricing, rider and driver applications all depend on.
This is a Platform team role requiring close partnership with data science, engineering, mapping, matching, and pricing teams.
Main Tasks And Responsibilities
Own the strategy, roadmap, and delivery of Bolt's routing APIs, ETA adjustment models, and live traffic products end-to-end.
Drive quality improvements across routing KPIs - ETA accuracy, distance precision, and traffic prediction - and define how success is measured.
Build and evolve live traffic models that identify and predict the evolution of congestion using Bolt's internal movement data.
Understand and manage the effects of routing changes on Bolt marketplace.
Identify commercialisation opportunities for Bolt's traffic data in underserved markets.
About You
You have experience building and scaling routing, traffic, location technology, or other complex platform products in fast-paced environments.
You have knowledge of routing engines, live traffic models, and dynamic road event systems, with the ability to solve technically complex product problems.
You work effectively with Engineering, Data Science, and Machine Learning teams to improve model performance, accuracy, and product outcomes.
You use data to evaluate trade-offs and make informed product decisions that maximise customer and business impact.
You recognize how routing quality influences marketplace performance, including matching efficiency, pricing accuracy, and operational reliability.
You communicate clearly with technical and business stakeholders, translating complex concepts into actionable product priorities.
Why you’ll love it here
Accelerate your professional growth with unique career opportunities.
Enjoy a rewarding salary and stock options, knowing that as Bolt succeeds, so do you.
Take care of your physical and mental health with our wellness perks.
Celebrate 5 years at Bolt with a 1-month paid sabbatical to recharge.
Connect with colleagues at annual company events and smaller team gatherings.
Balance flexibility and in-person collaboration with our hybrid model, including at least 12 monthly in-office days.
Some perks may differ depending on your location and role.
At Bolt, using AI daily is a baseline expectation for everyone. If there’s a faster, smarter way to do your work, we expect you to find it. You’re still responsible for the output, so the standard stays high when using AI tools.
Experience is great, but what we really look for i

title: Senior Product Manager, Supplier Portal
jd_text:
Change the way the world travels
Join the GetYourGuide journey to connect people with unforgettable travel experiences around the world. Millions look to us for unique activities they can trust, and it’s all powered by our commitment to make every single journey extraordinary - including yours.
Ready to unlock your potential with a community of fellow explorers? Find your next role at our Berlin HQ or one of our local offices around the globe, from New York to Bangkok. Head to getyourguide.careers to take the first step.
Team mission
Tens of thousands of suppliers run their businesses on GetYourGuide from inside the Supplier Portal, and every Supply product team reaches them through it. That makes the portal the most connected surface in all of Supply, and the one most at risk of fragmenting as team after team builds into it.
This team exists to make sure it doesn't happen. We own the foundations everyone else builds on, the design system, navigation, communication and we own how the whole thing feels to use: how suppliers get set up, find their way around, stay on top of what's new, and get unstuck when they need help. When we get it right, the payoff lands twice. Every Supply team ships faster on top of us, and suppliers get one coherent product instead of the seams between the teams behind it.
Your mission
As a Senior Product Manager, you will own this product area end-to-end. We're looking for someone who can hold a coherent twelve-month vision for the portal as a platform while making the supplier experience measurably better today.
Set the strategy for the Supplier Portal as both a platform and a coherent supplier experience, translating it into clear priorities, measurable outcomes, and an explicit view of what you will not do.
Own the coherence of the cross-portal supplier experience, raising the bar on how suppliers navigate the portal, stay informed of what's new, and help themselves when they're stuck, with the help experience growing more intelligent over time.
Own the shared foundations the rest of Supply depends on, and make them the default other teams reach for rather than rebuild.
Use data and supplier insight to find the highest-leverage opportunities, measure impact, and iterate based on outcomes.
Your toolkit
5+ years of product management experience, with a track record of owning a product area with genuine autonomy at a fast-paced tech company.
Experience with platform, design-system, or internal-facing products, where your customers include other product and engineering teams, and your impact shows up in their velocity and consistency.
Strong analytical skills: you define the right success metrics and make evidence-based prioritization calls.
Hands-on AI fluency: you actively use LLMs and AI tools in how you work and build, so you can judge where they genuinely unlock better outcomes
Proven ability to lead cross-functional, multi-stakeholder work and build alignment across teams you don't own.
Experience with B2B product, marketplace platforms, or supplier/partner-facing tools is a strong plus.
Excellent written and verbal communication in English.
How We’ll Make Your Career Journey Extraordinary
Annual personal growth budget and mentorship programs for continuous learning and development
Work from anywhere in the world for 30 days per year
A hybrid working approach with three days of in office collaboration (Mon, Tue, Thur) and two days of optional at home focus time.
Opportunities to collaborate and socialize with team members through quarterly team events and yearly company-wide events
Monthly transportation and fitness budget
Discounts for you, your friends, and family on GetYourGuide activities
Language reimbursement program
Health and wellness benefits
And more…
How To Apply
Submit your CV/resume in English using the form below. For tips and insights into our hiring process and culture, check out ‘how we hire’ and ‘life at GetYourGuide’. If you have any further questions, please don’t hesitate to g

title: Mobile Maintenance Product Manager (Human)
jd_text:
We move big things and are constantly in motion ourselves. So that it stays that way, we are looking for you. Together we make Europe's leading company for wagon rental and innovative logistics solutions even more successful. We are looking forward to meet you! You can find more information about current job offers at vtg.de/karriere
For our headquarters in Hamburg, we are looking for you as Mobile Maintenance Product Manager (Human) at the earliest possible start date.
Would you like to actively shape a business-critical function in a technical environment? In this strategic role, you will take responsibility for the “Mobile Maintenance” product in rail freight operations.
You will define how mobile maintenance is deployed, the value it creates, and how it sustainably improves wagon availability, safety, and fleet performance.
The position is clearly strategic in nature – you will steer through concepts, data, and stakeholders, without being operationally involved in maintenance activities.
What are your responsibilities?
Development of a clear product strategy and a robust roadmap
Definition of service offerings, deployment models, and intervention boundaries of mobile maintenance
Steering and monitoring of relevant KPIs (e.g. safety, availability, quality, costs, customer satisfaction)
Responsibility for the cost structure and value proposition of mobile maintenance compared to workshop services
Ensuring compliance with safety regulations, ECM requirements, and technical standards
Translating customer requirements into clearly defined and deliverable service offerings
Continuous development through standardisation, optimisation, and innovation in mobile maintenance
What qualifications do you have?
Experience in maintenance, asset management, or technical service environments, ideally in industries such as rail, logistics, aviation, energy, agriculture, or other asset-intensive sectors
Background in product management, asset strategy, or service development
Good understanding of complex technical systems and their lifecycle as well as maintenance requirements
Knowledge of safety requirements, regulatory frameworks, or technical standards is an advantage
Strong analytical and commercial understanding, particularly in dealing with cost structures and business cases
Ability to work across functions and successfully engage and manage different stakeholders
Experience in service-based business models or working with fleets/assets is a plus
Ideally, knowledge of CMMS or EAM systems, as well as an interest in data-driven optimisation
Very good English skills, written and spoken; please submit your application in English
What benefits do we offer?
Flexible working hours and the option to work remotely
Diverse training opportunities for your professional and personal development
Attractive additional benefits, such as meal subsidies, accident insurance and contributions to your company pension
Mobility benefits through financial support for the Germany Ticket and JobRad
Shopping benefits through our Corporate Benefits program
Fitness and wellness through membership in the Urban Sports Club
Employee referral program with attractive rewards
Health-focused services, including flu vaccinations, back therapy, skin screenings and psychosocial support
Are you keen on tasks that move? Shape the future of VTG with us and experience exciting challenges and genuine cooperation in motivated teams. We look forward to your know-how and your enthusiasm!

title: Product Manager (f/m/d) Discovery & Development
jd_text:
Inside
Innovation in therapy and diagnosis - that is what we are committed to.
For more than 20 years, leading medical technology companies worldwide have trusted NDI as the market leader in 3D tracking technology. Leveraging our high-tech expertise, we develop advanced digital solutions for modern medical applications.
With offices in Germany, Canada, the United States, and Hong Kong, we operate in a truly international environment defined by short decision-making paths, open communication, and a strong team culture. Diversity, trust, and passion shape the way we work — we know each other by name, support one another, and celebrate our successes together.
Tasks
We are seeking a dynamic and passionate Product Manager to contribute to our team, reporting to the Director of Global Product Management. In this pivotal role, you will spearhead initiatives crucial to the success of a new product line, shaping its direction and growth. Working closely with cross-functional teams, you will craft and implement product strategies that not only meet market demands but also fuel revenue growth, making a tangible impact on the healthcare industry.
Leading the formulation and implementation of product strategies for a cutting-edge product line
Comprehensive market research and analysis to identify customer needs, recognize market trends and understand the competition
Collaborate with product managers, engineers, clinicians, and other stakeholders to define product requirements, features and the roadmap
Ensure product launch success by collaborating with Marketing on go-to-market activities and strategies, including pricing, positioning, and messaging
Take a leadership position in the further development of medical navigation technology
Profile
3–5 years of experience in Product Management, ideally within medical devices, technical hardware, or another complex regulated environment
Experience in medical navigation markets, such as electrophysiology, orthopaedic & spine surgery, neurosurgery, interventional oncology, surgical robotics, or image-guided interventions
Demonstrated success in product discovery and development for complex technical hardware
Bachelor’s degree in Business, Science, Engineering, or a related field
Strong analytical and strategic thinking, with the ability to translate insights into actionable strategies
Professional fluency in written and spoken English
Kontakt
Ready to make an impact?
We look forward to your application (in english), stating your earliest possible start date and salary expectations.
NDI Europe GmbH
Human Resources
Güttinger Straße 37
78315 Radolfzell
www.ndieurope.com

title: Produktmanager (m/w/d)
jd_text:
JENZ ist führender Hersteller von Maschinen zur Aufbereitung von Biomasse. Bei uns steht der nachhaltige Umgang mit unserer Umwelt und die Nutzung erneuerbarer Energien im Fokus. Wir produzieren hochmoderne Maschinen, die für die effiziente Zerkleinerung von Holz- und Grünabfällen sowie Biogassubstraten eingesetzt werden.
Was uns besonders macht?
Wir sind ein mittelständisches Familienunternehmen: Bei uns kannst du mitgestalten und etwas bewegen! Du hast Lust dich einzubringen?
Werde Teil unserer #jenzcrew als:
Produktmanager (m/w/d)
DEINE AUFGABEN
Für unsere Produkte (Großholzhacker & Biomasseaufbereiter) übernimmst du folgende Aufgaben:
Verantwortlicher Produktmanager (m/w/d) für die zugewiesenen Maschinentypen und Begleitung dieser über den gesamten Produktlebenszyklus
Entwicklung von Maschinenkonzepten und Initiierung von Produktverbesserungen auf Basis von Wettbewerbs- und Markt- und Zielgruppenanalysen
Marktexperte und Ansprechpartner für die Abteilung Konstruktion und Entwicklung
Business Development für den Bereich der Biomassezerkleinerung
Unterstützung des Vertriebs bei der Vermarktung vorhandener und neuer Produkte
Erarbeitung von Markteinführungskonzepten (Marketing und Vertrieb)
Erstellung von Schulungs- und Informationsunterlagen
Durchführung von Vorführungen und Produktschulungen
Begleitung des Vertriebs bei speziellen Kundenbesuchen
Teilnahme an Messen
Durchführung und Koordination von Maschinenerprobungen
Durchführung interner Produktschulungen
DAS BRINGST DU MIT
Erfolgreich abgeschlossenes Ingenieurstudium oder eine vergleichbare Ausbildung
Erste Erfahrung im Produktmanagement oder ähnlichen Bereichen
Gutes technisches Verständnis sowie Kenntnis von Produktentwicklungsprozessen
Hohe Kundenorientierung, analytisches Mind-Set sowie ein ausgeprägter Gestaltungswille
Engagement, Leistungsbereitschaft sowie Kommunikations- und Präsentationsstärke
Ausgeprägte Hands-on-Mentalität
Gute Englischkenntnisse
Sicherer Umgang mit Standard-PC-Anwendungen
Hohe Reisebereitschaft
WAS DICH ERWARTET
Gestaltungsspielraum & Unternehmenskultur
Mitarbeit in einem mittelständischen Familienunternehmen, in dem deine Ideen zählen
Kollegiales Umfeld mit Austausch auf Augenhöhe
Kurze Entscheidungswege und offene Kommunikation
Regelmäßige Mitarbeiterevents und ein starkes Teamgefühl
Attraktive Vorteile
Attraktives Gehaltspaket
Flexible Arbeitszeiten und die Möglichkeit zum mobilen Arbeiten
30 Tage Urlaub

Mitarbeiterrabatte bei zahlreichen Top-Marken
Werde Teil einer Gemeinschaft, die zusammenhält, voneinander lernt und in der du wirklich etwas bewegen kannst.

als
Product Manager (m/w/d) - Energy Storage Systems

Unsere Kunden durch unsere Batterietechnologien. Unsere Mitarbeiter durch ein Mehr an Perspektive. Und als technologieführende Weltmarke öffnet VARTA Dir Gestaltungsspielraum in spannenden Projekten und Aufgaben im Zukunftsmarkt innovativer Energie-Lösungen.
Deine Aufgaben
Verantwortung für die strategische und operative Weiterentwicklung eines integrierten, digitalen Produktportfolios im Bereich ESS
